volume.h1:
SOUTH INDIAN INSCRIPTIONS
VOLUME XXII
Part I
—
INSCRIPTIONS COPIED IN 1906.

inscription.head:
No. 1.
(A.R. No. 1 of 1906)
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT, KANCHIPURAM TALUK, KĀÑCHĪPURAM.
ŚOKKĪŚVARA TEMPLE NEAR KĀMĀKSHĪ TEMPLE.
ON THE SOUTH WALL.
PARAKĒSARIVARMAN—15th YEAR.
Published in S.I. I. Vol. XIX., No. 365.

No. 2.
(A.R. No. 2 of 1906)
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT, KANCHĪPURAM TALUK, KĀÑCHĪPURAM.
ĒKĀMRANĀTHA TEMPLE.
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE FLOOR AT THE ENTRANCE
INTO THE SMAŚĀNĒŚVARA SHRINE.
PAR. UTTAMA-CHŌḶA—1[5]th YEAR (A.D. 984)
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 15 kaḻañju of gold by a lady by name••• Nāmagaḷ
Nāṭṭaḍigaḷ, deposited with the committee of management (kaḍavuḷ-sabhai) of Tiru-
mayāṉappuram for burning a perpetual lamp before the image of Brahmīs11varattāḻvār
of Tirumayāṉam in the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 3.
(A.R. No. 3 of 1906)
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT, KANCHĪPURAM TALUK, KĀÑCHĪPURAM.
ĒKĀMRANĀTHA TEMPLE-
ON THE SAME STONE.
PAR. UTTAMA-CHŌḶA-DATE LOST.
inscription.p:
The inscription is mutilated. It records a similar gift of 30 kaḻañju of gold
deposited with the same body by queen Vīranāraṇiyār.

l.ta:
kaṭṭaḷai kallāl tuḷainiṟai muppatiṉ kaḻañcu poṉ
koṇ••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 4.
(A.R. No. 4 of 1906)
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT, KANCHIPURAM TALUK, KĀÑCHĪPURAM.
ĒKĀMRANĀTHA TEMPLE-ON THE WALL OF THE BIG GŌPURA.
VĪRA MALLIKĀRJUNADĒVA-MAHĀRĀYA-Śaka 1378.
inscription.p:
The details of the date are given as Śaka 1378, Dhātu, Tulā, śu. 5, Friday,
Hastā which would correspond to A.D. 1456, October 4. But the week-day and
nakshatra do not tally with the tithi.

The inscription is left unfinished. It mentions a tax (?) called Iḍaṅgai-vari and
some land endowed to the temple of Tiruvēgambam-uḍaiya-Nāyanār in Kāñchīpuram,
a nagara in Eyiṟ-kōṭṭam, a division of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

edition.l:

tulānāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu pañcamiyum śukravāramum peṟṟa attattu nāḷ
jayaṅkoṇ-

kampamuṭ[ai]ya nāyiṉār tiruppaṇikkuc cellukiṟa putuppākkattukkum

inscription.head:
No. 5.
(A.R. No. 5 of 1906)
SALEM DISTRICT, NAMAKKAL TALUK, NAMAKKAL
DESERTED TEMPLE ON THE HILL-ON THE NORTH WEST AND SOUTH WALLS.
JAT. SUNDARA-PĀṆḌYA-2nd YEAR.
inscription.p:
This registers an endowment of wet lands 11 kāṇi in extent, in different places
for the requirements of food offerings and lamp in the temple of Aruḷāḷa-Viṇṇagar
alias Edirilipperumāḷ on the hill at Tiruvāraikkal, by the Sabhā of Ēḻūr-nāḍu and the
Nāṭṭār and the Nagarattār.

inscription.head:
No. 6.
(A.R. No. 6 of 1906)
SALEM DISTRICT, NAMAKKAL TALUK, NĀMAKKAL.
DESERTED TEMPLE ON THE HILL-ON THE EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
These are two copies of the same inscription one in Kannaḍa and the other in
Tamil. It is merely dated in the cyclic year Śrimukha, Mārgaśira, śu. 5. (Mārgaḻi 5)
and mentions a certain Lakshmīkānta-arasa son of Gajapati-vibhāṭa Tipparasa-
Oḍeya.

inscription.head:
No. 7.
(A.R. No. 7 of 1906)
SALEM DISTRICT, NAMAKKAL TALUK, NĀMAKKAL
RAṄGANĀTHA TEMPLE ON THE HILL-IN THE MONOLITHIC CAVE
FORMING THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This consists of a few Sanskrit birudas in the Pallava-Grantha script of about
the 8th century A.D. like those found for the Pallava king Mahēndravarman I in
the Pallavaram and Tiruchirapalli cave temples, and of two verses in Sanskrit both
damaged. One of them seems to record the excavation of this cave temple called
the Atiyēndra-Vishṇugriha by a king of the Atiyānvavāya (family) whose name is
lost. It also makes mention of the grandsons by the daughter (dāuhitras) of this
king (?). In the second verse we can read the names Śaśi, Sūrya, Tumburu, Nārada,
Madhu and Kaiṭabha. The images of these (and other) heavenly beings are actually
found cut in relief on the walls of the temple. For a detailed description of this and
the other two temples on the hill at this place, see the Annual Report on Epigraphy for
1906. Part II, paragraphs 35 and 36. The following are the birudas mentioned above:
Utpala-karṇika, Naravāhana, Madana-vilāsa, Mānapara, Nayapara, Udārachitta,
Prakritipriya and Naradēva.

l.de:
utpalakarṇṇika | naravāhana | madanavilāsa | mānapara | nayapara | udāracittaḥ | prakṛtipriyaḥ |
naradevaḥ |

inscription.head:
No. 8.
(A.R. No. 8 of 1906)
SALEM DISTRICT, NAMAKKAL TALUK, NĀMAKKAL.
RAṄGANĀTHA TEMPLE ON THE HILL-ON SLABS BUILT INTO THE WALLS
OF THE ROOM AT THE ENTRANCE.
inscription.p:
This is a collection of some fragmentary pieces of writing of about the 13th
century A.D. The inscription of which these should have formed parts, is dated in
the reign of a certain Tribhuvanachakravartin••••• and seems to register a gift of
land as Tirumaḍaippaḷḷippuṟam to the temple of Vēdanāyaka-perumāḷ on the hill at
Tiruvāraikkal, and gives the names of some benificiaries of the gift.

inscription.head:
No. 9.
(A.R. No. 9 of 1906)
SALEM DISTRICT, NAMAKKAL TALUK, NĀMAKKAL.
RAṄGANĀTHA TEMPLE ON THE HILL-
ON THE (INNER) WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
TRIBH. RĀJARĀJADĒVA-30th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This is an incomplete inscription recording the consecration of god Vēda-
nāyaka-Perumāḷ and the endowment of some land for worship in the temple, by a
chief named Aṅgarāyaṉ belonging to the Pullai Veṭṭuvar (?) community, who had the
titles Naṟkāvēri-Nāḍāḻvāṉ, Dēśi Āḷappiṟandāṉ and Mummuḍiśoḻa-chakravartti.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] tirupuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī irācarācatevaṟku yāṇṭu
- vatu pullai veṭṭu-

cakkaravatti āṉa aṅkarāyaṉe-

n nāṉ ukantaruḷuvitta vetanāyakapperumāḷukku pavittiramāṇikkat
til tutakkuḷattil vaṭakka-

inscription.head:
No. 10.
(A.R. No. 10 of 1906)
SALEM DISTRICT, NAMAKKAL TALUK, NĀMAKKAL.
NARASIMHA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE.
ON THE WALL OF THE MONOLITHIC CAVE.
inscription.p:
This records an agreement among the artisans of all parts of Koṅgumaṇḍa-
lam to give an impost of one paṇam in coin and a specified quantity of corn in
kind levied on each family, for the renovation of the temples of Siṅga-Perumāḷ
and Paḷḷikoṇḍa-Perumāḷ at Tiruvā[lu]ttalam.

edition.l: [ta]ṅkaḷ talaimelk koṇṭom [ iddha] rmma[m*] [ūru] malaiyum

inscription.head:
No. 11.
(A.R. No. 11 of 1906)
SALEM DISTRICT, NAMAKKAL TALUK, NĀMAKKAL.
NARASIMHA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-IN THE UNDERGROUND CELLAR
NEAR THE ENTRANCE INTO THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This consists of a number of disconnected fragments probably forming parts
of more than one inscription which are built over in several places. One of the pieces
seems to mention the 9th year of Rājarāja and to record some gift of land (?) for
worship and repairs (in the temple). Another makes mention of a person by name
Viḍukādaṉ alias Vattarāyaṉ of [Miṉ]ṉāmoḻi-nāḍu (?) and one Vaṇigachakravarti of
Pavitramāṇikkam. A third piece records a gift of land to god Emberumāṉ Śiṅga-
pperumāḷ of Tiruvāraikkal by a private individual by name Kāśyapaṉ Śendāmarai-
kkaṇṇaṉ. Still another piece refers to the [2]2nd year of an unspecified king and
to a gift of tax-free land to the temple as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam.

edition.l: —ṟku 1- vatu mutal iṉṉāya[ṉ*]māṟku tiruvārāta—
—vom 9- vatu mutal puṟakkaṭamai nāṭṭuvini—

edition.l: —ḻi 5- lum—
—[2]2- vatu kārmutal pullu—

inscription.head:
No. 12.
(A.R. No. 12 of 1906)
SALEM DISTRICT, NAMAKKAL TALUK, NĀMAKKAL.
NARASIMHA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—LEFT SIDE OF THE ENTRANCE.
inscription.p:
This is merely dated as Sādhāraṇa, Puraṭṭāśi 26, and records a solemn oath
(mariyādi-muṟi) given by the sthānikas and staff of the temple at Tirunāmakkal to
one Svāmināyakkar-Ayyaṉ (or before God ?) that they would not misappropriate
any gifts received towards offerings to the deity.

edition.l: ci mātam 26- l cuvāmiṉāya—

inscription.head:
No. 13.
(A.R. No. 13 of 1906)
SALEM DISTRICT, NAMAKKAL TALUK, NĀMAKKAL.
MUNIYAPPAN TEMPLE—ON A ROCK CLOSE BY.
TRIBH. KON. SUNDARA-PĀṆḌYA-8th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records an order of the king making a tax-free grant of the village Vāna-
vamādēvi in Ēḻūr-nāḍu a sub-division of Vaḍakoṅgu to a surgical doctor, (aṅga-
vaidya) by name Savarṇaṉ Pārāśrayaṉ Ādittadēvaṉ Tiruvambalapperumāḷ of Nāṅgūr
who had the title “Vaidya-purandara”. This order is given effect to and executed
in another inscription (No. 13 A.), engraved below, by the Sabhā, the Nāṭṭār and
Nagarattār of Ēḻūr-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 14.
(A.R. No. 14 of 1906)
SALEM DISTRICT, NAMAKKAL TALUK, NĀMAKKAL.
MUNIYAPPAN TEMPLE—ON THE SAME ROCK.
TRIBH. KŌNĒRINMAIKOṆḌĀṆ—5th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This is also probably an inscription of Sundara-Pāṇḍya mentioned in No. 13
above. It registers the execution of an order issued to them by the king, by the
sabhā, the nāṭṭār and the nagarattār of Ēḻūr-nāḍu, granting 1020 kuḻi of wet land
free of taxes to a certain Sarvadēvabhaṭṭa-Sarvakratu of Pāśāṟṟūr.

inscription.head:
No. 15.
(A.R. No. 15 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
TRIBH. VIKRAMA-CHŌḶADĒVA—2nd YEAR.
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It records an agreement by the mahā-sabhā of Tirukkaḍavūr
in Ambar-nāḍu a division of Vīranārāyaṇa-vaḷanāḍu to pay the taxes on a land
endowed for the maintenance of a maṭha called the “Āyirattaññūṟṟuvaṉ-maḍam”
established by a Veḷḷāḷa of the place by name Tiruveṇkāḍuḍaiyāṉ Nīṟaṇindappēra-
raiyaṉ for feeding Śivayōgis. The assembly is said to have received for this purpose
a lump-sum payment of 40 kāśu from the donor.

inscription.head:
No. 16.
(A.R. No. 16 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒSVARA TEMPLE—ON THE EAST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
RĀJAK. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA I—48th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This begins with introduction Pugaḻmādu etc. It is damaged and incomplete
and stops with the mention of the date Āṉi, śu. 4. Pūram and the expression agara-
brahmadēyam denoting a village.

inscription.head:
No. 17.
(A.R. No. 17 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒSVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
RĀJAK. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA—44th YEAR.
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with introduction Pugaḻmādu etc. The inscription is
much damaged and seems to record an endowment of land for the daily feeding of 2
Śivayōgis, and of money for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Kālakāla-
dēvar at Tirukkaḍavūr in Ambar-nāḍu a sub division of Rājanārayaṇa-vaḷanāḍu, by
one Sīriḷaṅgō Ādittadēvar alias Arindamaṉ-Mūvēndavē[ḷāṉ] of Eramel in [Ki]ḷā-
nāḍu a division of Rājādhirāja-vaḷarāḍu, who is said to have been the Paḷḷiyaṟai
nāyakam of the god.

inscription.head:
No. 18.
(A.R. No. 18 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒSVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
RĀJAK. KULŌTTUṄGA CHŌḶA I-[40]th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This also begins with the same introduction. It records an endowment of a
quarter [vēli] of land at Erukkāṭṭuchchēri by Sīrāḷaṉ Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻaṉ Rājarāja-
Pallavaraiyaṉ of••• maṅgalam in Veṇṇi-nāḍu for burning a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Kālakāladēvar.

l.ta: nāṭṭu uṭpaṭa eviya tirukkaṭavūruṭaiyār śrī kālakāla-

edition.l: tāviḷa[ kku idē] var dēvatāṉam erukkā[ṭṭucceri]-

inscription.head:
No. 19.
(A.R. No. 19 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJAK. KULŌTTUNGA-CHŌḶA—DATE LOST.
inscription.p: The inscription is badly damaged after line 13.

inscription.head:
No. 20.
(A.R. No. 20 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH BASE OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
PARAK. RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA I—15th YEAR.
(A.D. 1028—29)
inscription.p:
This begins with the introduction Tiru manni vaḷara etc. It records an endow-
ment of 140 kāśu by a merchant of Gaṅgaikonḍaśōḻapuram by name Māṇikka-
vāṇiyaṉ Śandiraṉ••• Kiḍāraṅgoṇḍaśōḻa Māyilaṭṭi, for the purpose of feeding 2
māhēśvaras after the midday offerings to the god. The amount was deposited with
the mahāsabhā of Tirukkaḍavūr. Ambar-nāḍu is here mentioned as a division of
Uyyakkoṇḍār-vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:
tiruma[ṉ*]ṉi vaḷara irunila(ṉ)maṭantaiyum porcceyap
pā[v*]aiyum cirttaṉi celviyum taṉ perunteviyarāki yiṉpuṟa neṭu taṉatūḻiyuḷ

yom nāḷ 91- ṉāl uṭaiyār śrī kālakālatevar śaṇḍēśvararkku oṭṭi

inscription.head:
No. 21.
(A.R. No. 21 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE EAST BASE OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
RĀJAK. RĀJARĀJA I—23rd YEAR (A.D. 1007-8)
inscription.p:
The inscription commences with the introduction Tirumagaḷpōla etc. It
records a sale of land made tax-free by the mahāsabhā of Tirukkaḍavūr in Uyyak-
koṇḍār-vaḷanāḍu for 15 kāśu received by them from the temple of Kālakāladēva.
This amount seems to have been a gift (from the palace ?) for burning a lamp
called “Mummuḍiśōḻaṉ” (evidently after the king's title) in the temple.

edition.l:
svasti śrī |||— tirumakaḷpolap perunilaccelviyum taṉakke yurimai
pūṇṭamai maṉakkoḷak kānta-

l.ta:
ḷūrccālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi veṅkaināṭum kaṅkapāṭiyu[m*] nuḷampapāṭiyum
taṭikaipāṭiyum kuṭamalaināṭum kollamum kaliṅkamum

[eṇṭi]cai pukaḻtara īḻamaṇṭalamum iraṭṭapāṭi eḻaraiyilakkamum
tiṇṭiṟal veṉṟit taṇṭāṟ koṇṭa taṉṉeḻil vaḷar ūḻiyuḷellā yāṇṭum
toḻute-
ḻavi[ḷaṅku yāṇ]ṭe ceḻiyarait tecukoḷ śrī ko

ttavallerikka tiruviḷakkuppuṟamāka viṟṟuk
kuṭutta ṉilam aḷaṉāṭaṉ vākkānkaraiy kiḻttalaippattu ṉilam nivaṉtam i-

inscription.head:
No. 22.
(A.R. No. 22 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME BASE.
RĀJARĀJA I—14th YEAR. (A.D. 998)
inscription.p:
The short introduction lists only the victories of the king over the enemy's
ships at Śālai and his conquests of Gaṅgapāḍi, Nuḷambapāḍi, Taḍigaivaḻi and
Vēṅgai-nāḍu. The inscription records an endowment of lands wet and dry in Kīḻūr
village to the temple of Kālakāladēva by one Dīran Kūttaṉ alias Ādichchapanma-
Viḻupparaiyaṉ for the daily supply of 4 nāḻi of milk, evidently for the sacred bath
of the deity, for burning a perpetual lamp and for the akkāraḍalai offering to the
god on the days of Saṅkrānti. Among the boundaries of the land mention is made
of a field (?) called Varaguṇa-viḷāgam.

l.ta: cālai kala maṟuttu keṅkapāṭiyum nuḷampapāṭiyum taṭikai-

[va]ḻiyum veṅkaiñāṭum koṇṭa śrī korāja rājakesari[vanma]kku
yāṇṭu 14- lāvatu muḻaiyūrnā-

tiraṉ kūttaṉāṉa āticcapaṉma viḻupparaiya-

kellaiyuḷḷu makappaṭṭatel-

inscription.head:
No. 23.
(A.R. No. 23 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME BASE.
RĀJARĀJA I—15th YEAR (A.D. 998-9)
inscription.p:
This begins with the introduction Tirumagaḷ pōla etc., The details of the
date given viz. Āvaṇi śu. 12, Saturday, Āślēsha, give the equivalent A.D. 999,
August 26, but the nakshatra was Śravaṇa not Āślēsha. The inscription records a
grant of land by the sabhā of Kaḍavūr to two persons by name Pādaśivaṉ Āyira-
ttaññūṟṟuvaṉ Kālakālaṉ alias Kaḍavūr-Vēḷāṇ and Rāmaṉ Chandraśēkharaṉ alias
Nūṟṟeṇma-Vēḷāṉ, probably to provide for the playing of musical instruments
during the several services and for offerings, to the deity in the temple of Tiruvīra-
ṭṭānattu-Perumānaḍigaḷ.

edition.l:
svasti śrī |— tirumakaḷpolap peru[nilaccelviyum taṉakke yuri]mai
pūṇṭamai maṉakkoḷak kāntaḷūrccālai kala maṟuttaruḷi veṅkaināṭu[ṅ] kaṭikai
vaḻiyuṅ kaṅkapāṭiyu nuḷampa-

ellā vāṇṭun toḻutakai[vi]ḷaṅku māṇṭe ce[ḻiya]rait tecukoḷ korāja

[ni]ccayitta pulam••••varkku

edition.l: rāka kuṭuttom sabhaiyom |—

inscription.head:
No. 24.
(A.R. No. 24 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME BASE.
PARAK. RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA I—13th YEAR, (A.D. 1026)
inscription.p:
The introduction is Tirumanni vaḷara etc., the list of conquests enumerated
extending up to that of the Gaṅga. The inscription records the gift of a golden
kalaśa set with 9 gems of high quality and weighing about 109 kaḻañju, to God
Kālakāladēvar by one [Sāga]ṇi Vaḍugaṉ of Nallūr in Nallūr-nāḍu a division of
Nittavinōda-vaḷanāḍu. This gift is said to have been got engraved on stone, by
the sabhā of Tirukkaḍavūr on receipt of a royal order to that effect.

l.ta:
tiruma[ṉ*]ṉi vaḷara irunilaṉ maṭantaiyum porceya
pāvaiyum [ci]rtaṉi celviyum taṉ [peru]nteviyarākki yiṉpuṟa neṭutiṉa
lūḻiyuḷ iṭaituṟai[nāṭu]m tuṭar vaṉaveli paṭar vaṉavāciyum cuḷḷiccūḻ matiḷ
ko[ḷ*]ḷippākkaiyum naṇṇal karumuṟaḷ ma-

ṇṇaikkaṭakkamum porukaṭaliḻattaraiyartam muṭiyum āṅkavaṉ teviyar
oṅkeḻil muṭi[yum muṉṉa]vaṉpakkal teṉṉavaṉ vaitta [cuntaramu]ṭiyum i

kulataṉa-

cāntivarṉṉam oṉpatum uḷpaṭa niṟai poṉ nū[ṟ*]ṟu orupatiṉ kaḻañce i The continuation of this seems to be built in and hence inaccessible.

inscription.head:
No. 25.
(A.R. No. 25 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH BASE OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
RĀJAK. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA I—26th YEAR. (A.D. 1095-6)
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with expression Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga etc., It registers
an endowment of two pieces of land making up 2(3/4) vēli in extent by the sabhā of
Tirukkaḍavūr, for the maintenance of some Śivayōgis attached to a maṭha called
the Kuṅgiliyakkalai[yar-maḍam] in the temple, which was evidently established by
one Paḻandīparāyaṉ of Śiṟṟāmūr, and for the requirements of worship during
the ardhayāma and the paḷḷi-eḻuchchi services in the temple. The gift is said
to have been made for the well-being of the king's person. The record is incomplete.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅkac cayamātu viru[m]pa nilamakaḷ vaḷara
malarmakaḷ puṇara urimaiyiṟc ciṟanta maṇimuṭi cū[ṭi mi]navar nilaikeṭa villavar
kulai[tara vikka]lan ciṅkaṇaṉ

peṟṟu ippaṭikku eṟaṭṭuk koḷvāriṉṟi oṭai•••••••

inscription.head:
No. 26.
(A.R. No. 26 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJA I—16th YEAR, (A.D. 1000-1)
inscription.p:
The details of date given viz. Mithuna, śu. 5, Thursday, Aśvati would give
the probable equivalent A.D. 1001, May 29, f.d.t. 17. The nakshatra was how-
ever Āślēsha, ·80, and not Aśvati. The record is incomplete. It seems to register
a sale of land by the Sabhā of “Paḍai-ēviya” Kaḍavūr in Ambar-nāḍu to a certain
[Śe]mbiyaṉ-Aḍigaḷ-Chaturvēdibhaṭṭaṉ of the same village. Mention is also made
of another person by name Bāradāyaṉ-Aḍigaḷ-Chaturvēdibhaṭṭaṉ who is stated to
have been sent to Malai-nāḍu by Rājarājamārāyar (evidently the king) on some
mission.

rccālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi veṅkai[nā]ṭum••••••• mpapāṭiyum taṭik[ai]
pāṭiyum kuṭa-

malaināṭum(ṅ) kollamuṅ kaliṅkamum ti••••• ṟi taṇṭāṟ koṇṭa
taṉṉeḻil vaḷarūḻiu-

ḷ ḷellā yāṇṭu[n*] toḻutakai viḷaṅkum yāṇṭe [ce]ḻiyarai
tecukoḷ śrīko[virājarāja]ke

inscription.head:
No. 27.
(A.R. No. 27 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME BASE.
RĀJARĀJA I—16th YEAR, (A.D. 1000-1)
inscription.p:
The details of date are given as Pūraṭṭādi ba. 2, Monday, Punarvasu (A.D.
1000, Sept. 23). The tithi should be ba. 8. This registers the sale of 3/4 vēli and odd
of land as tirunāmattukkāṇi to the temple of Tiruvīraṭṭānam-uḍaiya-Paramasvāmi by
the sabhā of Kaḍavūr. It seems that some money was due to the palace by way of
loan taken by one Bāradāyaṉ Pullaṉ Vīranārāyaṇaṉ alias Brahmaśrīrāyaṉ on behalf
of a lady of the Mañjanattār-vēḷam. The amount had doubled itself into 30 poṉ in
course of time, and some members of the Samarakēsari-teriñja Kaikkōḷar who were
orderd to collect the debt, put severe pressure upon the sabhā of the village to do so.
Brahmaśrīrāyaṉ had died in the meanwhile and the sabhā sought out a jñāti of his
by name Sāmanta-Tanikar who, however, disowned any responsibility towards his
cousin. The sabhā attached the debtor's property which had descended to the
deceased's son Vīranārāyaṇaṉ Śivaṉ and the latters' nephew Pullan Vīranārāyaṇaṉ.
Since these two could not be found, the property was taken over (?) exparte in lieu
of the amount due and was sold to the temple. The inscription is damaged and left
incomplete towards the end.

inscription.head:
No. 28.
(A.R. No. 28 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA
IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
MĀṞAVARMAN. KULAŚĒKHARA I—DATE LOST.
inscription.p:
The king is given the attribute “who was pleased to take every country”. The
inscription is mutilated and incomplete. The date portion preserved is 2*) Stops hereyear ... śu 7,
Friday, Aśvati (probably Junuary 16, A.D. 1293). The object of the record is to
register a gift of nine mā and odd of land, to the temple of KAlakāladēva by one
Saduraṉ Kēśavapperumāḷ of the village ŚeḻuvattUr in Pāṇḍimaṇḍalam after purchasing
the same and getting it made tax-free by the Sabhā of TirukkaḍavUr. The land
appears to have been previously endowed for a flower garden to a PiḍAri temple by
the same donor, and passed on to private ownership subsequent to the 13th year of
Perumāḷ Sundara-Pāṇḍya. The details however are not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 29.
(A.R. No. 29 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
VIKRAMA-CHŌḶA—7th YEAR, (A.D. 1124-5)
inscription.p:
The inscription has the introduction commencing with Pūmādu puṇara etc.
It is mutilated at the beginning. It records a gift of 11 kāśu deposited with the
temple authorities of Kālakāladēvar, for burning with the interest thereon a
perpetual lamp before the deity, by one Rājarāja-Munaiyadaraśar a resident of
Iḷaṅgārikuḍi in Rājanārāyaṇa vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 30.
(A.R. No. 30 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
VIKRAMA-CHŌḶA—6th YEAR, (A.D. 1123—24)
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the introduction Pūmālai miḍaindu etc., The
continuation after 1. 18 is inaccessible, and hence the purport of the record is not
known. It stops with the mention of the details of date viz. Vriśchika [śu. 1],
Wednesday, Mṛigaśiras and of the temple of Kālakāladēvar at “Paḍai-ēviya”. Tiru-
kkaḍavūr in Ambar-nāḍu a division of Rājanārāyaṇa-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 31.
(A.R. No. 31 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
TRIBH. RĀJARĀJADĒVA (II)—5th YEAR (A.D. 1150-51)
inscription.p:
The inscription seems to pronounce a ban against members of the royal family
receiving any dēvadāna lands belonging to the temple of Kālakāladēvar at Tirukkaḍa-
vūr in Ākkūr-nāḍu a division of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, and in the case of ryots
against receiving more than two vēli probably on tenancy lease.

edition.l: tiruvā[ṇai] śrī yājñaiḥ ||—

inscription.head:
No. 32.
(A.R. No. 32 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒSVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE FIRST PRĀKĀRA.
TRIBH. RĀJARĀJADĒVA (III)-18th YEAR. (A.D. 1233-34)
inscription.p:
This gives a comprehensive list of the lamps which had been donated to the
temple of Kālakāladēvar at Tirukkaḍavūr up to the 18th year of the king, and for
which lands had been endowed at Maṇaṟkuṉṟu in the dēvadāna village Erukkāṭṭu-
chchēri by various persons. The list of the donors includes such names as (1)
Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyāṉ Perumāṉambiyār alias Pallavarāyar of Kārigai-kuḷattūr
in Śiṟukuṉṟa-nāḍu of Āmūr-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, (2) Kūttāḍum-
dēvar alias Kaḷappāḷarāyar of Tiruveṇkāḍu, (3) Vēdavanamuḍaiyāṉ Ammaiyappaṉ
of Paḻaiyaṉūr in Mēlmalai-Paḻaiyaṉūr-nāḍu of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, (4) Tillai
nāyakaṉ Rājarājadēvaṉ of Kuṟṟālam in Paṭṭiṉakkūṟṟam of Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu,
(5) Ādittaṉ Rājēndraśōḻanār Kārāṉai Viḻupparaiyar of Perunallūr and (6) Ādittaṉ
Tiruvēgambamuḍaiyāṉ Vīrarājēndra Pallavaraiyar of Nāvalur, in addition to many
others.

inscription.head:
No. 33.
(A.R. No. 33 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. RĀJARĀJADĒVA III—21st YEAR (A.D. 1236-37)
inscription.p:
This registers an agreement among the aḍaippar and the kuḍimakkaḷ of
Erukkāṭṭuchchēri which is called a tirunandāviḷakkuppuṟam village of the temple of
Kālakāladēvar, that they would burn 3 lamps in the temple from the 21st year
onwards of the king, in token of their grateful appreciation of the help rendered to
them by Araiyaṉ Āḷuḍaiyāṉ [Nāṅgūrā]ḻvār of Śīgār in Puliyūrnāḍu a division of
Arumoḻidēvā-vaḷanāḍu. During his administration as Piḷḷai Kaikkaṇakkanār he is
said to have got them exemption from viniyōgam and attended to all their needs
without stint and also further restored to them after diligent search some charter of
privilege in original which had been granted to them in the 13th year of ‘Periyadēvar’
and which he now passed on to the palace through the village watchman as a charter
of security to the village.

l.ta:
jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻavaḷanāṭṭu ākkūrnāṭṭu ampa[r*]ṉāṭu uṭpaṭame
•••••• vūr uṭaiyār śrīkālakālatevar koyil [ā]ticaṇṭe

ppuṟam erukkāṭṭucceri aṭaipparum kuṭimakkaḷum ivvaṉaivom nāṭṭuk
kuṭutta paricāvatu piḷḷai kaikkaṇakkaṉār arumoḻitevavaḷanāṭṭu puliyūrnāṭṭu
cikāruṭaiyāṉ ar[ai]yaṉ āḷuṭaiyā [nāṅkūrā]ḻvār eṅkaḷai ūrāka aḻait
taruḷi eṅkaḷūrkku viṉiokam varātapaṭiyum maṟṟu oru kuṟai piṟavāta—

paṭiyum nokki [ā]ḷukaiyālum periyatevarkku patiṉmuṉṟāvatu nāḷil
eṅkaḷūrkku māṉalūruṭaiyāṉ eḻutiṉa oḻuku eṅkaḷpakkal niṉṟum corvupaṭṭu
poṉa[yi]tu iruntaṭam ārāyntu eṅka[ḷuk]ku vāṅkit tantaruḷi ivvoḻukiṉpaṭi
eṅkaḷūrkku[ḷḷa]ṭai eḻutivittu tantaruḷi mūlavoḻuku tiruvācali—

le eṅkaḷūrkkāka niṟkiṟa meykāppār vaḻiyāka vaippittaruḷiṉa itu
ūrkku irakṣaiyākaiyālum ivvūr irupatāvatuvarai iṟuttuvanta tirununtā
viḷakku irunūṟṟut toṇṇūṟṟeḻe kāle araikkālukku meṟpaṭa ivarkku
[taṉ]mamāka iraṇṭaraiye araikkāl viḷakku—

kku veṇṭum [ni]lattukku nila[mu]ṟṟum aṉupavikkiṟārperilum tirutti
aṉupavikkiṟārperilum ēṟakkaṇṭa nilamāy nāṅkaḷ pervaḻi eḻutikkuṭuttapaṭi
yey irupattoṉṟāvatu aippaci viṣu mutal cantirātittavaṟ tirumuṉpe eriyak
kaṭavatākavum ippaṭikku tirumāḷikaiyile kal—

veṭṭikkoḷvatākavum ippaṭi sammatittu nāṭṭuk kuṭuttom uṭaiyār
śrīkālakālatevar koyil āticaṇṭe

ratevarkaṉmikaḷukku tirununtāviḷakkup
puṟam erukkā[ṭ*]ṭucceri aṭaipparu[m*] kuṭimakkaḷum ivvaṉaivom
[ippaṭikku i] cāttaṅkuṭai (kuṭai)yāṉ kuṟumpūr nāṭ—

ṭukkoṉ eḻuttu ivai neṭuñ[ce*]rikuṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu ivai tiru
viraṭṭāṉaveḷāṉ eḻuttu itu uṟattuṭaiyāṉ kāṉav[āṉai]veḷāṉum
putuvūruṭaiyāṉ ci[ṅ]kayaveḷāṉum kaimāṭṭāṅkāṉamaikku ivai kālaṉaiya
karntaveḷāṉ eḻuttu ivai puḷiyaṅkuṭaiyāṉ kālaṉaiyakarnta veḷāṉ

eḻuttu ivai kaṇṇaṅkuṭaiyāṉ ākkūrnāṭṭukkoṉ eḻuttu ivai
maṅkiḻaṅkiḻavaṉ eṇṇātutaittaveḷāṉ eḻuttu ivai cātta[ṉu]ṭaiyāṉ kāla
kālateva[ṉ] eḻuttu ivai erukkuṭaiyāṉ ātitta[veḷā]ṉ eḻuttu itu
maṅkalaṅkiḻavaṉ nampiyum cāttaṉuṭaiyāṉ tevanum kaiymāṭṭ[ā]ṅkāṉa—

maikku ivai cāttaṉuṭaiyāṉ kālakālatevaṉ eḻuttu itu
tiruvāñciyamuṭaiyāṉ marutaṉum paruttikkuṭaiyāṉ [vāṭṭi] cevaṭiyāṉum
piṟaiyāṟukiḻavaṉ kālakālatevaṉum piṟaiyāṟukiḻavaṉ periyāṉum kaiymāṭṭāṅ
kāṉamaikku ivai cāttaṉuṭaiyāṉ kālakālatevaṉ eḻuttu itu cātta
ṉuṭaiyāṉ kāṟṟukai••• kuḷa—

ttuṭaiyāṉum••• yāṉu[m*] kiḻavaṉ nānanampiyum tirunalakkuṭaiyāṉum
kaimāṭṭāṅkāṉamaikku ivai kuṟumpūrnāṭṭu[kon] eḻuttu••••piḷḷaiyā•••••
veḷāṉ kaimāṭṭāṅkāṉamaikku ivai ālaṅkuṭaiyā[ṉ eḻu]ttu itu••••••
liyar kiḻavaṉ tevaṉu[m] toṭṭakkuṭaiyāṉ tāḻiyum

•••••kaimāṭṭāṅkānamaikku ivai kuṟumpūrnāṭṭukkoṉ eḻuttu
itu piṟaiyāṟukiḻava•••••••• tirucciṟṟampalaveḷāṉum piṟaiyāṟukiḻavan kūṟṟu
utai[t*]tāṉum kaimmāṭṭāṅkāṉamaikku cāttan uṭaiyān kālakālatevan
eḻuttu ivai [ā]ttappāṭi uṭaiyān eḻuttu

ivai tevakaḷnāyakan kaimāṭṭāṅkānamaikku ivai kālaṉaikāñca
veḷān eḻuttu ivai puṟaiyāṟukiḻavan viman eḻuttu ivai pāmpuṇikiḻavan
a[ṇ]ṭān aruḷāḷan eḻuttu itu iluppai uṭaiyāṉ celvan kaimāṭṭāṅ
kānamaikku ivai cāttan uṭaiyān ampalavaṉ kaimāṭṭāṅkānamaikku ivai
kuṟum—

[pūr]nāṭṭukon eḻuttu itu puṟampiluṭaiyā[ṉ*] perumānum cātta
nuṭaiyān vempanum tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyānum kaimmāṭṭāṅkānamaikku ivai
ulakuṇṭāṅkātta cūriyateva

ṭṭan eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai tirukkaṭavūr
āccāryan eḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 34.
(A.R. No. 34 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. VĪRARĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA—2nd YEAR, (A.D. 1064-5)
inscription.p:
This records the provision made by two ladies for burning a ‘half’ and a
‘quarter’ perpetual lamp in the temple by bringing into cultivation two pieces of land
in dēvadāna village Erukkāṭṭuchchēri for that purpose. Tirukkaḍavūr is stated to
be in Ākkūr-nāḍu a division of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:
coḻa[tevaṟku]
yāṇṭu iraṇṭāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻavaḷanāṭṭu ākkūrnāṭṭu tirukkaṭavūr
uṭaiyār śrīkālakāla

vamaṭaiñca ammai kallittiruttivicca nilattāl ikkoyilil eriyum
tirunantā—

viḷakku arai aruntavañ ceytāḷ kalli tirittivicca ni[lattāl eriyum]
tirunantāviḷakku kāl [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 35.
(A.R. No. 35 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. RĀJĀDHIRĀJADĒVA (II)—3rd. YEAR (A.D. 1165-6)
inscription.p:
This records a similar provision for burning 5 perpetual lamps in the temple,
three by one Tirayaṉ Tiruvālandūraiyuḍaiyāṉ of Ēmappēṟūr in Ēmappēṟūr-nāḍu
a division of Nāḍuvil-nāḍu alias Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, and one each by two other
persons one of whom was a native of Puriśai-nāḍu in Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇ-
daśōḻa-maṇḍalam and the other of Naḍār in Pāmbūr-nāḍu in Uyyakkoṇḍār-
vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:
vaḷanāṭṭu emappeṟūrnāṭṭu emappeṟūr
muḷḷuḻān arayan tiruvālantuṟai uṭaiyān tiruviḷakkuppuṟamāka erukkāṭṭuc
ceriyil kallit tiruttuvitta nilattāl ikkoyilil eriyun tirunantāviḷakku
muṉṟu jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu maṇa—

viṟkoṭṭattup puricaināṭṭup piccima••••••••• tirukkaḻippālai uṭaiyān
tiruviḷakkup puṟamāka erukkāṭṭucceriyil kallit tiruttuvitta nilattāl
ikkoyilil eriyum tirununtāviḷakku oṉṟu u[y*]yakkoṇṭārvaḷanāṭṭu
pāmpurnāṭṭu naṭāril naṭār kiḻān celvan coṟaṉāna

inscription.head:
No. 36.
(A.R. No. 36 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. RĀJĀDHIRĀJA II—12th YEAR (A.D. 1174-5)
inscription.p:
The king claims to have conquered Madurai and Īḻam. This records another
similar gift of a perpetual lamp each by Kuḍitāṅgi Tiruvuḍaiyāṉ a resident of
Būdalūr in Pāṇḍikulāsani vaḷanāḍu and Sōmattiyan Nīlan Pāṇi of Tirukkaḍavūr
by means of reclamation of land at Erukkāṭṭuchchēri.

l.ta:
vaṟku yāṇṭu panniraṇṭāvatu pāṇṭikulācaṉivaḷanāṭṭup
pūtalūrvaṭṭattup pūtalūruṭaiyāṉ kuṭitāṅki tiruvuṭaiyāṉ uṭaiyār śrīkāla
kālatevarkkut tirunantāviḷakkup puṟamāka

i[tt]evar tirunantāviḷakkuppuṟamāṉa erukkāṭṭucceriyil maṇaṟ
kuṉṟu kallik tiruttivitta nilattāl tirunantāviḷakku

tirukkaṭavūr comattiyaṉ nilaṉ pāṇi e[rukkāṭ]ṭucceriyil maṇal
[kuṉ]ṟu kalli iṭṭa tirunantāviḷakku

inscription.head:
No. 37.
(A.R. No. 37 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. KULŌTTUNGA-CHŌḶA III—9th YEAR (A.D. 1186-7)
inscription.p:
The king is given the epithet ‘who was pleased to take Madurai’. This
records yet another gift of the same kind by the reclamation of 700 kuḻi of land
each at the dēvadana village Erukkāṭṭuchchēri by two persons, one by name Sattiva-
nanāyakaṉ alias Dānavinōda-Pallavaraiyaṉ of Mūlaṅguḍi in Nallāṟṟūr-nāḍu a
division of Virudarājabhayankara-vaḷanāḍu and the other called Bārantāṅgināṉ
alias Kalikaḍindaśōḻa-Pallavaraiyaṉ of Magadaiyūr.

Below this inscription is an unfinished single line record of the [2]6th year
of Tribh. Rājarājadēva stopping with the mention of the dēvakanmis of the Kāla-
kāladēver temple.

l.ta:
tiripuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ maturaikoṇṭaruḷiṉa
śrīkulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu oṉpatāvatu jayaṅk[oṇṭacoḻa] vaḷa
nāṭṭu ākkūrnāṭṭu tirukkaṭavūr uṭaiyār śrīkālakālatevar

koyilil tirununtāviḷakku eriya virutarājapayaṅkaravaḷanāṭṭu nallāṟṟūr
nāṭṭu mulaṅkuṭiyil kāṇiyuṭaiya aṇai[kku]ṭaiyān cattivananāyakanāna tāna
vinotappallavaraiyaṉ ittevar tevatāṉam

eḻunūṟṟukkum nāḷ oṉṟukku ney uḻakkāka onpatāvatu āṉimātamutal
cantirātittavaraiyum erikka

oṉṟukkum ittevar teva
tāṉam erukkāṭṭucceriyil maṇaṟkuṉṟil viḷainilamākak kallina kuḻi

eḻunūṟ
ṟukkum [nāḷ] oṉṟukku ney uḻakkāka cantirātittavaraiyum erikka

jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻavaḷanāṭṭu ākkūrnāṭṭu ampaṉāṭu uḷpaṭa[meviya
tirukkaṭa]vūr uṭaiyār śrī kālakālatevar koyil āticaṇṭe

inscription.head:
No. 38.
(A.R. No. 38 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA III—9th YEAR. (A.D. 1186-7)
inscription.p:
The king claims to have taken Madurai. This records yet another gift of
two perpetul lamps by the reclamation of 1400 kuḻi of land in the same village by
one Arayaṉ Tiruvanantīśaṉ alias Kēraḷarāyan of Perunallūr in Kīḻvēṅgai-nāḍu a
division of Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:
tiripuvanaccakkaravarttikaḷ maturai koṇṭaruḷiṉa
śrīkulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu oṉpatāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻavaḷanāṭṭu
ākkūrnāṭṭu tirukkaṭavūr uṭaiyār śrīkālakāla

vaḷanāṭṭu kiḻveṅkaināṭṭu perunallūru(u)ṭaiyāṉ
arayaṉ tiruvaṉantican[ā*]rāṉa keraḷarāyaṉ ittevar tevatāṉam erukkāṭṭuc
ceriyil maṇal kuṉṟil viḷainilam kalliṉa kuḻi

māvukku nāḷ oṉṟukku ney [uḻak]kāka oṉpatāvatu puraṭṭāti māsam
piṟanta irupattuañcāntiyatimutal cantirātittavarai eriya ēṟiṉa tirununtā
viḷakku iraṇṭu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 39.
(A.R. No. 39 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA—4th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records another gift of a lamp by reclamation of 600 kuḻi or 6 mā of
land by Śēkkiḻāṉ Ammaiyappaṉ Parāntakadēvaṉ alias Karikālaśōḻa Pallavarāyaṉ
of Kuṉṟattūr in Kuṉṟattūr-nāḍu a division of Puliyūr-kōṭṭam alias Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-
vaḷanāḍu in Jayangoṇḍaśōḻamaṇḍalam.

l.ta:
kuṉṟattūr cekkiḻān ammaiappan [parā]ntakatevanāna karikālacoḻap
pallavaraiyaṉ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻavaḷanāṭṭu āk—

kūrnāṭṭut tirukkaṭavūr uṭaiyār śrīkālakālatevar koyilil tiripuvanac
cakkaravattikaḷ śrīkulottuṅka coḻatevaṟku yā—

ṇṭu nālāvatu mutal erikiṟa tirununtāviḷakku cantirātittavaṟ cella
ittevar tevatāṉam erukkāṭṭucceriyil maṇaṟkunṟu kalli-

edition.l:
yum kaḻitūr[r*]ttu[m*] tiruttina nilam āṟu mā[vu]kku ikkoyilil
eriyum tirununtāviḷakku oṉṟu |||—

inscription.head:
No. 40.
(A.R. No. 40 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA III—17th YEAR. (A.D. 1194-5)
inscription.p:
The king claims to have taken Madurai and the crowned head of the
Pāṇḍya. The inscription states that after the demise of Bhāradvāji Kālavinōda-
paṇḍitan of Tiruvalañjuḻi who, along with his brother had the right of worship in
the temples of Tiruvīraṭṭānam-Uḍaiyār and Tirumayānam-Uḍaiyār at Tirukkaḍa-
vūr, a resident of the village by name Kunikkumpirāṉ Śaiva-vidyādhara-Brahma-
mahārāja and his father Śaivachintāmaṇi-Brahmamahārājaṛ claimed to have been
appointed priests in their place by order of the king which was also got engraved
on stone in these two temples and were accordingly performing worship. This
reached the ears of Svāmidēvar (evidently the king's preceptor) who found out
their unworthiness and false pretensions and hence dismissed them from service
and reappointed the previons and rightful incumbents of the posts, viz. Bhāradvāji
Brahmīśvaramuḍaiyāṉ Kūttappirāṉ Kālavinōdapaṇḍitaṉ and Dēvapperumāṉ Śaiva-
sūrya-Brahmamahārajaṉ and others for the conduct of worship in the two temples.
He also restored to them the rights and perquisites attached to their posts as they
were in practice up to the 16th year of the king. They were given in addition the
right of worship of the other deities also set up in the temples like Kālakāladēver,
Kūttāḍumdēvar, Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷīśvaramuḍaiyār and Vikrama-Chōḷīśvaramuḍaiyār.
On the representation of Vikramaśōḻa-Brahmamahārājaṉ that this appointment may
receive royal assent and be engraved on stone, the king now issued his orders to the
temple through his officers Rājanārāyaṇa-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ and six others confirming
the above arrangements.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ maturaikoṇṭu pāṇṭiyaṉai
muṭittalai koṇṭaruḷiya śrīkulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu patineḻāvatu
nāḷ irunūṟṟaṟupattiraṇṭiṉāl

edition.l:
jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻavaḷanāṭṭu brahmateyam tirukkaṭavūruṭaiyār tiru
viraṭṭāṉamuṭaiyārum tirumayāṉamuṭaiyārum koyilil tevarkaṉmikaḷukkum
śrīmā[ hēśvarak] kaṅkāṇi ceyvārkaḷukkum śrīkāriyamceyvārkaḷukkum ikkoyil
kaḷil caiv—

l.ta:
tiruva[la]ñcūḻiyuṭaiyāṉāṉa kālaviṉotapaṇṭitaṉuk
kum ivaṉuṭaṉpiṟantāṟkum paḻaicāyk kāṇiyā yivarkaḷ ceytuvaracce[y*]
tey kālaviṉotapaṇ[ṭi]taṉ cettavāṟey eṅkaḷūr kuṉikkumpir[āṉā]ṉa caiva

jaṉum taṅkaḷukku kāṇiyāka nam olai eḻutivittu
koṇṭu ippaṭikku ikkoyilkaḷile kallum veṭṭivittu ceytuvantā[r*]
kaḷeṉṟum itu uṭaiyār

ivarkaḷe ceyyakkaṭavarkaḷāka aruḷicce[y*]tār enṟum ikkoyil
kaḷil

vācāriyam cevvārkaḷukkākap pūjittu varukiṟār pūjainilai uṭaiyār tiru
viraṭṭāṉa-

edition.l:
muṭaiyār koyilil eḻuntaruḷiaruḷum kālakālatevaraip pūjikkum
[pū]jainilai yonṟum kūttāṭuntevarai pūjikkum pūjai[nilai yoṉ]ṟum
kulottu[ṅ]kacoḷi[ śvaramu] ṭaiyāraiyum vikramacōḷi[śvara]muṭaiyārai[yu]m
pūjikkum pūjai-

l.ta:
nilaikaḷum ivarkaḷe kāṇiyākap peṟṟuc
ceyyavum ivaiyiṟṟukkup patiṉāṟāvatuvaraiyum peṟṟuvantapaṭiyāluḷḷa nimantam
muḷḷiṭṭa peṟukaḷ peṟavum ippaṭikku ikkoyilka[ḷi]le kalve—

jaṉ namakku
coṉṉamaiyil ippaṭi ceyyakaṭavatākac coṉṉom ippaṭi ceyyappaṇṇuka |||.
eḻutiṉāṉ tiru

jaṉ eḻuttu ivai nikarilicoḻapallavataraiyaṉ eḻuttu
||| ivai

tikaṅkaraiyaṉ [eḻuttu ||| i]vai nilakaṅkaraiyaṉ eḻuttu ivai amara
kon eḻuttu || ivai coḻakaṅkaraiyaṉ eḻuttu ||| .

inscription.head:
No. 41.
(A.R. No. 41 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PAR. RĀJARĀJADĒVA II—14th YEAR, 336th DAY (A.D. 1159-60)
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the introduction Pūmaruviya poḻilēḻum etc. It
records an endowment of land made tax-free, as archanābhōga for the worship of
god Rājarājīśvaram-Uḍaiyār consecrated in the temple, by one Sōmattiyaṉ Kalaiyaṉ
Śēndaṉ for securing the well-being of the king's person, the mahā-sabhā of Tiru-
kkaḍavūr making itself responsible for the payment of kuḍimai, kaḍamai and
other dues to the palace. It is signed at the end by no less than 65 persons, who
were members of the assembly. Further gifts of lands are recorded in continuation
of the above made for the same purpose by (1) the mahāsabhā of Pāṇḍikulā-
śani-chaturvēdimaṅgalam to the south of Tirukkaḍavūr, represented by 15
signatories (2) certain residents of the place who had their jīvitam at Tirukkaḍavūr,
like the kaṇkāṇiśeyvār, the kaināyakam-śeyvār and the paḍaittalaivar who gave lands
as kāśukoḷḷā-iṟaiyili for offerings to the deity during one service. These are repre-
sented by about 30 signatories at the end (3) the Śāliyanagarattār of Anantamaṅga-
lam alias Kulōttuṅgaśōḻapuram in Kuṟumbūr-nāḍu who alienated 6 mā of land in
favour of the same deity for being reclaimed (with 1 mā as a flower-garden), and
agreed to pay the kaḍamai and kuḍimai thereof for a consideration of 37 kāśu
received by them from the temple this is signed by 28 persons, and (4) the vyāpāra-
nagarattār of the same place who gave 7 mā and odd of land as archanābhōga, and
in return for 18 kāśu paid to them by the temple agreed to pay all the taxes thereon.
This is attested by 14 signatories. This document is said to be got engraved on the
walls of the Kālakāladēvar temple.

l.ta:
pū maruviya poḻileḻum poruppeḻu puṉainittilat tāma
neṭuṅkuṭai poḻintat tavaḷa veṇṇilā kuḷira potiyaccu••••••• palataṉa
••••••••• yuñ ceṅkolu manaittuyiṟkuṅ kāval pūṇap paṇiyaṇaimicaip
parañcoti•••••••• tāḷeṉa maṇmaṭantaiyaik kaippiṭittu malarmaṭantai maṇiyār
varaimār[pi]ṟ kuṭivāḻak kulamaṭantai komuṉṉākip pormaṭantaiyai maṇampu—

ṇarntu paruti mutaṟkulam viḷaṅkic curutikaḷiṉta•• ḻutti paḻantāṟu
camaiyattai miḷaveṭut tātiulakaṅ koḻuntu viṭṭut taḻaittoṅka koṭātaṟaṅ
kuḷirtūṅka pārivā••••ḷañcurakkat tāraṇi me•••••• nāṉmaṟaiyor toḻil
vaḷara yellārun taṉittaṉiye vāḻntaṉa miyāmeṉa•••• oruvaruṭaṉ oruvaṟkum
on••••nṟiṟkum••••varu pakaivaṉ ta••••viḻaintu kātaluṭcer intiraṉ
mutaṟ ṟicāpāla reṇmarum oru vaṭivāki vantavaṭiveṉa niṉṟu maṉuvāṇai taṉi
naṭātti kaliyāṉaiye piṇi—

ppuṇpaṉa maṇiccilampe yaraṟṟuvaṉace••••••••• ṭuppaṭuvaṉa māmalare
kaṭiyavāyiṉa varupuṉale ciṟaippaṭuvaṉa kāvukaḷe koṭiyavāyiṉa kaḷḷuṇpaṉa
vaṇṭukaḷe•••••• pārmalivaṉa veḻukaḻaṉiye maiyyuṭaiyaṉa neṭuvaraiye
maruḷuṭaiyaṉa iṉ•••••• me piṟattoḻukuṅ kaittāyare kaṭintoṟup pāriyaṟ
pulavare poruḷ veḻppā ricaippāṇare kūṭṭañ ceyvāreṉṟu kūṟi ivaṉ
kākkuṉ tirunāṭṭiniyalvituveṉa ninṟu kāval neṟi pūṇṭu neṟiyallatu niṉaiyātu
taṉ—

taiyilor tantaiyāyun tāyarilor tāyarāyum maintarilor
maintar[āyu miṉ]ṉuyirkaṭkuyirāyum viḻipeṟṟa payanenṉavum meypeṟṟa
aruḷeṉṉavum ettalaikku miṟaivanenṉavum yāñ ceyta peruntavamenṉavum
•••• talaivanenṉavum muṉ•••• nenṉavum araciyaṟkai niṟainiṟutti yallavai
kaṭintāṟuyattip porukaliyin iruḷ nikkip pukaḻḻenṉum nilāp parappik
ka[ṉ]ṉaṭaruṅ kāliṅkarum [teṉ]ṉavaruñ ciṅkaḷarum

kaiykayarum koṅkaṇarum kaṟpakarum kācaparum kāmpotarum kocalarum
[konta]ḷarum pappararum pāñcālarum poppaḷarum pūluvarum mattara••• nāṭṭarum
vattavarum māṟṟava•••• lavantaṇṭi iṭutiṟaikaḷ ḷeṉekkoṭiyāl iyaṟ
pāvalare eri[ya]vakaṟṟi karumāmukil tiruniṟattu kaṉakakkaḷiṟu••• rājaṉ tirumārpilun
tiruttoḷilum tirumaṉattilum piriyātu mana[t]tārak kuḻaliyaṟkor makuṭa
cūṭāmaṇi•• nañcintāmaṇi•• hā

mpūmakaḷilum jayamakaḷilum pukaḻmakaḷilun nāmakaḷilun talaittevi nā•••
••• ṟkivaḷenṉa yārācar perunteviyar pallāṇṭenṟaṭi poṟṟa peraracu taṉi
naṭāttip peṇṇaracāy muṭicū••••••••• uṭanciṟappum kaṭanā••• paṭaittaruḷi
aṟampurakkuṅ karuṇaivalli [i]vaṉvayaṟ pacumpuravi kaṭavu••• ṭā kaṭavivarum tavaṉ
kulattu ulakantoḻa vantaruḷiya canti(ra)rotaiyam nāṇmutaṟ kuṇanānkum
nalloḻukkamum peruṅkaṟpum peṇumayil

ḷorunānkum pukala varutanināyaka puvanamuḻutuṭaiyāḷum akkāmak kaliya
palar•••• ḻaipoḻi kuḷirmati veṇkuṭai cakkaravatti jeṉanātan taraṇipālan
taṉ nāyaki ālaṅkal malaṟcolaiyil āluṅkaḷiya vanta••• māruḷ[ḷil] oru pāṟ
kaṭalil [vi]ḷaiyāṭum peṭaiy naṉma[lai]yāyaṭaitta cilaintal mayil pukavoru cunta
•••••• paṭaitta nānmukatton perumpaṭappai vaḷarkkum piṇi vaiyya mettum
camantakamaṇi māṉatana puṉaika putuvamaṇ taiyalāṟkoru cūḷāmaṇi cakateva
cintāmaṇi••••••••• lai ko—

ṇṭu pukaḻeṟun maṇipuṉaintu peṇṇiya pavutaṉ••••piṟantatenṉa••••
ṇimanṉavartan teviyar ninvaḻiyaṭiyom aṭiyomeṉa muṉṉinṟu toḻutetta
mutalmai peṟṟamul nāṭṭi iṉam poḻiya [vira]

nāy veḷai taritta poṟkoṭi icai muḻutum koṭai
muḻutum eḻil muḻutum ticaimuḻutum maṇmuḻutum tirumuḻutum uṭaiyaḷāki
munṉa muḻutulakuyya muṭicūṭum

japaṇṭitan tanṉai muḻutuṭaiya tevi taraṇi
muḻutuṭaiyāḷum•••••• maṟaivāḻvu-

m paṉuval vāḻvum ivarkaḷil tevatātutti••••kku peṇmai uṭan
veruṅ kirtti maṇmicai vaḷarkkum kuyil ulakuṭai mukkokkiḻānaṭikaḷennum
alakil kaṟpil aṟaneṟi maṭantai•• kirutti kuṇattāṟ ciṟantoṅkiaṟantaḻaippa
aruntutiyeṉa varum perumai avaṉimuḻutuṭaiyāḷum••••perum pukaḻ•••••••
mānaṭikaḷum ūḻiyūḻi pala kaṟpil vāḻi maṇam puṇantiruppa utaiyakiri ucci eṟi
mativeṇkuṭaip putuniḻalkiḻ cantiramukamaṇṭala t(t)tāmarai••• pavaḷavā[y]

edition.l:
intiranila•• moḷiram paṭiṭṭana icai•••• pon virasiṅhāsa[ṉattu]
puvaṉamuḻutuṭaiyāḷoṭu viṟṟiruntaruḷiya kopparakecaripaṟmarāṉa tripuvaṉaccakkara
vattikaḷ śrīrājarājadēvaṟku yāṇṭu 14- ā[vatu] nāḷ 33[6]- nāl jeyaṅ
koṇṭa coḻavaḷanāṭṭu ākkūrnāṭṭu ampa[r*]nāṭuṭ paṭa[ye]viya tiruk
kaṭavūr mahāsabhaiyom nammuril [co]mattiyan kalaiyaṉ centan ulakuṭaiya
nāyanār tirumeṉi kalyāṇatirumeṉiyāka ivvūril eḻuntaruḷuvitta uṭaiyār
rājarājīśvaramuṭai[yārkku] aṟ[ccaṉāpo]kamāka ūrkkiḻ iṟaiyili ceytu kuṭutta

nilamāvatu tiripuvaṉamātevivatikku kiḻakkum meṟkum arumoḻi teva-
vāykkālukkut te[ṟkku]•• kaṇṇāṟṟu 1- catirattu caṅkarakaṇṭanenṟu per
kūvappaṭṭa viḷainilamuṅ kulaitiṭarum oṭaikkum ellaiyāvatu kaṭa[lā]ṭṭi āṟṟukku
meṟku veṇkāṭuteva vākkālukku vaṭakku omayinta[ṉ] aṉannan vaṭukan
nilattukku kiḻakku comattiyan tiruviraṭṭāṉamuṭaiyān kalaiyan nilattuk
kum kaṭalāṭṭi āṟṟukku naṭuvuḷppaṭṭa vi[ḷainilam] ntaṅapa-m ivvellaiyil
akappaṭṭa kulaitiṭarum oṭaiyum vikkirama tūrattavatikku kiḻakku ivvāykkālukku
vaṭakku 2- kaṇ•••• catirattu kollaippaḷḷaviḷai nilam iupa•••• nakku veṇ-

l.ta:
•••••••• ittevaṟku ūrkkiḻ iṟaiyiliyāka nāṅkaḷ viṭṭamaiyil
innilaṅkaḷukkut tiruvācalil puṟappaṭṭa kuṭimai kaṭamai epperppaṭṭatum nāṅkaḷe
iṟukkakaṭavomāka iṟaiyili ceytu kuṭuttom ivvūr

yom [||*]
paṇiyāl ivai āyirantaiññūṟṟuvap piriyan eḻuttu

edition.l:
yayājinaḥ gautaman kumaran kaṇṇibhaṭṭa kāḍakasōmayājinaḥ sōmatyan
sadāśivan kumārabhaṭṭa[sya] || gautaman dakṣiṇāmūttibhaṭṭasya gautaman ārāvamutu
cuntarattoḷuṭaiyānbhaṭṭa sya ||—gautaman ārāvamutu tevabhaṭṭasya ||—mōkiliyan
tiruveṇkāṭubhaṭṭasūnō ragnici[ taḥ] ||— mōkiliyan kāṭan candraśēkharabhaṭṭasya
mōkiliyan tiruveṇkāṭubhaṭṭa agnicinaḥ ||—ātrayan nakkan tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyān
coki eḻuttu (uninscribed space) sōmatyan kalaiyan centan eḻuttu sōmatyan
tevan kalaiyan eḻuttu sōmatyan nilani[lan eḻu]ttu mōkiliyan pūtan tirucciṟṟampala
muṭaiyān eḻuttu sōmatyan kalaiyan

tevaṉ eḻuttu sōmatyan cantiracekaran centan eḻuttu omayintan
kaṇṇan koṟṟan eḻuttu mōkiliyan k[āṭa]n kāṭan eḻuttu sōmatyan tevan
tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyān eḻuttu omayintan nilan paṭaiyan eḻuttu sōma[ tyaṉ

pāṇi vayitikattereṟi eḻuttu sōmatyan ta••• centan eḻuttu mōkiliya[n rā]
jentiracoḻa[n] ta••• eḻuttu sōmatyan tiruviraṭṭāṉa muṭaiyān kalaiyan eḻuttu
sōmatyan kaṇṇi nilan eḻuttu sōmatyan tātan tiruviraṭṭāṉa[muṭaiyā]n

yan koṟṟan nilan eḻuttu omayintan koṟṟan cūriya

māka iṟai[yiliy]āka innāyanār śrīpātattu nāṅ[kaḷ] nirvāttukkuṭutta nilamāvatu
śrīmattūrāntakavatikku meṟku jeṉaṉātavākkālukku vaṭakku 5- kaṇṇāṟṟu 2-
catirattuk kiḻakkaṭaiya oṭaiyum uṭaippu••••taramiliyāyk kiṭanta nilam say na

edition.l:
ṭan eḻuttu nampūr colai aracupaṭṭan eḻuttu iruṅkaṇṭic caṅkara
nārāyaṇapaṭṭan eḻuttu mantirattu nārāyaṇapaṭṭan e[ḻu]ttu kantāṭaic
colaippirān paṭṭan eḻu[ttu] iruṅkaṇṭik kovintapaṭṭan eḻuttu poṟpuṟattu
nārāyaṇapaṭṭan eḻuttu iruṅkaṇṭi nārāyaṇapaṭṭan eḻuttu mantirattuc cūriya
tevapaṭṭan eḻuttu || . ṣpace innāṭṭu ākkūrnā[||ṭ*]ṭut tirukkaṭavūril jivita

kaṇkāṇi ceyvvārkaḷum kaiññāyakañ ceyvvārkaḷum paṭai[tta]ḷavañ
ceyvārkaḷum ivvaṉaivom ivvūr uṭaiyār rājarāja īśva-

ramuṭaiyāṟku nāṅkaḷum nāyanār tirumeṉi kalliyāṇa tirumeṉiyāka
oru canti amutu ceytaruḷukaikku nāṅkaḷ kācu koḷḷā iṟaiyili ceytu kuṭutta
nilamāvatu ivvūril eṅkaḷukku jivitamaṭaitta viracoḻa nallūril śrīmāṟkaṇṭaiya
vatikkuk kiḻakku irājentiracoḻavāykkālukku vaṭakku 1- kaṇṇāṟṟu 1-
catirattum [2] kaṇṇāṟṟu 1- catirattum nāṅkaḷ viṭṭa nilam paṉaṅkāṭṭukku
melaikkollai aṟutikku meṟku rājentiracoḻavākkālukku vaṭakkum
kuṭukkinṟoṅkollaikkuk kiḻakkum tivakkāṟar kuṭiiruppukkup pukum vaḻikkut
teṟku inṉānku cimaiyilum naṭuvuḷ uḷḷa••••menṟu perkūvappaṭṭa

iruppukkellai nilam

tikamāka kācu kācu koḷḷā iṟaiyili
yāka innāyanār śrī pātatte nirvā[r*]ttuk kuṭuttom innilattukku tiruvāca

maṇṇaikoṇṭacoḻak koṟṟakkuṭaikkon eḻuttu paṉaiyūrnāṭṭuk koṟṟak

yāṇapuraṅkoṇṭacoḻak koṟṟakkuṭaikkon eḻuttu ulakuyyakkoṇṭa

māttāṇṭa koṟṟakkuṭaikkon eḻuttu ulakaḷantacoḻak koṟṟakkuṭaikkon
eḻuttu coḻativākara koṟṟakkuṭaikkon

eḻuttu coḻaviccātirak koṟṟakkuṭaikkon eḻuttu |||—innāṭṭuk
kuṟumpūrnāṭṭu aṉantamaṅkalamāṉa kulottuṅkacoḻapurattu nakarattom eṅkaḷūr
uṭaiyār tiruvaṉanti

kūṭi iruntu eṅkaḷūr maṟuvum orupūvum ivvāṇṭu cāviyāyt taṭṭuppaṭṭu iṟukka
oṇṇāmaiyil ittaṭṭiṟaik kuṭalāka eṅkaḷilicaintu innāṭṭu ākkūrnāṭṭut
tirukkaṭavūr uṭaiyār

tevaṟkku innakarattuc cāliyanakarattom ūr(ṟ)kiḻ kācukoḷḷā iṟaiyili
yāka iṟaiyiḻicci iṟaiyiliceytu ku-

ṭutta nilamāvatu eṅkaḷūril kiḷaitāṅkiccatirattukkuk kiḻakku paḷḷiyāṟu
niroṭukālukkut teṟkum meṟkum ivvāṟṟil teṉkulaikku vaṭakku innāṉku
simaiyilum naṭuvuḷ [va]ram[pa]ṟaiyāyp payiṟ ceyya uṇṇātu pāḻkiṭanta
taramilip paṭukaiyil

ṉen[ṟu] tirunāmañ cāttiṉa nilamaḷavukolāl
nūṟu kuḻikoṇṭatoru māvāka nilam vaupa innilam āṟumānilam viḷainilamāka kallit
tiruttikkoḷḷak kaṭavarākavum orumānilam itu kalli eṟaṭṭa maṇṇilait

inscription.head:
No. 42.
(A.R. No. 42 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PAR. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA III—16th YEAR (A.D. 1193-4)
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with introduction Puyal vāyppa etc. The king is given
the attribute “who took Madurai and was pleased to take the crowned head of the
Pāṇḍya (evidently Vīra-Pāṇḍya)”. The details of the date are given as Mēsha, śu.
[8], Thursday, Pushya (A.D. 1194, March 31). This registers the remission of
taxes on a vēli of temple land (kāśukoḷḷā-iṟaiyili) by the mahāsabhā of Tirukkaḍa-
vūr, for the expenses of worship and offerings to god Rājarāja-Īśvaramuḍaiyār
consecrated in the shrine to the west side of the Kālakāladēvar temple, by Araiyaṉ
Rājarājadēvar alias Vāṇādharāyar for the welfare of the king. This image of the
deity is evidently different from the one mentioned in No. 41 above.

l.ta:
puyalvāyppa vaḷam peruka poyyāta nāṉmaṟaiyiṉ
ceyalvā[y*]ppa tirumakaḷuñ ce[ya]makaḷuñ ciṟantuvāḻa veṇmatipolk kuṭai
viḷaṅka [velv]ettar aṭivaṇaṅka ma[ṇma]ṭantai maṉaṅkaḷippa maṉuniti taḻait
toṅka cakkaramuñ ceṅkolun

muṭipuṉaintu taṇṭo[ṉṟāl] virapāṇṭiyaṉtaṉ makaṉai mukkarintu [vikkirama
pāṇṭiyaṟku kūṭalmānakar kuṭut]tu miṇ[ṭapiṉ] eṭuttuvanta paripavattāl etirtta
virapāṇṭiyaṉai [muṭittalai] koṇṭu amar muṭittu aṭutta mutal cuṟṟattoṭum
pukal laṟṟu kuṭa kollam pukka [t]eṉṉannum mikka ce[ranu]m

[van]taṭi vaṇaṅka avar muṭimel laṭivaittu [cen]tamiḻ nāṭṭaraicum
mavuliyun teṉṉa[vaṉuk kaḷi]ttaruḷi virakkaḻal puṉaintu virakkoṭiyun tiyāka
koṭiyum uṭaṉ eṭuttu [kaiykai]yar[koṉ] kātalaṉai perkāṭṭac kaṇṭaruḷi
eytariya celvattuḷ ellaiyil paricaṭṭam nalki minavanām virakeraḷan ve-

[ḷai k]oṇṭu [viral ta]ṟittu tānaṭi[mai pu]kutu[talāl tarā]tipar peṟā
vāḻvaḷittu pakkamiru[ntuṇṇa] parikalapariccinna nalki tikke[ṭṭu]m evalkeṭṭu
cakkaraveṟpil pukaḻ eṟippa cempon vira

inscription.head:
No. 43.
(A.R. No. 43 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PAR. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA III.—16th YEAR. (A.D. 1193-4)
inscription.p:
The details of the date are given as Mēsha, Śu. 8, Thursday, Pushya, corres-
ponding to A.D. 1194, March 31.

This records the same transaction mentioned in No. 42 above, which is here
addressed to the concerned officers nilavāriyaṉ and nilakkaṇakkaṉ evidently for the
purpose of entry in their tax registers.

l.ta:
puyal vāyppa vaḷam peruka poyyāta nānmaṟaiyin
ceyal vāyppat tirumakaḷum ceyamakaḷum ciṟantu vāḻa veṇmatipoṟ kuṭai
viḷaṅka velventar aṭivaṇaṅka maṇmaṭantai maṉaṅkaḷippa maṉu[ni]ti taḻait
toṅka cakkaramuñ [ce]ṅkolum tikkaṉaittum ce-

eṭuttu vanta paripavattāl eti[r*]ttu vanta virapāṇṭiyaṉai muṭittalai koṇṭamar
muṭi[ttu] aṭutta mutal putal[vara*]ṟṟu kuṭakollam pukka te[n]-

naṉu mikka ceraṉum vantu aṭivaṇaṅka avaṉ muṭimel aṭivaittu

virakkoṭit tiyākakkoṭi uṭan eṭuttu kaiykaiyarkon kātalaṉaip perkāṭṭak
kaṇṭaruḷi eytariya

śrīkulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu 16- vatu meṣanāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu

inscription.head:
No. 44.
(A.R. No. 44 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE BASE OF THE VERANDAH
ENCLOSING THE CENTRAL SHRINE, SOUTH SIDE.
inscription.p:
This states that two pieces of land, one known as Kānattūrnāṭṭu-Vēḷāṉ
Maṉaikkollai and the other Tillaināyakaṉ-mayakkal-kaṇḍam measuring respectively
4 mā and 6 mā in extent, are the tirunāmattukkāṇi (temple land) belonging to god
Kālakāladēvar at Oḻugamaṅgalam.

edition.l:
svasti śrī . oḻukamaṅkalattu kālakālate[var] tirunāmattukkāṇi kānat
tūrṉāṭṭu veḷāṉ maṉaikkollai nilam 4- m āṟṟukkuk kiḻakku

l.ta:
tillai nāyakaṉ mayakkalk kaṇṭattu m[el]ppaṭi per kūvappaṭṭa vaṭatalai
meṭuṭpaṭa nilam
āṟumāvum āka nilam i nilam arai .

inscription.head:
No. 45.
(A.R. No. 45 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME BASE.
JAT. VĪRA-PĀṆḌYA DĒVA—14th YEAR.
inscription.p:
The details of the date given viz. Dhanus, ba. 10, Wednesday, Svāti in the
14th year of the king correspond to A.D. 1266, December 22. This records a
communication addressed to the governing authorities of the temple of Kālakāla-
dēvar by one Kāśipaṉ Tiruchchiṟṟambalamuḍaiyāṉ-Bhaṭṭaṉ, confirming the gift
of two pieces of land measuring seven mā in extent at Perumuḷai in Viḷanagar-nāḍu
a division of Jayangoṇḍaśōḻa-vāḷanāḍu, which he had made some time previously,
and authorising them to get the deed now engraved on stone and copper. The latter
part of the inscription seems to register the actual execution of the document
defining the boundaries of the land, among which is mentioned a land belonging to
a temple called the Vikrama-Chōḷīśvaramuḍaiyār.

edition.l:
svasti śrī . kocca[ṭai*]yapaṉmar tirupuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīvira
pāṇṭiyatevaṟku yāṇṭu 14- vatu [ dhanusu nāyaṟṟu] aparapakṣattu budhaṉ
kiḻamaiyum cotiyum daśamiyum peṟṟa nāḷ uṭaiyār (kāla)kālakālatevar koyi-

l.ta:
tevar kaṉmikaḷukku perumuḷaiyil kācipaṉ tiruc
ciṟṟampalamuṭaiyā[ṉ paṭṭaṉ]eṉ

ṇam paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu
muṉṉāḷ iṉṉāyanāṟku nāṉ utakam paṇṇikkuṭutta perumuḷaiyil e-

edition.l:

pattāṅkaṇṭattu kūttañcey maṉai uṭpaṭa nilam va-m āka nilam vaḍa- m iṉṉilam
eḻumāvum nirurimaikaḷumuṭpaṭa dānamākak kuṭukkayil i nilam m

inscription.head:
No. 46.
(A.R. No. 46 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME BASE.
MĀR. KULAŚĒKHARA—34th YEAR (A.D. 1301)
inscription.p:
The details of the date are given as Kanyā śu. 7, Sunday, Mūlā and yield
the equivalent A.D. 1301. September 10.

The object of the record is to register a joint resolution of the three assemblies
Kīḻkūṟṟu-Brahmadēśaṅgaḷilār, Agara-Brahmadēśāṅgaḷilār and Veḷḷāḷa-Ūrgaḷilār of
Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḷa-vaḷanāḍu to set apart 10 out of the 35 vēli of land belonging to
them by purchase in the tirunāmattukkāṇi village Ponpaṟṟi, for burning 40 perpetual
lamps in the temple of Kālakāladēvar for the welfare of the king (Ulaguḍaiya-
Perumāḷ). The occasion for this gift is stated to be that their prayer to the god
was answered by way of the king having resumed for himself the administration
of their nāḍu from his younger brother, and thus having enabled them to return to
their own homes from other regions to which they had fled owing to the disturbed
state of the country for a long time, and where they had had to suffer great
privations and miseries.

edition.l:
. svasti śrī . śrīmeṟkirttikkumel komāṟapaṉpar tirupuvaṉaccakkara
vattikaḷ śrīkulacekaratevarkku yāṇṭu 34- vatu muppattuṉālāvatu kaṉṉināyaṟṟu

nāṭṭai viṭṭu [ dēśāntiraṅkaḷi] ley(y)

inscription.head:
No. 47.
(A.R. No. 47 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE WALL OF THE THIRD GŌPURA.
KṚISHNADĒVARĀYA-Vijaya, Āḍi 20 (A.D. 1533—34)
[or Vṛisha, A.D. 1521, July 18]
inscription.p:
This is a pair of verses in Tamil in praise of one Āpatsahāyaṉ Abhaṅguraṉ
who is called ‘the chief of Kaḍavai’ and who is stated to have taken part in the
invasion of Raichur and Bījāpūr (Vijayānagara) along with the king. The ins-
cription records the renovation of the temple and the institution of the daily recital
of the Vēdas by suitable provision of lands.

l.ta:
ṇit tiruvaḷar colaik kaṭavūrar tevatāṉam uṇṭākkivittāṉ
arumaṟaiyoṉ yiyal āpaṟ[ca]kāyaṉ apaṅkuraṉeṉ [||*]

ceṟṟalāreṇ kiṟuṣṇarāyaṉ muṉṉe tirukkoyilkaḷil coṟpayi lākama
niti yellān turaikaḷ mecca niṟṟalum collit tiruppaṇi••

inscription.head:
No. 48.
(A.R. No. 48 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME GŌPURA.
inscription.p:
The inscription states that the throne in the paḷḷi-aṟai of god Kālakāladēvar
is the gift of one Tiruvālavāychchokkaṉ, a resident of Neḍunduḷadi alias Rāja-
mārttāṇḍanallūr in Tuvvūr-kūṟṟam a division of Pāṇḍimaṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 49.
(A.R. No. 49 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p: These are verses in praise of a certain later Pallava chief (Kāḍavarkōṉ).

edition.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XII. No. 265.

inscription.head:
No. 50.
(A.R. No. 50 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME GŌPURA.
inscription.p:
This is stated to be the composition of a poet by name Kālakālaṉ Īḻattaraiyaṉ.
It is a verse describing the beauties of Tirukkaḍavūr with its champaka groves
humming with bees and with the rolling Kāvēri river, the favourite place of residence
of god Kālakāladēva.

l.ta:
tiṅkaḷum voli aḻinta [ci]ntūramuñ ceṅkai nallāc
caṅkamum

koṅkaip puḷakamun tāṅkit tavaṉa [virap]poraṅkulil veṉṟaruḷuṅ
kālakālaṉ ka-

ṭavai veṟpiṟ paṅkayanteṭa naṉṉa vārarip paḷikkaṟ[ai]k kocilaik
kālakā-

laṉ tirukkaṭavūruñ ceḻun[ta]ṭamu mālaikkālulāviya ceṇpakac kāvu
matukaraṅkaḷ

[ku]laikkāvarampayil coṇāṭuma•• tanittalan taḷḷukiṉṟa alaik
kāviriyum i-
cai kātam vār eṉ taṉāraṇaṅke . kālakālaṉ īḻattaraiyaṉ kavi .

inscription.head:
No. 51.
(A.R. No. 51 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME GŌPURA.
PERUMĀḶ SUNDARA PĀṆḌYA-Date Lost.
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It seems to record an endowment in money of 125 [kaḻañju]
by some merchants of Kulaśēkharaṉ-pāṭṭinam, to provide for the various require-
ments in connection with the worship of the deity in the month of Mārgaḻi, in the
temple of Kālakāladēvar at Tirukkaḍavūr. This place is referred to as a brahmadēya
in the eastern division (Kīḻ-kūṟu) of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 52.
(A.R. No. 52 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR.
AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
SUNDARĒŚVARA SHRINE.
TRIBH. RĀJARĀJADĒVA (III)—10th YEAR, 299th DAY (A.D. 1225-26)
inscription.p:
This records an order of the king, issued through his Secretary Mīnavaṉ-
Mūvēndavēḷāṉ and other officers, granting the uḷvari (entry in Tax-Register) for
13 vēli of land presented by him tax-free in the village Podimaṅgalam in Kuṟumbūr-
nāḍu, to the temple of Tiruvīraṭṭānam-uḍaiyār at Tirukkaḍavūr, for the merit of
his queen (Tribhuvanamuḻududaiyāḷ).

rakkaṇkāṇi cey[v]ārkaḷukkum śrīkāriyañ[c]eyvāṉukkum it

inscription.head:
No. 53.
(A.R. No. 53 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUMAYĀNAM
BRAHMAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE GENTRAL SHRINE.
TRIBH. KŌNĒRIMAIKOṆḌĀṈ (?)—34th YEAR.
inscription.p:
The identify of the king is not clear. This records a tax-free gift of half a vēli
and odd of land at Erukkilantiṭṭai village near Ākkūr alias Rājēndraśīṅga-chaturvēdi
maṅgalam, for rearing a lotus garden for the supply of garlands to god Tirumayānam-
Uḍaiyār at Tirukkaḍavūr in Ākkūr-nāḍu and for the maintenance of the persons
looking after the garden.

l.ta:
teyam tirukkaṭa[vūr uṭai]yār tirumayāṉa
muṭaiyār koyil tevarkaṉmikkum śrīmā

rak kaṇkāṇi ceyvārkaḷukkum

tāmam cāttiyaruḷa tiruoṭaiyākavum ceṅkaḻunir ākkuvārkkuk koṟṟukkum
puṭavai mutalukkum veṇṭuvaṉavaiyikkum tevatāṉa iṟai[yili*]yiṭa nām nir
vārtta nilattukku ākkūrāṉa

ntiraciṅkac caruppetimaṅkalattoṭum kūṭṭi

iṟaiyiliyā•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 54.
(A.R. No. 54 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUMAYĀNAM
BRAHMAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. KŌNĒRINMAIKOṆḌAṈ ? 22nd. YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records the king's order to the tax-officers (Varikkūṟu-śeyvār) communi-
cated through the Secretary Rājendraśiṅga-Mūvēndvēḷāṉ and 4 other officers, renew-
ing the grant of a tax-free land 13 vēli and odd in extent, situated in Rājādhirāja-
maṅgalam which was a tax-free dēvadāna and tirunāmattukkāṇi village belonging
to god Tirumayānam-Uḍaiyār at Tirukkaḍavūr. The land is said to have been
converted into tirappu in the king's 4th year and hence perhaps this present order of
renewal.

l.ta:
teyam tirukkaṭavūr uṭaiyār tirumayāṉamuṭaiyār koyiṟ
tevakaṉmikkum

k kaṇkāṇi ceyvārkaḷukkum śrīkāriyañceyvāṉukkum
ittevarkkut tirunāmattukkāṇiyum tevatāṉa iṟaiyiliyumāṉa innāṭṭu

maṅkalattu nālāvatu māṟi tiṟappu nilam patiṉ muṉṟe aṟumāvarai araik
kāṇikkiḻaraiye muṉṟumāvum irupattiraṇṭāvatu pacāṉmutal paḻampaṭiye
tevatāṉa iṟaiyiliyāka antarāyam pāṭṭamuḷppaṭa niṟkakaṭavatākac colli
ippaṭi kaṇakkilum i

edition.l:
ṭṭukkoḷḷakkaṭavarkaḷāka varikkūṟu ceyvārkaḷukkum coṉṉom
innilam irupattiraṇṭāvatu pacāṉmutal paḻa[m*]paṭiye tevatāṉa iṟaiyili
yākak kaikkoḷka eḻutiṉāṉ tirumantiraolai rājentiraciṅka muventaveḷāṉ
eṉṟum ivai viḻuppādharāyaṉ eḻutteṉṟum ivai kurukularāyaṉ eḻutteṉṟum
ivai cetiyarāyaṉ eḻutteṉṟum ivai kāṅkayarāyaṉ eḻutteṉṟum ||—

inscription.head:
No. 54.
(A.R. No. 54 A of 1906)
inscription.p:
This is engraved in continuation of the inscription No. 54 above, though
it is evidently a separate and different record. It is dated in the 11th year and
169th day of an unspecified king, and registers a grant of land at Ākkūr after its
purchase by one Villavarāyar of Perunallūr for 10127 kāśu, for the merit of the
reigning king, to provide for the maintenance of persons who were to recite the
Vēdas and the Śrī-Rudram in the temple of Tirumayānam-Uḍaiyār at Tirukkaḍavūr.
The exact significance of the term vēda-vīṇai is not understood. Perhaps the
chanting of the vēdas was accompanied with the music on the vīṇā. Mention is
made of two villages Tiruttoṇḍattugai-maṅgalam and Śivapādaśēkhara-maṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 55.
(A.R. No. 55 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUMAYĀNAM.
BRAHMAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KŌNĒRINMAIKOṆḌĀN (?)—9th YEAR AND 100th DAY.
inscription.p:
This seems to register the grant of 2 pieces of land, one at Vīraśōḻanallūr
and the other at Virudarājabhayaṅkaranallūr and measuring together 23(1/2) [vēli] in
extent, to the temple of Tirumaiyānamuḍaiyār at Tirukkaḍavūr, by order of the king.
Mention is made of a flower garden called the “Rājādhirājaṉ tirunandavanam”
which was to be formed out of one of these lands after its reclamation from sandy
mounds. The record is left incomplete towards the end. A land settlement made
in the 16th year of Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa is referred to in the inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 56.
(A.R. No. 56 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUMAYĀNAM.
BRAHMAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL
OF THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
TRIBH. VĪRARĀJĒNDRADĒVA—6th YEAR (A.D. 1068-9)
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 6 mā of land after reclamation (from sandy mound) at
a place called Tirumayānaviḷāgam as provision for the daily supply of ghee for a
perpetual lamp in the temple, by Ambalakkūttaṉ, a resident of Karuppūr in Marugal-
nāḍu, a division of Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu. In continuation of the above is an
inscription of the 8th year of Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa recording the joint gift of a lamp to
the temple by two ladies.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] [ tribhuvanacca] kkaravarttikaḷ śrīvirarā[ jēndiṟate] var[kku
yāṇ]ṭu 6- vatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻavaḷanāṭṭu ākkūr[nāṭṭu] tirukkaṭavūr
uṭaiyār tirumayāṉamuṭaiya

nilam āṟu mā |||.

inscription.head:
No. 57.
(A.R. No. 57 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUMAYĀNAM.
BRAHMAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBHUVANAVĪRADĒVA (KULŌTTUṄGA III.)—35th YEAR (A.D. 1212-13)
inscription.p:
The king is introduced with his panegyric “who took Madurai, Īḻam and
Karuvūr and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya and performed the anointment of
heroes and victors”. The inscription is damaged. It registers a tax-free gift of land
at Erukkalandiṭṭai village in Ākkūr by Vāchchiyaṉ Tiruchchiṟṟambalamuḍaiyāṉ
Pinnai-ennādāṉ a devotee from Perumbaṟṟappuliyūr a taṉiyūr in Rājādhirāja-
vaḷanāḍu, to provide for the daily supply of a specified number of lotuses to the
temple during the several months in the year. The land seems to have been allotted
by the temple to three elders of Kuvaḷaiyūr, a hamlet of Tirukkaḍavūr with
necessary stipulations.

inscription.head:
No. 58.
(A.R. No. 58 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUMAYĀNAM.
BRAHMAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJ. RĀJĀDHIRĀJADĒVA II—14th YEAR (A.D. 1176-77)
inscription.p:
This inscription begins with the introduction Kaḍalśūḻnda Pārmādarum etc.
The king is given the attribute “who was pleased to take Madurai and Īḻam”.
This registers a gift of land about six vēli in extent in the village Śembiyaṉkuḷap-
pākkam and 4090 saplings of areca-palm to be grown therein, after purchasing
them from the sabhā of Maṇaṟkuḍi alias Uttamaśōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Tiru-
vindaḷūr-nāḍu, a sub-division of Rājādhirāja-vaḷanāḍu, as tirunāmattukkāṇi to
goddess Tirukkāmakkōṭṭa-Nāchchiyār in the temple of Tirumayāṉam-Uḍaiyār at
Tirukkaḍavūr by Arumoḻi-Āḻviyār (daughter of ?) Uṟṟanōytīrppāṉ of Iḷaṅgārikuḍi
in Pērāvūr-nāḍu, a division of Uyyakkoṇḍār-vaḷanāḍu, who was the wife of Vēda-
vaṉamuḍaiyāṉ Ammaiyappar alias Pallavarāyar of Paḻaiyaṉūr in Mēnmalai-Paḻai-
yaṉūr-nāḍu in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta: taru maṭal cūḻnta pormātaruñ cirmātaru mamaintu vāḻa
nāṟkaṭal cūḻ puviyeḻum pāṟkaṭal pol pukaḻ parappi yātiyukamā-

varkaḷ

inscription.head:
No. 59.
(A.R. No. 59 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUMAYĀNAM.
BRAHMAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
MĀR. KULAŚĒKHARA I—35th YEAR (A.D. 1302-3)
inscription.p:
The king is given the attribute “who was pleased to take all countries”.
The details of date are given as Ṛishabha, śu. 14, Monday, Mūla which probably
correspond to A.D. 1302, May 14. But the tithi was ba. 2 on this date. This is
stated to be copy of an order of the king (Kōnērinmaikoṇḍāṉ) to the effect that
the capital amount out of which taxes were being paid up to the 34th year of his
reign on the village Tirumaiyāṉaviḷāgam alias Taniyānaiyiṭṭaperumāḷ-nallūr which
was the tirunāmattukkāṇi and tiruviḷakkuppuṟam of the temple, were now to be
remitted in favour of the temple and the village, entered as tax-free in the registers
so as to provide for offerings and worship to the god during the service in the
name of the king, and for repairs to the temple. The signatories to the order
were the officers (?) Tuñjalūruḍaiyāṉ and Pallavarāyaṉ.

edition.l: [ya] perum[ā]ḷ kulacekaratevarkku yāṇṭu 35- vatu muppatta-

canti-

[lum ve]-

rāyaṉ eḻu-

inscription.head:
No. 60.
(A.R. No. 60 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUMAYĀNAM.
BRAHMAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBHUVANAVĪRA (KULŌTTUṄGA III)—33rd YEAR (A.D. 1210-11)
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the mention of the king's conquest of Madurai
and Īḻam and Karuvūr and his anointment as hero and victor. It records a gift
of one mā of land at Tirumayāṉaviḷāgam after reclamation by a Śivabrāhmaṇa of
the temple by name Āttiraiyar Nambi Āḷavandi alias Poṟkōyil-Bhaṭṭaṉ to provide
for the requirements of worship to the goddess Vāḍāmulai-Nāchchiyār on a special
festival day in the year. The land was left in charge of one Śēṟṟuḍaiyāṉ Perumāḷ
on vāḍākkaḍamai (perpetual lease) and he was to measure out 5 kalam of paddy
for the purpose mentioned.

edition.l: [ṇṇi]aruḷiṉa śrī [ tiribhu]-

inscription.head:
No. 61.
(A.R. No. 61 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUMAYĀNAM.
BRAHMAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
KULŌTTUṄGA (III)—8th YEAR (A.D. 1185-6)
inscription.p:
The king is given the attribute “who was pleased to take Madurai”. This
records a gift of 600 kuḻi or 6 mā of land at the dēvadāna village Tirumayānaviḷāgam
after reclamation by Tillaināyaka-Perumāḷ of Kiḷiyūr-nāḍu in Pāṇḍikulāśani-
vaḷanāḍu for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple with the daily supply of an
uḻakku of ghee.

tirumai[yā]namuṭaiya perumāḷkoyilil tirunantāviḷakku eriya pāṇṭi
kulācaṉivaḷanāṭṭu

inscription.head:
No. 62.
(A.R. No. 62 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUMAYĀNAM
BRAHMAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
KULŌTTUṄGA III—9th YEAR (A.D. 1186-7)
inscription.p:
The king has the attribute “who was pleased to take Madurai”. This
records a similar gift of 7 mā of land in the same place for a perpetual lamp in
the temple, by one Arayaṉ Tiruvanantaṉ Kēraḷarāmaṉ of Kiḻvēṅgai-nāḍu in
Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu.

ṭu ākkūrnāṭṭu tirukkaṭavūr uṭaiyār tirumayānamuṭaiya perumāḷ

mutal eriya

inscription.head:
No. 63.
(A.R. No. 63 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUMAYĀNAM
BRAHMAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
KULŌTTUṄGA III—9th YEAR (A.D. 1186-87)
inscription.p:
The king is given the attribute “who was pleased to take Madurai”. This
also records a similar gift of 6 mā of land after reclamation by payment of money
by Vaṇṇaṉ Maṉṟuḷḷāḍuvāṉ alias Śembiyaṉ Pallavaraiyaṉ of Puduvūr in Ārvalak-
kūṟṟam, a division of Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, for burning a lamp in the temple.

tiripuvaṉa cakkaravattikaḷ maturai koṇ[ṭa]ruḷiṉa śrī kulot

[ta]varai cella-

inscription.head:
No. 64.
(A.R. No. 64 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUMAYĀNAM
BRAHMAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE—ON WEST WALL OF THE FIRST PRĀKĀRA
TRIBH. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA III—17th YEAR (A.D. 1194-95)
inscription.p:
The king is given the attribute “who was pleased to take Madurai and the
crowned head of the Pāṇḍya”. The record begins with his title Kōnērinmaikoṇḍāṉ.
This is almost a copy of the inscription No. 40 above found in the Amritaghaṭēśvara
temple as the contents pertain to this temple also.

inscription.head:
No. 65.
(A.R. No. 65 of 1906)
TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUMAYĀNAM
BRAHMAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SAME PRĀKĀRA
KULŌTTUṄGA III—13th YEAR (A.D. 1190-91)
inscription.p:
The king is mentioned with his usual attributes. This records the appoint-
ment of one Naṟputtuḍaiyāṉ••• Guṇachcharaṉ in place of a certain Chaṇḍēśurap-
pichchaṉ (deceased) as nandavaṉakkuḍi to look after the flower garden which had
been endowed to the temple by a devotee by name Vāchchiyaṉ Jātavēdaṉ alias
Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyāṉ of Rājādhirāja-vaḷanāḍu on the northern bank of the
river (Kāvērī).

edition.l:

inscription.head:
No. 66.
(A.R. No. 66 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, KALLAKKURICHCHI TALUK, TIRUVARAṄGAM.
RAṄGANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE RIGHT AND LEFT WALLS OF THE ENTRANCE.
SADĀŚIVADĒVA—ŚAKA 1480 (A.D. 1557)
inscription.p:
The details of date are given as Piṅgaḷa, Kaṉṉi, śu. 2, Rēvati, Friday, which
yield the equivalent A.D. 1557, September 10. But the tithi was ba. 2. The record
states that on this day Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Obaladēva-Mahārāja, son of Kōnēṭayya-
Mahārāja and grandson of Rāmarāja Koṇḍudēva-Mahārāja of the Ātrēya-gōtra,
Yajuś-śākhā and Āpastamba-sūtra who had the Nāyakattanam of Magadaimaṇḍalam
conferred on him by the king, granted the income from three villages Eḍuttavanallūr,
Māḍāmpūṇḍi and Periya-Pavaṇḍai in Muḍiyaṉūr-paṟṟu for the several requirements
of daily worship and offerings, and for special festival occasions to god Raṅganātha
at Uttara-Tiruvaraṅgam in the same paṟṟu (division). On the representation of
one Tirumalainambi Śrīrāmayyaṅgār to the chief, that the grant did not cover the
full expenses incurred in the temple, the latter is said to have made an additional
gift of the income from Pāḍiyēndal. Muḍiyaṉūr-paṟṟu is stated to be situated in
Vaḻudilampaṭṭu-chāvaḍi in Koṟukkai-kūṟṟam, a su-bdivision of Meyguṉṟa-vaḷanāḍu
in Magadaimaṇḍalam. It is also stated that it was situated in Tiruvadi-rājyam in
Magadaimaṇḍalam. Hence it seems likely that Tiruvadi-rājyam was a major division,
the same as Meyguṉṟa-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 67.
(A.R. No. 67 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
RĀJ. TRIBH. KULŌTTUṄGA (II)—3rd YEAR (A.D. 1135-36)
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 64 cows for burning two perpetual lamps in the
temple of Tāntōṉṟi-Āḷuḍaiyār at Śaṇbai alias Vīrarājēndrapuram in Śeṅkuṉṟa-nāḍu
on the north bank of the Peṇṇai river, by one Aḍiyanambi Kōvalarāyappēraiyaṉ
of Muḍiyanūr in Kīḻkoṉṟai-nāḍu on the southern bank, as atonement demanded
of him by the members of the Chitramēḻi community (of artisans or cultivators ?)
of the 79 nāḍu, for having killed by accident a certain Poṉpaṟṟi-uḍaiyāṉ Kuṉṟaṉ
Śīruḍaiyāṉ, a Veḷḷāḷa resident of Vāḻaiveṭṭi in their nāḍu (Śeṅkuṉṟa-nāḍu). The
arrow shot by him at an animal probably in a hunt, is said to have missed its aim
and pierced this Śīruḍaiyāṉ instead.

l.ta: karaic ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭu vāḷaiveṭṭiyil irukkum veḷḷāḷaṉ po-
[va]ṭaiyile ivaṉ ceri aṭiyanampi virukattai eyya piḻai-
melintap paḻitirak koṇṭa naṭaiyāvatu iṉnāṭṭuc ca-
ṟi āḷḷuṭaiyār śrī koyilile iraṇṭu tirununtāvi-

inscription.head:
No. 68.
(A.R. No. 68 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KULŌTTUṄGA I—40th YEAR (A.D. 1109-10)
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the introduction Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga etc. It
registers the gift as tax-free dēvadāna of a village by name Kuḷakkuḍi alias Vīraśōḻa-
nallūr on the south bank of the Peṇṇai by a royal order issued at the request
of Vāṇakōvaraiyaṉ Śuttamallaṉ Muḍigoṇḍāṉ, for the requirements of the temple
of Tāntōṉṟīśvaram-uḍaiya-Mahādēva at Śaṇbai alias Vīrarājēndrapuram on the
northern bank of the river and situated in Vāṇagappāḍi Rājēndra-vaḷanāḍu. The
antarāyam tax included in the transaction is fixed at 79 kaḻañju.

l.ta: nilamakaḷ nilava malarmakaḷ puṇara urimaiyiṟ ciṟanta maṇimuṭi
cūṭi miṉavar nilaikeṭa villavar kulaitara eṉaimaṉ
ṇṭum nimantaṅkaḷukku kuṟaivutira oru ūrriṭaveṇ[ṭu]meṉṟu vāṇa-

vāykkālukkut teṟkum uṇṭāṉ erikku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai cip-
vāykku vaṭakkum itaṉ meṟmaṭattuttuṟai ūṭaṟuttu teṟkeṉikki••

ṟkellai pākkattukkup poṉa peruvaḻikku kiḻakkum talaivāyai ūṭaṟu-

uḷppa-

rakṣaiḥ ||—

inscription.head:
No. 69.
(A.R. No. 69 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḺA—38th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records a tax-free gift of 1000 kuḻi of land at Māmāndūr on the southern
bank of the river for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple by a certain Āḷavandāṉ
Rājarāja-Śiśupālaṉ of Pērāyil.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] tribhuvaṉaccakravattikaḷ śrīkulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu
38- vatu peṇṇai vaṭakarai caṇpaiyāṉa virarācentiracoḻapurattu uṭaiyār
tiruttāṉtoṉṟi āḷuṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku tirununtāviḷakkuppuṟamāka peṇṇait
teṉka-

rai meykuṉṟanāṭṭu māmāntūril māmāntūruṭaiyāṉ taṉmattil
perāyiluṭaiyāṉ coḻaṅkap pallavaraiyaṉ āḷavantāṉāṉa piṟavāveṉṟāṉāṉa
irā[ja*]rā[ja*] cicupālaṉeṉ iṟaiyili viṭṭa kuḻi 1000- m cantirātittavarai celva
tāka viṭṭeṉ

inscription.head:
No. 70.
(A.R. No. 70 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KON. TRIBH. SUNDARA-PĀṆḌYA—8th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This is an incomplete inscription. It registers the gift as tax-free dēvadāna
the village(,) Nariyār-ēndal for the requirements of offerings and unguents to god
Tāntōṉṟi-Āḷuḍaiya-nāyaṉār at Śaṇbai alias Vīrarājēndraśōḻapuram in Vāṇagappāḍi-
nāḍu on the northern bank.

inscription.head:
No. 71.
(A.R. No. 71 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
BĀJARĀJA (I)—26th YEAR (A.D. 1010-11)
inscription.p:
This begins with introduction Tirumagaḷpōla etc. It is built in at the begining
of each line. It records the sale of some land tax-free by the assembly of Maṟuvūr
alias Vayiramēgha-Chaturvēdimaṅgalam on the southern bank of the river in
Vāṇagappāḍi to the temple of Tāṉṟōṉṟi-Mahādēva at Vāḷaiyūr (Jambai) alias
Nittavinōdapuram on the northern bank, to provide for offerings to god Kshētrapāla
dēva in the temple. The cost of the land and the lump-sum amount towards the
payment of taxes (iṟai-dravyam) seems to have been received by the sabhā from a
certain Kāḍagachchōmāśi of Kūvaṇḍūr, a member of the āḷum-gaṇam.

inscription.head:
No. 72.
(A.R. No. 72 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH AND WEST
WALLS OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
TRIBH. RĀJARĀJADĒVA (III)—28th YEAR (A.D. 1239-40)
inscription.p:
This records to the gift of a village called Maṉanduyyanallūr as dēvadāna for
offerings, worship and repairs in the temple of Tāṉtōṉṟi-Āḷuḍaiya-Nāyaṉār at Śaṇbai
alias Vīrarājēndrapuram in Vāṇagappāḍi by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kumāra Padu-
madēvarasaṉ, who exempted the gift-village even from the antarāyam (rent ?).

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] tiripu[va*]ṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīrājarājatevaṟku yāṇṭu
28- vatu peṇṇai vaṭakarai vāṇakoppāṭic caṇpai

ellai kiḻpā-

inscription.head:
No. 73.
(A.R. No. 73 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA—38th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 1000 kuḻi of land in the village Nallūr Maruttuvaṉpāḍi
free of all takes, for offerings to the goddess Tirukkāmakkōṭṭa-Nāchchiyār in the
temple of Tāṉtōṉṟi-Āḷuḍaiyār at Vīrarājēndraśōḻapuram, by Śōḻagaṅga-Pallavaraiyaṇ
Rāja[rāja*] Śiśupālaṉ of Pērāyil (the donor mentioned in No. 69 above). The
village Maruttuvaṉpāḍi is said to have been a dēvadāna (?) which had been granted
to the temple by one Vāṇavichchādira-Vēḷāṉ.

edition.l:
[||*] svastiśrī tribhuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ kulottuṅkacoḻateva(r)ṟku
yāṇṭu 38- vatu coḻakaṅkappalla-

coḻapurattu uṭaiyār tiruttāṉtoṉṟi āḷuṭai-

yanāyaṉār tirukkāmakkoṭṭa nācciyārkku amutupaṭikku kaṟi amutukkum
māka vāṇaviccātiraveḷāṉ taṉmattil taṉmi iṭṭa nallū[r]

āyiramum aṉaittāya
muḷppaṭa iṟaiyilitevatāṉamāka viṭṭu pūcaiyum amutupa-

pallavaraiyaṉāṉa piṟavāveṉṟāṉ irāca[rāca*] cicupālaṉeṉ eḻuttu itumāṟuvāṉ

kutiraikku pulliṭum paṟaiyaṉukku taṉ miṇāṭṭiyai kuṭuppāṉ itu

inscription.head:
No. 74.
(A.R. No. 74 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KŌN. TRIBH. KULAŚĒKHARADĒVA—10th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This registers a gift of 3(1/2) vēli of wet land and 3 vēli and odd of dry land tax-
free in the village Naralōka-dīraṉpaṭṭu in Meykuṉṟa-nāḍu on the southern bank, by
the king for the expenses of worship and offerings during the service Kulaśēkharan-
Śanḍi in the temple of Tāntōṉṟi-Āḷudaiya-Nāyaṉār.

l.ta:
yaṉārkku kulacekaraṉ cantikku veñcaṉa[t*]tukkum peṇṇait teṉkarai
meykuṉṟa
meṟkku melpāṟkellai kaḷaviyappāṭi ellaikku kiḻakkum te-
ṉpāṟkellai kāvaṉūr ellaikku vaṭakku[m*] vaṭapāṟkellai peṇ-

ṇai āṟṟukku teṟkkum uḷppaṭṭa nañcai nañcai muṉṟarai ve[li]yuḷ
paṭṭa puñcai nila mu[ ṉ-

ṟu veliciṉṉa] mumāka iṟaiyiliyāka kuṭutteṉ koṉeriṉmel
koṇṭāṉ kulacekarataveneṉ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 75.
(A.R. No. 75 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PAR. RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḺA (I)—21st YEAR (A.D. 1034-35)
inscription.p:
The inscription is badly damaged. It begins with introduction Tirumaṉṉi
vaḷara etc., and seems to record some provision made for burning two lamps in the
temple including one in the shrine of Durgā, by the toll-collecting officers of Vāḷaiyūr
on the north bank.

l.ta:
tiruma[ṉ*]ṉi vaḷara irunila [maṭan]taiyum poṟc[eyappā
vaiyum cirtaṉic] celviyu

tuyalūḻiyi liṭaituṟai[nāṭu]ntuṭarvaṉave lippaṭar va•••••••••
koḷḷippākkaiyu na[ṇ*]ṇaṟ karumuraṇ maṇ[ṇaikkaṭak]

(k)kamum porukaṭa liḻattaraicartam muṭiyumāṅkavaṉ teviya ro
••••••••• pakkal teṉṉavan vaitta cu

vum ceruviṟ ciṉavi•••••••••

ñcilaivira pañcapa[ḷ*]ḷiyum pācaṭai paḻaṉa mācuṇitecamum•••••••••
vaṇkir[tti]•••••• yiṟ ca

viḷaiyamarkaḷattu kiḷaiyoṭum piṭittu palataṉattoṭu niṟaikulata[ṉa]
••••••• ceṟimiḷai oṭṭa viṣaiyamum pūcurar cernal kocalai [nāṭum]

edition.l: rāṉa uṭaiyār śrīrājēntracoḷadēvarkku [ yāṇṭu2] 1- āvatu [vāṇa]-
uḻakkum ikkoyilil tukkaiyārkku cantiviḷakku 1- kku eṇṇai

l.ta: talaimelaṉa itu iṟakkuv[āṉ] eḻā narakattu kiḻānaraka-
