inscription.head:
No. 76.
(A.R. No. 76 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. KONĒRINMAIKOṆḌĀṈ (?) 17th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of the village Śīpādanallūr inclusive of the lands
on permanent tenure as iṟai-yili (tax-free) to the temple of Tāntōṉṟi-Āḷuḍaiya-
Nāyaṉār at Śaṇbai in Śeṅkuṉṟa-nāḍu a division of Vāṇagōppāḍi on the northern
bank, to meet the expenses of the service called the “Bhuvanēkavīraṉ-śandi” and
for repairs to the temple by two persons by name Bhārgavaṉ Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍāṉ and
Kauśikaṉ Yajñamūrtti of Iṟaivāṉaraiyūr in Narippaḷḷi-nāḍu on the northern bank, a
division of Magadaimaṇḍalam. “Bhuvanēkavīra” is known to have been a title of
Māṟavarman Kulaśēkhara I. (A.R. No. 260 of 1917).

l.ta:
tirupuvaṉaccakkaravatti koṉeriṉmaikoṇṭāṉ peṇṇai
vaṭakarai vāṇakoppāṭi ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭu caṇpai uṭaiyār tiruttāntoṉṟi
āḷuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyilttā[ṉattā*]ṟkku makataimaṇṭalam vaṭakarai narippaḷḷi
nāṭṭu iṟaivāṉaraiyūr

mutti
yum kāṇiāṉa cipātanallūr kiḻpāṟkellai kuṇamaṅkalattu ellaikku meṟkku
teṉpāṟkellai

cempiyaṉ mātevi arumpuṭaiyāṉ paṭṭu ellaikku vaṭakku melpāṟkellai
tammaṭṭapaṭṭu ellaikku kiḻakku peṇṇaiyāṟṟukku teṟkum āka ṉāṉkellaik
kuṭpaṭṭa nilaṅkaḷu[m] nattamu muṟṟum puvaṉekaviraṉ cantikkum tiruppaṇikkum
kuṭiniṅkā tevatāṉa mutalaṭaṅka iṟaiyiliyākap patiṉeḻāvatu āṭimātamutal utaka

varaiyum cella mutalaṭaṅka iṟaiyiliyāka tantom tiru
veḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 77.
(A.R. No. 77 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
RĀJARĀJA I—28th YEAR (A.D. 1012-13)
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with introduction Tirumagaḷpōla etc. It records a gift
of 10 kaḷañju of gold deposited with the ūrār of Pālaippandal on the southern bank
for a perpetual lamp in the temple by a merchant of the village by name Aṅgāḍi
Poṟṟāmaṉ for (the memory of) one Pirāṭṭi Śīrāḷaṉ of Munaippāḍi Nāvalūr. The
reason for the gift is stated to be that the latter trespassed into the former's house
in the night and tried to assault his mistress in his very presence, by which he
was enraged so much as to stab the intruder to death.

l.ta:
muṅ kali[ṅ*]kamu meṇṭicai pukaḻtara viḻamaṇṭalamu miraṭṭapāṭi yeḻarai
yilakkamu

ḻutaka viḷaṅku miyāṇṭe
ceḻiyarait tecukoḷ śrīkovirāca rāca rācakecari pa-

- āvatu ivvūr viyāpāri
aṅkāṭi poṟṟāmaṉ vaiccuk ko[ṇ*]ṭiru

[ci mā]tevi peṟṟiyai muṉaippāṭi nāvalūrilirukkum pirāṭṭi ci[rā]ḷaṉ
irāttiri [ci]mātevi peṟṟiyai valiyappukku piṭi-

•••• aṅkāṭi poṟṟāmaṉ eṉ maṇavāṭṭiyai nāṉirukkave valiya
piṭiccāṉeṉṟu pukku paṭak kuttiṉa[m*]aiyil aṅkāṭi

[po]ṟṟāmaṉmel vaḻakkaḻivu ilā[m*]aiyil pirāṭṭi cirāḷamāṭait
toṭoruṅki pirāṭṭi cirāḷaṉukkāka oru no

rātittavaṟ nicata muḻakku eṇṇai
yaṭṭi viḷakkerikka aṅkāṭi poṟṟāmaṉ kuṭutta po-

[ṉ pa]tiṉ kaḻañcum koṇṭu nicata muḻakkeṇṇai yaṭṭuvomāṉo
minnāṭṭu peṇṇait tenkarai pālaippa

inscription.head:
No. 78.
(A.R. No. 78 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA—20th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 1000 kuḻi of land tax-free by one Naralōkasūriyaṉ
Munaiyadaraiyaṉ to the temple of Tāntōṉṟi-Nāyaṉār at Śaṇbai in Vāṇagōppāḍi on
the northern bank, a division of Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] tirupuvaṉacakkaravatti kulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇ[ ṭu 20]-
vatu irācarācavaḷanāṭṭup peṇṇai vaṭakarai vā[ṇa]-

l.ta:
koppāṭi caṇpai uṭaiyār tiruttāntoṉṟināyaṉāṟku naruḷoka
cū[ri]yaṉ muṉaiyataraiyaṉ naraḷoka cūri-

yanallūrile āyiraṅkuḻi iṟaiyiliviṭṭeṉ itu māṟuvāṉ taṉ
tā[y*]kku na[l]lāṉ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 79.
(A.R. No. 79 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is only an incomplete introduction of an inscription of Rājēndra-Chōḷa I
beginning with the expression Tirumaṉṉi vaḷara.

l.ta: vanpakkal ṟennavan vaitta cuntarar muṭiyum in-
ceruviṟ ciṉa[vi*] irupattorukāl araicukaḷai kaṭṭa paracu-
rāman melvaruñ cāntimaṟ ṟiyvaraṇ karuti yiru[t*]tiya

kala••••

inscription.head:
No. 80.
(A.R. No. 80 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
PAR. RĀJENDRADĒVA—3rd YEAR (A.D. 1054-55)
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with introduction Tirumaruviya Śeṅgōlvēndan etc.
This records a gift of 32 kāśu in memory of a lady by name Śēndaṉ Umaiyāḷ,
for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tāntoṉṟi-Mahādēvar at Vāḷaiyūr
alias Rājēndrapuram by Malaiyamāṉ Kāṭṭimēlūruḍaiyāṉ Paḻaṅkūṟaṉ Kuṉṟaṉ of
Kūgūrppāḍi in Narippaḷḷi-nāḍu on the southern bank. The gift is said to be in
response to a demand made by the community Nāṉgudiśai Padineṇbhūmi Nānādēśi
who held him responsible for the suicide of that lady by poison because of some
threat held out by him with regard to a tax due.

l.ta:
tirumaruvi[ya] ceṅkolventaṉ muṉṉoṉ ceṉai piṉṉatu
vāka iraṭṭapāṭi eḻarai ilakkamum koṇṭu kol-

lāpurattu cayattampamnāṭṭi muṉṉāṇai tavirttu ta[ṉ*]ṉāṇai celutti
etiramar peṟātu eṇṭicai nikaḻa paṟaiya-

tu kaṟaṅka āṅkatu keṭṭu perāṟṟaṅkarai koppa[ttu*] vantetirporuta
āha[va]mallaṉ añci puṟaṅkiṭṭoṭa ā-

ṉaiyuṅ kutiraiyum peṇṭir paṇṭāramum āṅkavaṉ vipavam aṭaṅkaluṅ
koṇṭu

vaḷanāṭṭu peṇṇait [t]eṉkarai narippaḷḷināṭṭu kūkurppāṭi
yirukku[m*] malaiyamāṉ kāṭṭimelūruṭaiyāṉ paḻaṅkūraṉ ku-

ṉṟaṉ meṟpaṭiyūrirukkum virarpu[t]tiraṉ tāy ce[n]taṉ umaiyāḷai
iṟaikāṭṭa avaḷ iṟaikaṭaveṉalleṉeṉṟu colla [a]vaḷai

kocceyvikka avaḷ nañcu kuṭittu cāva nāṉku ticai patiṉeṇpūmi
nānāteciyum kūṭa ni[r]aintu inta paḻaṅkūraṉ kuṉṟaṉmel pa-
ḻiyākki [i]vaṉme linta paḻitira koṇṭa naṭaiyāvatu vāḷaiyūrākiya

koṉṟu vai[kka]kkaṭavaṉākki ivaṉ vaitta kācu muppattiraṇṭu ikkācu
muppattiraṇṭu vāḷaiyūrā[kiya]

m koṇṭu arumoḻiteva[ṉ] marakkāloṭu okkum uḻakkāl nicatam
uḻakkeṇṇai aṭṭakaṭavom ivveṇṇai vaṇikkirāmattom
irukūṟum caṅkarappāṭiyom orukūṟum cantirātittaval itu paṉmā

inscription.head:
No. 81.
(A.R. No. 81 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJ. RĀJĀDHIRĀJA I—32nd YEAR (A.D. 1049-50)
inscription.p:
This has a long introduction beginning with the expression Tiṅgaḷērtaru
etc. The record is mutilated and incomplete towards the end. It registers an
agreement by the Śaṅkarappāḍi Nagarattār of Vāḷaiyūr in Vāṇagōppāḍi Pūrvadēśam
to burn a perpetual lamp in the temple for the 20 kāśu received by them from
one Veṇkāḍaṉ Narēndrachūḷāmaṇi of Nallāvūr in Pāmbūrnāḍu a division of
Uyyakkoṇḍārvaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:
tiṅkaḷertaru taṉṟoṅkal [veṇkuṭai]kkiḻ nilamakaḷ nilava
malarmakaḷ puṇarntu ceṅkolocci karuṅkali kaṭintu taṉ ciṟiya tātaiyai-

yun tiruttamaiyaṉaiyuṅ kuṟikoḷ taṉṉiḷaṅkokkaḷaiyum neṟiyuṇar
taṉṟirupputarvartammaiyun tuṉṟeḻil vāṉavan valla[vaṉ]

kaṉṉakucciyar kāvalaneṉap poṉṉaṇi cuṭar maṇimakuṭañ cūṭṭip paṭar
pukaḻāṅkavaṟkava[r*]nāṭaruḷi pāṅkamar tātaimuṉ

coḻaṉnena puṉaintu maṉṉupallūḻiyuḷ teṉṉavar muvaruḷ
mānāparaṇa [maṇimu]ṭi ānāpparumaṇip pacu

rapāṇṭiyaṉ oṟṟaiveṇkuṭaiyuṅ kaṟṟaiveṇ cavari
yum ciṅkācaṉa-

mum veṅkaḷattiḻantu taṉmuṭiviḻat talaivirittaṭitaḷarntoṭa tollai
[mul]laiyūrt turatti olkalil veṇāṭṭaraicaic ceṇāṭṭotukki mevupukaḻ

rāmakuṭa muvar keṭa munintu viṭarkeḻu villavaṉ kuṭarmaṭikkoṇṭu
taṉṉāṭuviṭṭoṭik kāṭupukkoḷippa vañciyam putumalar malai

ḷūrccālai kalamaṟuppittāhomallaṉu mañcak kevutaṉ [ṟā]ṅkarum
paṭaiyālāṅkavaṉ ceṉaiyuḷ kaṇṭappayanuṅ [kaṅkātira]nu[m*] vaṇṭama[r*]
kaḷiṟṟo[ṭu]

maṭiya tiṇṭiṟal varitara vikkiyum [vicaiyātittaṉum tirumuraṇ cāṅka
maiyyaṉu] mutaliṉar

ṭittut taṉṉāṭaiyil ja•• koṇṭu tuṉṉār koḷḷippākkaiy
[u]ḷḷeri maṭuttaruḷit taṉitaṉittaṇṭāṟ porutirai uṭutta [i]laṅkaiyar
komāṉ vikkirama

ṉ makuṭamum muṉṟaṉakkuṭaintu teṉṟamiḻ vaṭakalai terittu te[ca]ttavar
veṟu[maiy ni]ṅka cuṭarkaṉakattavatarittu tu[lāpāram] tuṭara eṟikaṭaṟpaṭaiyaiy
celvitte-

ḻkaṭaliḻam pukkilaṅkecanākiya vikkiramapāṇṭiyaṉ parumaṇimuṭiyuṅ
kāṇṭaku ta[ṉṉatā]kiya taṉ ka[ṉ*]ṉakucciyaṉum ārkali liḻañ ciriteṉṟeṇṇi
uḷaṅkoḷ nāṭu

ṉṉuṟavoṭum pukuntu viḷaṅku muṭikavitta viracalāmekaṉ porkaḷattañ
cittaṉ [kālkaḷi]ṟiḻintu kavvaiyuṟṟoṭik kātaliyoṭu

ya āṅka[va*]māṉam niṅkutaṟkāka miṭṭum vantu vāṭṭoḻiliḻantu
veṅkaḷattula

ciṅ[kaḷattaraca]ṉ poṉṉaṇi muṭiyuṅ kaṉṉaraṉvaḻi vanturai
keraḷattaracanākiya śrī•••

n mataṉarājaṉ elloḷirt taṭamuṭimaṇiyuṅ koṇṭu vaṭapurattiru
[kālāvatum porupa]ṭai naṭātti kaṇṭaṉ tiṉakaraṉ nāraṇaṉ kaṇavati vaṇṭalar
teriyal maticūtaṉa-

ṉaṉṟeṉaippala araicaraiy muṉaivayyiṟṟuratti [va]mpalar tarupoḻil
kampi[linakaruḷ] caḷukkiyar māḷikaiy taka[r*]ppittiḷakkamil tantaimuṉ vanta
kutakkaḷiṟṟuc cakkaravatti

vikkiramanāraṇaṉṟaṉ perumpaṭai[ya]rai evi muṉpamar āha[va]mallaṉu[m
añci]•• eṉṉum

mel koṇṭu taṇpuṉal periyāṟṟaṅkarai pūṇṭūrk
kaṭakamāna-

karavaiyil muṉ[ṟā]muṟaimaiyil [e]ṉṟetirviṭṭa maṉaṅkiḷar
ṉeṭuvel [teluṅka] viccaiyaṉ ṟampiyar iruvaruma[ṟu]mpiyar cilakkai atta
rājaṉum akkappayaṉum

vikkiyar kaḷiṟṟup piṭakkac coḻaṉum coḻaviccayaṉum koṇṭayarājaṉum
kuḷicilai [muñcaya]ṉum mikkuyarkaḷiṟṟup piṭakkac coḻaṉum miṉappayaṉum viramāṇik
kaṉum eṉ

paṭaiñaroṭetirt tamar tutarkurājaṉum avaṉṟuṇaiy virarum vākai
viccayaṉ makaṉum mātāvum eṉumavar mutaliyar puṉaikaḻal araicarroṭu eṉamaru
makaḷiraiy tuṇ-

ṭittu eḻaraiyilakkamu[m*] uyarpāḻpaṭuttaruḷi pūṇṭūrpatiyai poṭi
paṭuttamaiyil īṇṭokaimukaiyarercela naṭātti vārkavaṭivitaittu maṇṇeṉa
tanāppi pār

mpa
nāṭṭuvittuyya liyacai vi[l*]lavar miṉavar veḻkulac caḷukkiyar va[l*]lavar
kaucalar vaṅkaṇar koṅkaṇar cinturarayyaṇar

ramutaliyar araicar iṭu tiṟaikaḷum āṟil l[oṉ]
ṟavaṉil kūṟukoḷ porukaḷum uyarnta nāṉmaṟaiyavar mukantukoḷak kuṭuttu

metañ ceytaracu viṟṟirunta jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻaṉ ṉuyarnta perumpukaḻ
kovirājakecari

••••• caṅkara[p*]pāṭi nakarattom kaittiṭṭu uyyakkoṇṭārvaḷa
nāṭṭu pāmpūrnāṭṭu nallāvū[r*] nallāvūrātai ūrāṉ veṇkāṭaṉāṉa
cūḷāmaṇi muventaveḷārkku nāṅkaḷ eḻuttiṭṭu iṭṭukuṭutta paricāvatu i-

edition.l:
••••••••• ṟoṉṟimātevar śrīkoyilil āṭavalārkku ivar vaitta
ti[ rununtā] viḷakku oṉṟu ittirununtāviḷakku oṉṟukkum ivāṭṭai āṭi mātam mutal
ivar eṅkaḷvacam tanta aṉṟāṭu nalkācu 20 ikkācu i-

l.ta:
••••••••• ḷapāy nicatam araicceviṭeṇṇaiyāka ikkācirupatukkum
nicatam muḻakkeṇṇai te••••••••• paramaṉ

kāyaneṉṉu marakkāloṭokkum
uḻa[kkā]l eṇṇai aṭṭi ca

inscription.head:
No. 82.
(A.R. No. 82 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PAR. RĀJĒNDRA CHŌḶA (I)—25th YEAR (A.D. 1036-37)
inscription.p:
This records an agreement given by the Nagarattār of Vāḷaiyūr alias Nitta-
vinōdapuram on the northern bank of the Peṇṇai, situated in Vāṇagappāḍi alias
Madhurāntaka-vaḷanāḍu in Jayangoṇḍaśōḻamaṇḍalam to the temple of Tāntōṉṟi-
uḍaiya-Mahādēvar, that they would meet the cost of burning the perpetual lamps
and of the other requirements in the temple to which they had been (previously)
committed, by means of certain specified imposts on their articles of merchandise.

ṟakkuvān eḻunarakattu kiḻnarakam pukuvān itu panmā-

inscription.head:
No. 83.
(A.R. No. 83 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJ. VĪRARĀJĒNDRA—3rd YEAR (A.D. 1065-66)
inscription.p:
This begins with a short introduction commencing with the words Vīramē
tuṇaiyāgavum. It records a gift of 10 kāśu for burning a lamp during the
three services in the temple of Tāntōṉṟiuḍaiya-Mahādēva by Tirumukkaṉ alias
Nambiyārūraṉ of Kommaipākkam a merchant of Vīrarājēndrapuram in Vīra-
rājēndra-vaḷanāḍu. The amount is said to have been deposited with the Tiru-
vuṇṇāḻigai-sabhā of the temple. The writing is engraved in a careless manner.

edition.l:
rājakecarivaṉmar[āna uṭaiyār śrī virarācentira]dēvaṟ[ ku yāṇṭu
3]- āvatu
virarājentravaḷanāṭṭup peṇṇai vaṭakarai vi[ rarācentra]-
kai sabhaiyom cantirātittaval itu panmāhēśva[ rarakṣai] [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 84.
(A.R. No. 84 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJA I—24th YEAR (A.D. 1008-9)
inscription.p:
This begins with introduction Tirumagaḷ pōla etc. It records a grant of land 22
Śeṟuvu (?) in extent made by the sabhā of Neṟkuṉṟam alias Vayiramēgha-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam to the ūrār of Kolliyūr in exchange for the lands acquired on their behalf
from the latter by Rājarājā-Vāṇakōvaraiyar for the construction of a tank called the
Araiyagaḷ-ādittappērēri in their village. The amount of the taxes on the lands
payable to the king by the Ūrār is said to have been 1000 kaḻañju of gold.

l.ta:
viṭṭa nilam taṅkaḷ ceṟuvaṭaiya [t]evampokiyum valla[ru]vūṭṭiyu

vukke āyirak kaḻañcu poṉ maṉṟapeṟuva[r*]kaḷāka icaintukuṭuttom
neṟku-

inscription.head:
No. 85.
(A.R. No. 85 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA—12th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 500 kuḻi of land at Araṅgam alias Śōḻaṉ-Arambai-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam on the southern bank of the Peṇṇai for the requirement of
offerings during the ardhajāma service in the temple of Tāṉtōṉṟi-Āḷuḍaiyanāyaṉār
at Śaṇbai alias Vīrarājēndrapuram in Vāṇagōppāḍi a division of Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu,
by Soṉṉatoṅgap-Pallavaraiyaṉ Āḷavandāṉ Nīrēṟṟaperumāḻ alias Rājarāja Śiśupālaṉ
of Pērāyūr.

edition.l: ḻatevaṟkku yāṇṭu 12- vatu rājarājavaḷaṉāṭṭu vā-

l.ta: eḻānnarakattil kiḻānarakam pukuvāṉ tan miṇāṭṭiyai ta-
ṉ kutiraikku pulliṭum paṟaiyaṉṉukku kuṭuppāṉ itu

inscription.head:
No. 86.
(A.R. No. 86 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJA I—26th YEAR (A.D. 1010-11)
inscription.p:
The introduction is Tirumagaḷ pōla etc. The inscription is left incomplete.
It mentions an order of Maṟavaṉ Narasiṁhapanmaṉ alias Rājarāja-Vāṇakōvaraiyar
to his subordinates to find out a land in the brahmadēya Koṭṭagam on the northern
bank in his nāḍu (i.e., under his jurisdiction) which would suffice to yield paddy at a
kuṟuṇi and 4 nāḻi per day as required for offerings to the god during the three services
in the temple of Tāṉtōṉṟi-Āḷvār. A panegyric of this chief is given in Sanskrit after
the regnal year of the king, calling him an “ornament of Mahābali-kula and
“Parivipuraparamēśvara”.

inscription.head:
No. 87.
(A.R. No. 87 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA—24th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of land after its reclamation for meeting the requirements
of worship on the day of festival in the month of Puraṭṭādi in the temple of
Tāntōṉṟīśvaramuḍaiyār at Śaṇbai alias Vīrarājēndrapuram in Vāṇagōppāḍi a division
of Rājarājavaḷanāḍu, by one Aḻagiyaperumāḷ daughter of Puṇṇīyañjeydāḷ a dēva-
raḍiyāḷ of the Tiruvēgambamuḍaiyār (Ekāmranātha) temple at Kāñchīpuram, who is
called the aṇukki (mistress ?) of Aḻagiyāṉ Ākāraśūraṉ alias Rājagambhīra-Chēdiya-
rāyaṉ the Malaiyamān chief of Kiḷiyūr.

edition.l: coḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu 24- vatu irācarācavaḷanāṭṭu

iṭai kumari-

inscription.head:
No. 88.
(A.R. No. 88 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
‘MAHAMAṆḌALĒŚVARA’ SADĀŚIVA-MAHĀRĀJA
Ānanda (Śaka 1477 = A.D. 1155)
inscription.p:
This seems to record the gift of a house in the Naḍutteru to the temple of
Tāntōṉṟi-Āḷudaiyanayiṉār and some land to provide for offerings when the deity
was taken in procession to this place. This service was probably entrusted to one
Uṇṇāmulai-Nayinār Ellappar.

edition.l: ṇṇāṟu velaṉuḷpaṭa kuḻi 300-

inscription.head:
No. 89.
(A.R. No. 89 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
ACHYUTADĒVA-MAHĀRĀYA—Śaka 1455 (A.D. 1534)
inscription.p:
The details of the date are given as Jaya, Chittirai 24, Saptami, Monday,
Pushya. (A.D. 1534, April 20. The Śaka year was 1456). The inscription
records the appointment of a certain Śeṇbagarāyappichchar as general supervisor for
getting some specified services performed in the temple of Tāṉtōṉṟi-Āḷuḍaiya-
nāyanār by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Bhōgaiyadēva-Mahārāja, the Sthānattār of the
temple, the Ūravar and the Palapaṭṭaḍai-śīrmaiyār. He was provided with a
house to reside, some land and a portion of the offered food from the temple daily.

irāca ayyaṉum tāṉattārum ūravar palapaṭṭaṭai cirmaiyārumāka

māṉiyam pututteruvil campantaperumāḷ nayiṉār koyilukku

inscription.head:
No. 90.
(A.R. No. 90 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA
IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
RĀJARĀJADĒVA—16th YEAR.
inscription.p:
The details of date are given as Tulā, śu. 10, Sunday, Svāti which yield the
equivalent A.D. 1232, Octr. 24. The Nakshatra was Śadayam on this date. The
inscription is built in at the beginning of the lines. It records a gift of 500 kuḻi of
land at Tamiṭṭanallūr in Meykuṉṟa-nāḍu belonging to Vāṇavichchādaravēḷāṉ, as
tax-free dēvadāna for the requirements of offerings to goddess Tirukkāmakkōṭṭattu-
Aḻagiyanāchchiyār in the temple of Tāṉtōṉṟi-Āḷuḍaiyanāyanār at Śeṇbai alias
Vīrarājēndraśōḻapuram in Vāṇagōppāḍi-nāḍu by Marundāḻvāṉāyaṉ [of Pērāyūr ?].

edition.l:
••••••••• śrīrā[ja*]rājadēvaṟkku yāṇṭu 16- vatu tulānāyaṟṟu
pūrvvapakṣattu nāyaṟṟukkiḻamaiyum dacamiyum peṟṟa cotināḷ peṇṇai vaṭakarai
vāṇakoppāṭināṭṭuc ceṇpaiyāṉa virarājentiracoḻapura[t]tu uṭaiyār
tiruttāṉtoṉṟi āḷuṭaiyanāyaṉār tirukkāmakkoṭṭattu aḻakiyanācciyārkkut
tiruvamutu kaṟiyamutu tirume[y*]ppūccu kuṟaipaṭāmalc ceyya perāyūruṭaiyāṉ

yaṉeṉ peṇṇait teṉkarai meykuṉṟa nāṭṭut
tamiṭṭanallū[r*] vāṇaviccātiraveḷāṉ taṉmappaṭṭiyil viṭṭakuḻi aiñūṟum

ttavarai iṟaiyili tevatāṉamāka viṭṭeṉ maruntāḻvāṉāyaṉeṉ itu
māṟuvāṉ eḻānarakattiṟ kiḻānarakam pukuvāṉ paṉmāhe

inscription.head:
No. 91.
(A.R. No. 91 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. VIKRAMA CHŌḶA—Date lost.
inscription.p:
The inscription is mutilated at the end of the lines. It seems to register
some gift (the nature of which is not clear) to the temple of Tāntōṉṟi-Āḷuḍaiyār
at Śeṇbai by Śōḻan Periyāṉ a shepherd (maṉṟāḍi) of the place in expiation of his
wife's death at his hands (though unintentionally in a quarrel ?). Mention is
made of “Diśai viḷaṅgu Diśai-Āyirattaiññūṟṟuvar” on whose accusation evidently
he made this gift.

l.ta:
ṉṟu tannakamuṭaiyāḷaik [k]aiyāle taḷḷa avaḷ marik[ka]•• kuticai
•••••••
melintap paḻiyākki ipperiyānmel paḻitira innāṭṭup pe••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 92.
(A.R. No. 92 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. VIKRAMA-CHŌḶA—9th YEAR (A.D. 1126-7)
inscription.p:
The inscription is built in at the beginning of the lines. It seems to record a
gift of 32 cows for burning a lamp in the temple of Tāntonṟīśvaram-Uḍaiyār at
Śaṇbai by one Kūḍaluḍaiyāṉ Kulattāṉ Śēndaṉ of Kaḍambūr on the southern bank
of the river in Vāṇagappāḍi (alias) Rājendra-vaḷanāḍu to atone for the death
caused by him, of Pichchaṉ Paṉman Kaṇḍan of the same village in a quarrel (?).
The relatives of the victim are said to have been consulted (by the donor ?) as
to the course of action to be taken. Another gift of 32 cows to the temple of
Tirunāgīśvaram-Uḍaiyār is mentioned at the end.

l.ta:
••••••••• ṇarap puvimātu vaḷara nām[ā*]tu vi[ḷa*]ṅka jayamātu nilava

inscription.head:
No. 93.
(A.R. No. 93 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
MAHĀMAṆḌALĒŚVARA VIRŪPĀKSHADĒVA—Śaka 1393 (A.D. 1471)
inscription.p:
The details of the date are given as Nandana, Mīna, ba. 9, Monday,
Uttirāḍam which yield the equivalent A.D. 1473, March 22. The Śaka year
should be 1394.

The inscription states that as a result of the Oḍḍiyaṉ-galabhai in the
country, the worship in the temple of Tāṉtōṉṟi-Nāyanār at Śaṇbai had been
stopped for ten years, and the walls of the temple itself had fallen down and the
precincts had been filled with sand. In the year quoted Immaḍi Rāmappa-Nāyaka
Timmappa-Nāyaka•• a chieftain of the king seems to have revived the worship
in the temple, by remitting as sarvamānya 150 poṉ out of the 200 being paid as
jōḍi and śūlavari to the king, leaving the remaining 50 for payment as land tax
in future.

l.ta: tu immaṭi irāmappa nāyakka timmappa nāyakka[r*]
•••• m carintu āṟukutti alaieṟum carintu puṉattalaiāy iruk[k*]aiyil

a[y*]mpatu poṉ

m ākak kaḻittu jaya varuṣam mutalukku coṭi cūlavarikku
eṉṟeṉṟaikkum aimpatu poṉ varuṣam toṟum

kiḻāṉarakam pukuvāṉ paṉmā

inscription.head:
No. 94.
(A.R. No. 94 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
VIRA-NARASIṄGARĀYA son of SĀḶUVA-NARASIṄGARĀYA—Śaka 1429.
inscription.p:
The details of date given viz., Prabhava, Tulā, śu. 12, Monday, Uttiraṭṭādi
yield the equivalent A.D. 1507, October 18.

This registers a gift of 600 kuḻi of land in the dēvadāna village [Āru]kaṇ-
maṅgalam belonging to the Svayambhunāthar temple, after purchasing the same
from the sthānattār of the temple itself, by Rāmayaśōḻa-Mahārājaṉ, son of Mahā-
maṇḍalēśvara Pottayaśōḻa-Mahārāja, the chief of Uṟaiyūr, to provide for offerings
and worship during two services in the temple. The chief claims to belong to
the Solar race and the Kāśyapa-gōtra and is mentioned with some birudas such
as Anṉimbara-gaṇḍaṉ, Mūvar-gaṇḍaṉ and Tribhuvanavīra-Veṉṉaśammaṭṭi.

l.ta:
rāyarakaṇṭaṉ cāḷuva naraciṅkarāyar kumārar viranaraciṅkarāyar piṟutivi
rācciyam

edition.l:

inscription.head:
No. 95.
(A.R. No. 95 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. KULOTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA—27th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This registers a gift of 200 kuḻi of wet land at Guṇamaṅgalam after purchase
by certain merchants (Daṉma-vāṇiyar) of the place to provide for the supply of
articles of apparel (?) required for god Tāṉtōṉṟi-Āḷuḍaiyār.

edition.l:
yāṇṭu 27- vatu tirutāṉtoṉṟi āḷvuṭaiyār koyil cimākecura
kaṅkā-

kumpamu-

inscription.head:
No. 96.
(A.R. No. 96 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL OF THE OUTER MAṆḌAPA.
KŌ-PERUÑJIṄGADĒVA—16th YEAR.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. XII No.

inscription.head:
No. 97.
(A.R. No. 97 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE EAST WALL OF THE SECOND PRĀKĀRA.
KANNARADĒVA (KṚISHṆA III)—Date Lost.
inscription.p:
The inscription is mutilated towards the end. It registers a sale of land,
eight mā in extent, by the sabhā of Raṇabhīmamaṅgalam in Vāṇagoppāḍi on the
southern bank of the Peṇṇai, for worship to god Aiññūṟṟuva-Vīdiviḍaṅgar
(Naṭarāja) in the temple of Tāntōṉṟi-Īśvara at Vāḷaiyūr on the northern bank,
for 40 poṉ (corresponding to 1(1/2) kaḻañju) received by them.

l.ta:
[vaṟ]ku yāṇṭu•• āvatu vāṇakoppāṭit teṉ
karai iraṇa-

oru kaḻañcarai poṉ koṇṭu kaṭavom
ippoṉ
ḷḷiṭṭut taṭi iraṇṭu [karattu] eṭṭu māvum icceṟuve aṭaiya teṟki-

inscription.head:
No. 98.
(A.R. No. 98 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE SAME PRĀKĀRA.
TRIBH. KULAŚĒKHARA (MĀR. KULAŚĒKHARA I)—33rd YEAR
(A.D. 1300-1)
inscription.p:
This registers the alienation of 900 kuḻi of dry land at Maṉanduyyaperumāḷ-
nallūr which was a tax-free dēvadāna of the temple of Tāntōṉṟi-Āḷuḍaiya-Nāyaṉār
at Śaṇbai alias Vīrarājēndraśōḻapuram in Vāṇagoppāḍi-nāḍu on the northern bank
of the Peṇṇai in favour of a Brāhmaṇa of Maṇalūr by name Tirukkoṭṭi-Āḻvāṉ
Periyāṉ Īśurabaṭṭaṉ for his services on the days of festival in the temple. It is
stated that the sthānikas received from him 31 paṇam in lumpsum for the payment
of kaḍamai on the land levied by the nāḍu.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] tirupuvaṉaccakkaravatti ci kulacekara tevaṟku yāṇṭu [3]3-
vatu peṇ-

purattu uṭaiyār

ti[ru]ttāntoṉṟi āḷuṭaiyanāyaṉār tevatāṉa iṟaiyili maṉantuyyap
perumāḷ nal-

lūr puṉceynilam āṟṟaṅkaraiyil kuṭiniṅkāt tevatāṉamāka maṇalūr
pirāmaṇaril

tirukkoṭṭiāḻvāṉ periy[ā*]ṉ īcurapaṭṭaṟkku vilaiyāka viṟṟa
maṇalūr kaṭuntuḷikku meṟ-

ippaṭik[kā]-

inscription.head:
No. 99.
(A.R. No. 99 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE WALL OF THE KITCHEN, LEFT OF ENTRANCE.
TRIBH. VIJAYARĀJĒNDRADĒVA—4th YEAR.
(KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA I ?)
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It records the construction of this tirumaḍaippaḷḷi (kitchen)
in the temple of Tāntōṉṟi-Āḷuḍaiyanāyanār at Śaṇbai alias Vīrarājēndrapuram
in Vāṇagōppāḍi (a division) of Rājarājavaḷanāḍu by a lady by name [Mēgan
Karuṇaiyāḷi] alias Svāmikkuppiḻaiyādāḷ wife of Akkivaḷavaṉ Pallavaraiyaṉ alias
Maṇḍaippiḷḷai Vijayarāyaṉ, the headman of Kurugāḍi in Tenkiḻār-nāḍu a division of
Nittavinōda-vaḷanāḍu. She seems to have also made a gift of land as tirumaḍaip-
paḷḷippuṟam (for its maintenance) and to meet the expenses of a festival in Puraṭṭādi
month.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrī vicaiyarācentiratevaṟku
yāṇṭu nālāvatu i

ṇakoppāṭic caṇpaiyāṉa virarājentirapurattu uṭaiyār tiruttān
to[ṉ*]ṟi āḷuṭaiya nāyaṉār[k]ku

ittirumaṭaippaḷḷi ceyvittāḷ nittaviṉotavaḷanāṭṭut tenkiḻār
nāṭṭuk kurukāṭic ceyamāṉa

ka[ruṇaiyāḷi]āṉa cuvāmikkup piḻaiyātāḷ paṇi iṉ•••• ṟkut tiru
maṭaippaḷḷippuṟamum puraṭṭā-

tit tirunāḷukkum viṭṭa viḷainilaṅ kuṉṟatteri etir••••ruṅkoḷ
viḷākam i••••nirpāya-

inscription.head:
No. 100.
(A.R. No. 100 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE AKHILĀṆḌĒŚVARĪ SHRINE.
RĀJ. VĪRARĀJĒNDRA—6th YEAR (A.D. 1068-69)
inscription.p:
This begins with introduction Vīramē tuṇaiyāgavum etc. It records a grant
tax free, of 500 kuḻi of land in the dēvadāna village Tagaḍi and another 40 kuḻi
(as house site and garden ?) besides some dry land in addition as kappaḍa vīśam (?)
by the sthānattār of the temple of Tirukkaṟṟaḷi-Mahādēva at Vīrarājēndrapuram
on the northern bank of the Peṇṇai in Rājēndra-vaḷanāḍu, to one Araṅgaṉ Śēndaṉ
of Akkaṇavallam, who is called the nagarakkaṇakkaṉ of the place, for looking
after all the accounts of the temple. Among the temple authorities mention is
made of a certain Mahāvratin Lakulīśvara-Paṇḍita the head of a maṭha attached
to the temple.

l.ta:
y[āka]me a[ṇiy]ākavum ceṅ
koloccik karuṅkali kaṭi

ṅ kaikkoṇṭu [i]rukālāvatum porkaḷaṅ kunittu kuṟitta
kaḷattāhavamallaṉai aiñcivittu

kolāl kuḻi

til paḻaiya maṭa-

ttukku vaṭakkil kallukku vaṭakku kuḻi nāṟpatum muṟṟūṭṭum iṟaiyili
uṇṭaṉu

eppe(o)ṟpaṭṭa kaṇakkum eḻutakkaṭavanākavum ivaṉukku
kappaṭavicattukku tiruva-

m ivvilakuḷiśvarapaṇḍitan nuḷḷiṭṭa sthāṉattom itu śrī panmāyeśvara
rakṣai ||—

inscription.head:
No. 101.
(A.R. No. 101 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
The inscription is in modern characters (of about the 18th century A.D.)
and is dated Khara, Śittirai 15. It records the gift of a house-site, 48 feet in width,
on the east street by the temple authorities of Tāmtōṉṟi-Āḷuḍaiyanayiṉār at Śaṇbai
to a person by name Śivaṉ Kāḷatti-nayiṉār of Narippari-nāḍu for the construction
of a maṭha thereon, and the privilege of collecting annually ten paṇam each from
the temple and the residents of the place as tirukkaivaḻakkam for its maintenance.

l.ta:
tiyati caṇpai uṭaiyār tiruttām
to[ṉ]ṟi āḷuṭaiyanayiṉār koyil paṇṭārattār narippari nāṭṭil civaṉ
kāḷattinayiṉārkku taṉmacātaṉam

paṇṇikuṭuttapaṭi nāḷatumutal caṇpaiyil kiḻakku tiruvitiyil kiḻciṟakil
kempu irāvuttaṉ irunta maṉaikku vaṭakku paṇipeṇṭāṭṭi malli maṉaikku teṟku
tiruvitikku kiḻakku

naṉtavaṉattukku meṟku iṉṉāṅku ellaikku uḷppaṭṭa maṉai oṉṟu[k]ku
aṭi

inta maṉai tāṅkaḷ maṭam kaṭṭikkoṇṭu iruntu tirukkaivaḻakkam
koyil paṇṭārattile pattu paṇamum

uḷḷūrilum cirmaiyilum pattu paṇamum āka varuṣattukku irupatu paṇamum
vaṟttiṉai āka paṟṟikkoṇṭu cantiṟātittavaraiyum cukattile irukkakaṭavarkaḷā
kavum ippaṭi cammatittu

coṉṉa avarkaḷ keṅkai karaiyil kārāmpacuvaik koṉṟa pāvattile poka
kaṭavaṉṉākavum naripparināṭṭu-

c civaṉukku caṇpai koyil paṇṭārattārom . ivai nāṟpatteṇ
ṇāyi[ra*]nampi eḻuttu ivai ālāla nampi eḻu

inscription.head:
No. 102.
(A.R. No. 102 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
TRIBH. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA (III)—14th YEAR (A.D. 1191-2).
inscription.p:
This records the endowment of a wet land as dēvadāna to the temple of
Tirukkapālīśvaram-Uḍaiyār at Śaṇbai alias Vīrarājēndrapuram on the northern
bank of the Peṇṇai in Vāṇagōppāḍi a division of Rājarājavaḷanāḍu by Śēdiyarāyaṉ
Ākāraśūraṉ alias Rājagambhīrach-Chēdiyarāyaṉ the Malayamāṉ chief of Kiḷiyūr.
Rājagambhīra was a title of Rājarāja II (M.E.R. 1927, Part II, para 25).

inscription.head:
No. 103.
(A.R. No. 103 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
ACHYUTADĒVA-MAHĀRĀYA—Śaka 1544, Nandana
(Mistake for Śaka 1454 = A.D. 1532-3)
inscription.p:
The details of the date are given as Nandana, Mithuna, śu. 10, Hastā,
Monday corresponding to A.D. 1532, June 10. This tithi was śu. 8 on this date.
The inscription registers an undertaking given by the residents of Śaṇbai and the
surrounding villages, comprising the Tānattār (Sthānikas), the Kaikkōḷas, the Seṭṭis
and the members of the 98 castes of the Iḍaṅgai and Valaṅgai communities, to
pay a contribution by impost of one paṇam from each big family and half a
paṇam from a small one and also one paṇam on occasions of marriage, to provide
for the maintenance of a maṭha presided over by Kāḷatti-Āṇḍār Magadaimaṇḍala-
Śivaṉ disciple of Perumkaṟchūla-Parvatammāḷ Kāttayar (?). This is very probably
the same maṭha mentioned in No. 101 above.

l.ta:
ṉ makāmaṇṭalecuraṉ pāṣaikkut tappuva irāyar
kaṇṭaṉ muvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭa nāṭu koṇṭu koṇṭa nāṭu kuṭāt[ā]ṉ ke[ca]
veṭṭai kaṇṭaruḷiya [vira]ppiṟutāpan naraciṅka-
teyva kumāra accuttaiya teyva makā irā[ya*]r piṟuti[vi*] irācciya

tunāḷ peṇṇai vaṭakarai vāṇakoppāṭi nāṭṭil
caṇpai uṭaiyār tiruttāmtoṉṟi āḷuṭaiya na[yi*]ṉār tev[va]tāṉamāna
[ca]ṇpaiāṉa [vira]rācentira[co]ḻapura

ḷar ceṭṭiyaḷ koyil cirmai[yi]l uḷḷa perum kaṟcūla paṟvatammāḷ
kāttayar ciṣyaṉ ciṣ[ya*]ṉ kāḷatti āṇṭār makataimaṇṭala civaṉukku nammuṭa
koyil cimaiāṉa caṇpaiyu[m] maṟṟum uṇṭāṉa kiṟāmam•• palapaṭṭaṭaiyum
iṭaṅkai vala-
ṅkai toṇṇūṟṟueṭṭu cātiyum ākavum kāḷatti āṇṭār maṭa

tukku kaṭṭaḷai iṭṭapaṭikku kuṭikku [o]ru paṇamum ciṟukkuṭikku
araipaṇamum kaliyāṇa

(varaiyum naṭatta)kaṭavomākavum yitukku akutam paṇṇiṉavaṉ kaṅkaik
karaiyil kārāmpacuvaiyum taṉṉiṭa tāyi takappaṉaiyum kuruvaiyum konṟavan pāvat
[ti]le poka[k*] kaṭa[va]ṉākavum [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 104.
(A.R. No. 104 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
SADĀŚIVADĒVA-MAHĀRĀYA—Śaka [1471]
inscription.p:
The date is given as Saumya, Tulā śu. 10, Rōhiṇi, Monday corresponding
probably to A.D. 1549, October 30. It was Wednesday and Śatabhishak on this
date.

This records an endowment of the village Śuttamalligai with all its wet and
dry lands in Meykuṉṟāda-vaḷanāḍu alias Narippaḷḷi-nāḍu in Magadai-Maṇḍalam
on the southern bank of the Pennai, by Rāmappa-Nāyakar (son of ?) Aḍappam
Sūrappa-Krishṇama-Nāyakar for the merit of the king, for burning 30 lamps during
the services every day in the shrine of Aḻagiya-Nāchchiyār in the temple of Tāṉtōṉṟi-
Āḷudaiyanayiṉār at Śaṇbai.

l.ta:
mahamaṇṭalecura metiṉi micura kaṇṭa kaṭṭāri
cāḷuva naraciṅkateva

irāyar
piṟutivi irācciyam paṇṇi aruḷāniṉṟa cākāttam••• ka itanmel cellāniṉṟa
cavvummiya cavvaccaram tulānāyaṟṟu pūṟuvapakṣattu tecamiyum rocaṇiyum peṟṟa
comavārattunāḷ peṇṇai vaṭakarai

vāṇakoppāṭināṭu virarācentiracoḻapuramāṉa caṇpai uṭaiyār tirut
tāṉtoṉṟi āḷuṭaiyanayiṉār ammai aḻakiyanācciyār ammaikku cantiviḷakku
cuvāmi aṭappam cūrappanāyakkaraiyyaṉ ki

ṭṭaman[āyakkarai]yya(ṉ) cuvāmi catāciva
tevamaharāyaṟku taṉmamā[ka] ammai aḻakiyanācciyār ammaikkuc cantiviḷakkukku
koḷḷa irāmappa nāyakkar viṭṭa

ciṟuvaṅkūr cirmaiyāṉa maṅkalattu piṭākai cuttamallikai ciṟuvaṅkūr
nāṭṭavar nāṭṭukkaṇakkappiḷḷai muṉpāka peṇṇaikkut teṉkarai makataimaṇṭala

tu
meykuṉṟāta vaḷanāṭāṉa narippaḷḷi nāṭṭil cuttamallikaiyil nañcai puñcai
nāṟpāṟkellaiyum cantiviḷakkukku tiṉam oṉṟukku muppatu viḷakkāka kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭu
cantirātittavaraiyum celvatāka inta

cuttamallikai kalveṭṭiviṭṭom inta cuttamallikai nāṟpāṟ kellaikku
viparam peṇṇai āṟṟukku teṟku taṉmam yiṭṭa nallūr yellaikku meṟku ārukaip
pāṭi yellaikkum ātaṉūr yellaikkum vaṭakku purocappaṭṭu yellaikkuk kiḻakku
yiṉṉāṉkellaiyum uḷpaṭṭa cuttamallikai cantiviḷakkukku viṭṭom inta cutta
mallikai caṇpait teva

inscription.head:
No. 105.
(A.R. No. 105 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
VIKRAMA-CHŌḶA—8th YEAR (A.D. 1125-6)
inscription.p:
This is built in at the beginning of each line. It seems to record a gift of
the taxes Śiṟupāḍikāval and Śilvari on lands at Tagaḍi a dēvadāna village on the
southern bank of the Peṇṇai for the mantrapōnagam offering to the deity during
the early morning service in the temple of Tirukkaṟṟaḷi-Mahādēva at Śeṇbai, by
Sagarayaṉ Anupaman Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-Malaiyakularāyan the [Malayamān] chief
of Kiḷiyūr in Tirumuṉaippāḍi Kuṉṟattūr-nāḍu a division of Gaṅgaikoṇḍaśōḻa-
vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:
ṟku kaṅkai
koṇṭa coḻavaḷanāṭṭut tirumuṉaippāṭik kuṉṟattūrnāṭṭuk kiḷi-

••••••• n anupa[ma]nāna kulottuṅkacoḻa malaikularāyanen ittevar
tevatāṉam peṇṇait tenkarait takaṭiyil nance[y*] nilattil pa-
ponakam amutupaṭikkuc ceytu kuṭutten cakarayan a[ṉu]pa-

inscription.head:
No. 106.
(A.R. No. 106 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
ACHYUTADĒVA—Śaka 1552 (wrong for 1454)
inscription.p:
The date is given as Nandana, Karkaṭaka, śu. 10, Monday, Rōhiṇi perhaps
corresponding to A.D. 1532, September 9, but the month was Kanyā.

This records an endowment, free of all taxes, of the village Ākkaippāḍi in
Meykuṉṟa-nāḍu alias Narippari-nāḍu in Magadai-maṇḍalam on the southern bank
of the Peṇṇai for the requirements of worship and offerings during the service
instituted in the name of the king in the temple of Tāmtōṉṟi-Āḷuḍaiyanayiṉār at
Śaṇbai, by Vaiyappa-Nāyakkar. Mention is made of the administration (of the
division) by Venkaṭādri-Nāyakkar.

l.ta: irāyar piṟuti [vi*] irācciyam paṇṇi aruḷāniṉṟa cakā-

taṉ kuru-

inscription.head:
No. 107.
(A.R. No. 107 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON A STONE LYING ON THE FLOOR CLOSE TO THE
SOUTH WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
PARĀNTAKA I—date lost.
inscription.p:
This is a mere fragment of an inscription which seems to refer to a gift of
land for (the worship of) the goddess Jyēshṭhā stationed on the side of the tank.

l.ta: śrī matirai koṇṭa koppara kecari paṉ•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 108.
(A.R. No. 108 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE FLOOR
AT THE SECOND ENTRANCE.
PARĀNTAKA I—34th Year (A.D. 940-41)
inscription.p:
The inscription is much damaged. It seems to record the construction of
a maṇḍapa (in the temple ?) by Vīranāraṇiyār the consort of prince Gaṇḍarāditya
and daughter (in law) of Śōḻamādēviyār in the name of the Nagarattār of Vāḷaiyūr,
and an endowment of seventy kaḻañju of gold by that body by levy of tax for its
upkeep (?) and for burning a lamp in it during the nights with the interest thereon.

inscription.head:
No. 109.
(A.R. No. 109 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON A SLAB SET UP ON THE LEFT SIDE OF THE
SECOND ENTRANCE.
KARIKĀLA CHŌḶA—27th YEAR
inscription.p:
The last few lines of the inscription are badly damaged. The sense of the
record is rather obscure. It seems to register the consecration of a temple of
Mahādēva by Vāḷaiyūr-Nakkar Yōgavāṇar a resident of Raṇabhīma-maṅgalam on
the southern bank of the Peṇṇai, and an endowment made for worship therein by
Vāṇakōvaraiyar Vīraparumar the chief of Vāṇagappāḍi-nāḍu by means of taxation
on the lands at that village. The ūravar seem to have been made responsible for
the collection and payment of the amount into the temple.

l.ta: tevaṟku innāṭuṭaiya vāṇakovaraiyar viraparumar eṅkaḷ vaṅ-
k kuṭuttārkaḷ nām [vāṇa]kappāṭi nāṭṭai aḻaittu i
m maṟṟuḷḷa āyaṅkaḷum śrī kāriyamellām nāṭu celu-

edition.p:
The rest is damaged. Four lamp-stands of increasing height and a pair of feet between the
third and fourth lamp-stands are cut in relief below the inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 110.
(A.R. No. 110 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE GŌPURA
AT THE SECOND ENTRANCE.
KANNARADĒVA (RĀSHṬRAKŪṬA KṚISHṆA III)—23rd YEAR (A.D. 960-61)
inscription.p:
This is a mere fragment of an inscription which seems to record a gift of
money for burning a lamp in the temple, by a resident of Karuppūr in Miṟaik-
kūṟṟam who left it in charge of the Nagarattār of Vāḷaiyūr.

inscription.head:
No. 111.
(A.R. No. 111 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE WALL OF THE GŌPURA (AT THE
FIRST ENTRANCE, RIGHT SIDE)
PARĀNTAKA I—35th YEAR (A.D. 941-42).
inscription.p:
The inscription is badly engraved. It seems to record an endowment of
land at Kaḍambūr by (the palace accountant ?) Śrī Pondaiyuḍaiyāṉ••••for the
daily requirements of worship in the temple (name lost).

inscription.head:
No. 112.
(A.R. No. 112 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
KANNARADĒVA (RĀSHṬRAKŪṬA KṚISHṆA III)—26th YEAR (A.D. 964-65)
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 96 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp before the
image of Sūryadēva and an endowment of 5 kaḻañju of gold for the daily offering
to the deity, in the temple of Tāntōṉṟi-Āḷvār at Vāḷaiyūr, by the ūrār of Kumaṇa-
maṅgalam.

l.ta: var ūr [kuma]ṇamaṅkalattu āyam kūṭṭi oru na

[ṟinu]kku nicatam uḻakkāka vai[c]ca cāvāmuvāpperāṭu toṇṇāṟṟāṟum
tiruvamu-

inscription.head:
No. 113.
(A.R. No. 113 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
KŌPERUÑJIṄGADĒVA—14th YEAR.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions Vol. XII, No. 182.

inscription.head:
No. 114.
(A.R. No. 114 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
TRIBH. VIJAYARĀJĒNDRADĒVA (KULŌTTUṄGA I ?)—4th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records the construction of the entrance and the gōpura of the temple of
Tāntōṉṟi-Āḷuḍaiyanāyanār at Śaṇbai alias Vīrarājēndraśōḻapuram in Vāṇagōppāḍi
by Vaḷavaṉ Pallavaraiyaṉ Maṇḍaippiḷḷai, Vijayarāyaṉ, the headman of Kurugāḍi in
Tenkiḻā-nāḍu, a division of Nittavinōda-vaḷanāḍu (See No. 99 above).

edition.l: yāṇṭu 4- vatu irācarācavaḷanāṭṭup peṇṇai va[ṭaka]-

inscription.head:
No. 115.
(A.R. No. 115 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
PARĀNTAKA I—date lost.
inscription.p:
This is a mutilated fragment recording some provision made for the sacred
bath of the god [on Saṅkrānti days] in the temple of Tāntōṉṟippirāṉ at Vāḷaiyūr.

inscription.head:
No. 116.
(A.R. No. 116 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
KANNARADĒVA (RĀSHṬRAKŪṬA KṚISHṆA III)—24th YEAR (A.D. 962-63)
inscription.p:
This is made up of two separate pieces found engraved on different slabs
built into the right and left sides of the entrance. It records a gift of ten kaḻañju
of gold deposited with the Nagarattār of Vāḷaiyūr for burning a perpetual lamp
in the temple of Tāntōṉṟipperumāḷ by one Vaḍugaṉ Kāḷaiyaṉ Āchchaṉ.

l.ta: tāviḷakku oṉṟiṉukku vaitta poṉ patiṉ kaḻañciṉukku nicatam u-
ḻakkeṇṇai caṉtirātittaval niṟka erippitāka vaittāṉ vāḷai-
yūr nakarattārvaḻi vaṭu[ka]ṉ k[āḷai]yaṉ āccaṉ itu paṉ

inscription.head:
No. 117.
(A.R. No. 117 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This is also, like No. 116 above, made up of two fragmentary pieces found
separately engraved. It is incomplete. It seems to record some gift for burning
a perpetual lamp before the image of Vīdiviḍaṅgar (processional image ?) in the
temple of Tāntōṉṟippirāṉ at Vāḷaiyūr by one Aiyāṟṟaḍigaḷ, the headman of
Iraiyānkuḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 118.
(A.R. No. 118 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
KANNARADĒVA (RĀSHṬRAKŪṬA KṚISHṆA III)—23rd YEAR (A.D. 961-62).
inscription.p:
This is mutilated. It records a gift of a lamp to the temple of Tāntōṉṟip-
perumāḷ alias Vīdiviḍaṅgadēvar at Vāḷaiyūr on the northern bank of the Peṇṇai
by one Vēṇāḍuḍaiyār Nāṭṭaḍigaḷ.

inscription.head:
No. 119.
(A.R. No. 119 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
KŌPERUÑJIṄGADĒVA—13th YEAR.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions Vol. XII, No. 178.

inscription.head:
No. 120.
(A.R. No. 120 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME GŌPURA LEFT OF ENTRANCE.
MAHĀMAṆḌALĒŚVARA SADĀŚIVA-MAHĀRĀYA
—Śaka 1485, Pramōdūta (wrong).
inscription.p:
The inscription is damaged and incomplete. It seems to record a gift of
land to the temple of Śeṇbainātha by a certain Vīramarasayyaṉ.

edition.l: •••• timātam 27- tiyati caṇpai cempainātaṟku kiṟuṣṇamarā-

inscription.head:
No. 121.
(A.R. No. 121 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
PARĀNTAKA I—date lost.
inscription.p:
This is multilated. It records a gift of 50 kaḻañju of gold for burning
5 perpetual lamps in the temple of Tāntōṉṟiyāḻvār by the Śaṅ[karappāḍiyār ?] of
Vāḷaiyūr.

inscription.head:
No. 122-123.
(A.R. Nos. 122 and 123 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
KŌ-PERUÑJIṄGADĒVA—11th YEAR.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. XII, Nos. 164 and 165.

inscription.head:
No. 124.
(A.R. No. of 124 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
PARĀNTAKA I—33rd YEAR (A.D. 939-40).
inscription.p:
This is fragmentary. It seems to record a gift of money for a perpetual
lamp in the temple by one Śaḍaiyaṉ Eḻuvaṉ, who probably deposited the amount
with the Nagarattār of Vāḷaiyūr.

inscription.head:
No. 125.
(A.R. No. 125 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
KANNARADĒVA (RĀSHṬRAKŪṬA KṚISHṆA III)—28th YEAR (A.D. 964-65)
inscription.p:
This is mutilated. It seems to record a gift of gold for a perpetual lamp
in the temple of Tāntōṉṟippirāṉ at Vāḷaiyūr by the Nagarattār of the place.

inscription.head:
No. 126.
(A.R. No. 126 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
KANNARADĒVA (KṚISHṆA III)—date lost.
inscription.p:
This is also mutilated like the above. It records the gift of lamp a called
the Īḻaviḷakku and of gold for burning the light (with the interest thereon) before
god Vīdiviḍaṅgar during the service in the temple of Tāntōṉṟipperumāḷ at Vāḷaiyūr.
The donor was a resident of Tañjāvūr by name Vēṭṭarai Kaviriyār. The amount
is said to have been deposited with the Nagarattār of the place.

inscription.head:
No. 127.
(A.R. No. 127 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI.
JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON A STONE SET UP AT THE RIGHT SIDE OF
THE (MAIN) ENTRANCE.
ACHYUTADĒVA-MAHĀRĀYA—ŚAKA 1452 (wrong for 1454).
inscription.p:
The details of date are given as Nandana, Mithuna, śu. 10, Svāti, Wednesday
which correspond to A.D. 1532, June 12.

This records the remission of the taxes jōḍi and śūlavari on all the dēvadāna
lands on the banks of the Peṇṇai belonging to the temple of Tāntōṉṟi-Āḷuḍaiya-
nāyiṉār at Śambai alias Vīrarājēndraśōḻapuram in Vāṇagōppāḍi-nāḍu for meeting
the expenses of worship and repairs in the temple, on the representation made to
the king by Vaiyappa-Nāyakkar, during the administration of the region by
Bommu-Nāyakar of Vēlūr.

inscription.head:
No. 128.
(A.R. No. 128 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE EAST WALL OF THE FIRST PRĀKĀRA.
KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA—9th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 120 kāśu by Savarṇaṉ Kāśyapaṉ alias Tuṇaiyāḻvāṉ
Kaiyugirāḷi (a resident) of Peruṅgūr in Tirumuṉaippāḍi for burning a lamp with
the interest thereon during the services in the temple of Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍaruḷiya-
Nāyaṉār. The amount is said to have been deposited for this purpose with a
Śivabrāhmaṇa who had share in the right of worship in the temple.

l.ta:
yaṟṟu uṭaiyā rūrpākaṅkoṇṭaruḷiya nā[yaṉā*]r[k*]ku tirumuṉaippāṭi
peruṅkūr

edition.l:
cantrādityavaṟ celuttuvutāka vaitta sandhiviḷakku 1- kku policaiyāle
erippatāka i-

l.ta:
kkoṇṭu ināyanārkoyilil aṟupatu vaṭṭattu nāṉ uṭaiya nāḷe
talaicce-

inscription.head:
No. 129.
(A.R. No. 129 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PAR. RĀJARĀJA (II)—17th YEAR (A.D. 1162-63).
inscription.p:
The inscription is mutilated at the beginning and is also incomplete. Hence
the object of the record is not known. The details of the date are given as Dhanus,
ba. 9, Sunday, A[śvini] or A[ttam] which would correspond to A.D. 1162,
December 2. The nakshatra was Hasta on this date.

Mention is made of Rājanārāyaṇanallūr the eastern area of Iraiyānaraiyūr
alias Śōḻakēraḷa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Pāṉūr-kūṟṟam, a division of Malāḍu alias
Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu.

edition.l:
vanaccakravattikaḷ śrī rājarājadēvaṟku yāṇṭu 17- vatu [ dhanu nāyaṟ] ṟu
aparapa

inscription.head:
No. 130.
(A.R. No. 130 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. VĪRARĀJĒNDRADĒVA—7th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This might possibly be a record of Kulōttuṅga III. It registers a gift of
28 kāśu with the interest on which to burn a light during the services in the
temple, by one Vāśishṭaṉ Śrīrāghavaṉ Paramēśvaraṉ. The amount was left in
deposit with a Śivabrāhmaṇa of the temple, (with his right of worship as security
therefor ?).

l.ta: kku kuṭutta kācu irupatteṭṭum kaikkoṇṭu eri[kka*]kkaṭaven āne-

tillaimuvāyira nampiyen en kāṇiye talaice[y*]māṭāka
eri[kka*]kka-

edition.l: ṭavenṉānen itu cantrādittavaṟ paṉmāhēśva[ra*] rakṣai |||—

inscription.head:
No. 131.
(A.R. No. 131 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PAR. RĀJARĀJADĒVA (II)—18th YEAR (A.D. 1163)
inscription.p:
This is a mutilated and incomplete record stopping with the mention of
the regnal year and the name of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 132.
(A.R. No. 132 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA II-11th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 60 kāśu for burning a light during services in the temple
of Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍaruḷiya-Nāyanar at Iṟaiyānaraiyūr alias Śōḻakēraḷa-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam by a lady by name Māśaṟuśōdi Śāṉi wife of Āritaṉ Śaṅkaranārāyaṇaṉ
Śrīkrishṇa-Bhaṭṭaṉ. A Śivabrāhmaṇa of the temple was entrusted with the amount
for this purpose.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] tiripuvaṉacakkaravattikaḷ śrī kulottuṅkacoḻadēvaṟku yāṇṭu
11- vatu i-

cāṉi pakkal cantrādityavaṟcella [e*]rikkum viḷakku 1- kku sthānapati
śivabrāhmaṇaril ṛṣabha-

inscription.head:
No. 133.
(A.R. No. 133 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SAME PRĀKĀRA.
UḌAIYĀR ŚŌḺAKĒRAḶADĒVA—3rd YEAR.
inscription.p:
This registers a gift of 102 cows by Parāntakaṉ Yādavavīman alias Uttama-
śōḻa-Malāḍuḍaiyār of the Bhārgava-gōtra for the daily supply of milk and ghee,
and an endowment of land for the (other) requirements of the akkāraḍalai offering
to the god in the day time and rice offering during the ardhayāma service, in the
temple of Ūruḍaiya-Mahādēvar at Iṟaiyānaraiyūr, a brahmadēya in Pānūr-kūṟṟam
a division of Malāḍu alias Jananātha vaḷanāḍu. The land seems to have been
purchased before endowment, on behalf of the donor by the sabhā of the place
from a private individual by name Āritaṉ Tiruvaraṅgaṉ Kēśavaṉ of Nallaraiśūr.
The details of this transaction are lost as the record stops incomplete towards the
end. It may be observed here that the surname (or other name) of the village
Iṟiayānaraiyūr is not found in this inscription. It was evidently added in memory
of prince Śoḻakēraḷa later on.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] uṭaiyār coḻakeraḷadēvarkku yāṇṭu 3- vatu malāṭāna
jaṉanāta vaḷanāṭṭu pāṉūṟ kū[ṟ]-

l.ta:
kottirattu parāntakan iyātavavimanāṉa uttamacoḻa
malāṭuṭaiyār vaitta a-

kkāraṭalait tiruvamutukkup paḻavarici nānāḻiyum tūpparuppu nānāḻiyum pāl
kuṟuṇiyum caṟka-

rai patiṉpalamum neyyamutu uḻakkaraiyum aṭaikkāyamutu pākku nālum
ilaiyamutu pattum at-

tacāmattiruvamutukku arici nānāḻiyum porikkaṟiyamutum neyyamutu
āḻ[ā*]kkum aṭaikkāyamu[tu]

pākku nālum ilaiyamutu pattum āka ittaṉaiyilum ippālamutukkum
neyyamutukkum uṭaiyā[r]

ivvuttamacoḻa m(ā)lāṭuṭaiyār vaitta pacu nūṟṟiraṇṭu nūṟṟiraṇṭi[l]
nikki niṉṟa tiruvamu[tu]-

kaḷukku nimantañ ceyta pūmi ivvūr kiḻpiṭākai nallaraicūrkkuḷattukkiḻ
kaḻaniyuḷ taṉiyāka

inscription.head:
No. 134.
(A.R. No. 134 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJADĒVA—14th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 16 kāśu for burning a light during the evening service
in the temple of Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍaruḷiya-Nāyanār, by Maṅgalaṅkiḻāṉ Aimboḻil-Rāmaṉ
of Māṇḍaiyūr in Tirumuṉaippāḍi-nāḍu. This was deposited with a Śivabrāhmaṇa
of the temple.

edition.l: 14- vatu āṭi māsat[tu*] tirumuṉaippāṭi nāṭ
pakkal kuṭutta kācu 1[6]- kku immāsamutal tiṅ-

inscription.head:
No. 135.
(A.R. No. 135 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA I—13th YEAR (A.D. 1024-5).
inscription.p:
This is stated to have its introduction Tirumanni vaḷara etc. which is how-
ever not given. It registers an endowment of 30 kāśu to the temple of Ūruḍaiya-
Paramēśvara at Iṟaiyānaraiyūr a brahmadēya and dēvanāṭchi (dēvadāna ?) in
Pānūrkūṟṟam a division of Malāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu, made by an officer
of the king by name Kiḻavaṉ Chaturanār alias Vānavaṉ-Mūvēndavēḷār who is
referred to as a native of Vannikuḍi in Kīḻchchembai-nāḍu a division of Rājarāja-
Pāṉḍināḍu. The sabhā of the place who received the amount were to carry out
the renovation of the Aindiram (Vāhana) of the procession image of the god, the
two side roads and the smaller and bigger maṇḍapas with their walls lime and
colour washed once in every ten years with the accumulated interest on the amount
endowed, at 6 kāśu per year (i.e., at 20 per cent per annum).

inscription.head:
No. 136.
(A.R. No. 136 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
MĀR. ŚRĪBALADĒVA (ŚRĪVALLABHA)—5th YEAR.
inscription.p:
The details of date are given as Makara, ba. 9, Monday, Svāti corresponding
to A.D. 1312, January 3, if the date of accession was A.D. 1307. This records
an endowment of about 808 kuḻi of land after purchase by one Kāḷattinādar
son of Kachchiyarāya of Paruvūr, for burning two perpetual lamps in the temple
of Ūrbāgaṅkoṉḍaruḷiya-Nāyanār at Iṟaiyānaraiyūr alias Śōḻakēraḷa-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam.

yāṇṭu [5]- vatu makaranāyaṟṟu aparapakṣattu

inscription.head:
No. 137.
(A.R. No. 137 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PAR. RĀJARĀJADĒVA (II)—15th YEAR
inscription.p:
The details of date are given as Mīna, śu. 5, Monday, Mṛigaśīrsha which
yield the equivalent A.D. 1160, March 14 for his 14th year. The inscription
begins with the introduction Pūmaruviya Tirumādum etc. It records an endowment
of a few plots of land with wells, trees and channels at Vikkachchimaṅgalam
after purchasing the same from a private individual, by one Ēśanuḍaiyāṉ Uḍaiyāṉ
Śūranāyakaṉ alias Nānūṟṟuva-Pallavaraiyaṉ of Tiruttuṟaiyūr for meeting the
specified requirements of worship and offerings to god Ūrbāgaṅkoṉḍaruḷiya-Nāyānār
at Iraiyānaraiyūr on five occasions viz., during the sacred bath and procession
on the day of festival in the Paṅguṉi and Puraṭṭādi months and the sacred
bath on the day of Tiruvādirai in Mārgaḻi. Mention is made of a hall called
the Tāḻaṅkuṭṭa-tirtha-maṇḍapam where the deity was taken and offered worship.

l.ta: pū maruviya tirumātum puvimātuñ jayamātum nāmaruvi-
ppa varumuṟai urimaiyil maṇimuṭi cūṭit tiṅkaḷ veṇkuṭai-

inscription.head:
No. 138.
(A.R. No. 138 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. RĀJARĀJADĒVA—10th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 32 cows to the temple of Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍaruḷina-
Mahādēva at Iṟaiyānaraiyūr alias Śōḻakēraḷa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, by Muḍigoṉḍāṉ
Kāri alias Vīrapālaṉ a resident of Vāṇapirāṭṭi village in Vaḍanaraiyūr kūṟṟam
for the merit of a certain Tillai alias Āṟāyirappōyaṉ whom he had hit with an
arrow by accident and who died as a consequence.

edition.l:
vaṟku innāṭṭu vaṭannaraiyūṟkūṟṟattu vāṇapirāṭṭiyilirukkum [ cantra]

inscription.head:
No. 139.
(A.R. No. 139 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. RĀJĀDHIRĀJADĒVA (II)—10th YEAR (A.D. 1172-73).
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 15 kāśu each by Sōman Tōṉṟi and Sōman Uḍaiyān
residents of Naṟaiyūr in Tirumunaippāḍi-nāḍu for burning two lamps during the
evening service in the temple of Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍaruḷina-Nāyanār.

comaṉ uṭaiyānum iviruvom uṭaiyār ūr[pāka]ṅkoṇṭaruḷina

inscription.head:
No. 140.
(A.R. No. 140 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
PAR. TRIBH. RĀJARĀJADĒVA (II)—15th YEAR (A.D. 1160-61).
inscription.p:
The inscription is damaged in places. It begins with introduction Pūmaruviya
Tirumādum etc. Details of the date are given as Mēsha, śu. 8, Wednesday, Pushya
corresponding to A.D. 1161, April 5.

It seems to record an endowment both in money and in land by the Kiḷiyūr
Malaiyamān chief Periyuḍaiyāṉ Sūryadēvaṉ alias Chōḷēndraśiṅga-Malaiyakularāyaṉ
for providing for the various requirements (specified in detail) connected with the
worship and offerings in the temple of Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍaruḷiya-Nāyanār at Iṟaiyā-
naraiyūr on the days of festival in the months of Paṅguṉi and Puraṭṭādi and also
on other important occasions. Reference is made probably to a remission of the
kuḍimai and pāḍikāval taxes on the lands at the hamlet Puliyuṇṇipāḍi to the
north of Iṟaiyānaraiyūr.

ṭān kiḷiyūr malaiyamān periya uṭaiyān cūriyatevanāna coḷ[e]

inscription.head:
No. 141.
(A.R. No. 141 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME PRĀKĀRA.
RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA I—5th YEAR (A.D. 1016-17).
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the introduction Tirumanni vaḷara etc. It
records an endowment of lands to the temple of Ūruḍaiyār at Iṟaiyānaraiyūr for
the formation of a flower garden and for the maintenance of a gardener who
was to look after the same and to supply flower garlands daily, by Vāśishṭaṉ
Tiruvaraṅgaṉ Tiruvaraṅgaṉ a resident of the place. The donor is also stated
to have appointed the person for hereditary service in the temple in this connection.
The writing and wording of the record seem to be later than the period of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 142.
(A.R. No. 142 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KRISHṆADĒVA-MAHĀRĀYA—Śaka 1439.
inscription.p:
The date is given as Īśvara, Māgha, Paurṇami corresponding to A.D. 1518,
January 26. It was a Tuesday. The inscription is much damaged. The contents
are similar to those of Nos. 74 and 288 of 1903 and No. 125 of 1904 which
describe the emperor's conquests of the several regions and record his remission
of the jōḍi and śūlavari from villages specified amounting to ten thousand varāhas
in favour of the Śiva and Vishṇu temples in the Chōḷa country of which this is one.

edition.l: varṣamkaḷ 1439- melc cellāniṉṟa iśvarasamvat-
sir[ mai] yilum vijavāṭai mallikārjuṉadēva simaiyilum
pūjai punaskāramum [ aṅga] raṅga vaibhōgaṅkaḷum ti•••

inscription.head:
No. 143.
(A.R. No. 143 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE SAME PRĀKĀRA.
RĀJARĀJADĒVA I—24th YEAR (A.D. 1008-9).
inscription.p:
The characters of the inscription seem to be later than the period of the
king. This records an endowment of land at the hamlet Kuñjiramaṅgalam after
purchase by a certain Vāśishṭaṉ Tiruvirāmaṉ Tiruvaraṅgaṉ of Iṟaiyānaraiyūr to
the temple of Ūruḍaiya-Mahādēvar of the place to provide for offerings to god
Amarabuyaṅgar-dēvar (?) during the three services in the temple.

Portion after line 24 does not seem to belong to this record. It mentions
a grant of 120 kāśu to the temple by the chief Uttamaśōḻa-Milāḍu[ḍaiyār] for
offerings to the god. With this amount the temple authorities are stated to have
purchased a land inclusive of tax-payment.

l.ta: vāko•• ciṟutuṟai eṇṭicai pukaḻtaru īḻamaṇṭalamum tiṇṭiṟal ve-
ṉṟit taṇṭāṟ koṇṭa taṉ neḻil vaḷar ūḻiyuḷ ḷellāv yāṇṭun

toḻutakai viḷaṅku[m*] yāṇṭe ceḻiñarait tecukoḷ śrīkovirāja
rājake

edition.p:
The following piece though engraved in continuation of the above seems to be part of a
different record.

inscription.head:
No. 144.
(A.R. No. 144 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJADĒVA—19th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 15 kāśu deposited with a Śivabrāhmaṇa of the
temple of Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍaruḷiya-Nāyaṉār, for burning a lamp in the maṇḍapa on
the “tirumalai” during the evening service of the god, by Mūvāyirattoruvaṉ
Śōlaippirāṉ a merchant of Kōmar. The monthly interest on the amount is said
to be 3/8 kāśu i.e., at the rate of 2(1/2) per cent.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] rājarājadēvaṟkku yāṇṭu 11- vatu

l.ta: ṭṭan ikkoyilil mā••••• tanattu kāṇi tāṉe talaiycā-

inscription.head:
No. 145.
(A.R. No. 145 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KULŌTTUNGA-CHŌḶADĒVA—2nd YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records a similar gift of 15 kāśu for a twilight lamp in the temple by
a lady by name Kāraikkālammai wife of one Dēvargaṇḍaṉ Tiruvēṅgaḍam.

inscription.head:
No. 146.
(A.R. No. 146 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJADĒVA—19th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This also records a gift similar to that of No. 144 above, made by a
merchant of the place by name Avayampukkāṉ Nāyakkaṉ.

edition.l:
brāhmaṇan kauśikan vayiramekan karumāṇikkamāna paracamaiyakoḷari nampi
pakkal kuṭutta kācu 15-

pukkāṉ nā-

inscription.head:
No. 147.
(A.R. No. 147 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶADĒVA—2nd YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records still another gift similar to that of No. 146 above, made by a
dēvaraḍiyāḷ of the temple by name Duggai Nīrēṟṟāḷ alias Vikramaṉ Kulamāṇikkam.

l.ta:
- āvatu uṭaiyār ūrpākaṅkoṇṭaruḷiya nāyaṉār koyilil teva
raṭiyāril tukkai nireṟṟāḷā-

mutal tiṅkaḷ kāleyaraikkāṟ kācu palicai poliva
tā[ka ip]-

[va ṉāḷava]ntāṉāṉa kulottuṅkacoḻapaṭṭan tannuṭaiya kāṇi tāṉe
talaicce-

inscription.head:
No. 148.
(A.R. No. 148 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. RĀJĀDHIRĀJA (II)—10th YEAR (A.D. 1172-3)
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 32 cows for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple
of Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍaruḷiya-Nāyanār by one Kōchchāttau7 Kāmaṉ of Māḍūr in Pānūr-
kūṟṟam at the instance of the Nāṭṭavar, in expiation of the death of a neighbour's
girl caused by the donor's wife who threw a stick at her daughter (in anger). The
stick missed its aim and hit the girl who died as a consequence a few days later.

l.ta: p pāṉūrk kūṟṟattu māṭurillirukkum koccāttan kāman a-
kamuṭaiyāḷ [tan] makaḷai oru [arai] maṇaṅkoliṭṭ[e]ṟiya ivaḷait tap-
pi miṇṭan kāman makaḷmele paṭṭu ippeṇ irupatānāḷāl marittitu i-

tu kai piḻaippāṭena maṭam nāṭṭukkeṟi nāṭṭava[r*] ūrpākaṅ koṇ
ṭaruḷina nā-

yanār ko[yi*]lile oru tirununtā viḷakku vai yenṟu colli
ippaṭikku iraṇṭu

tiṟavaru micaintu tiṭṭiṭṭu ikkoccāttan kāman ivvānimātattile
muppattiraṇ-
ṭu pacup poṟittuk koṭuvantu viṭṭān ivviḷakku onṟum

inscription.head:
No. 149.
(A.R. No. 149 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJ. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA (II)—10th YEAR (A.D. 1142-43).
inscription.p:
The date may be read as the 12th also. The inscription records an endow-
ment of 3/4 vēli of land at Nallaraiśūr to the temple of Ūruḍaiya-Paramēśvarar
by Kūttan Vīmaṉ alias Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻā Mārāyaṉ a kaikkōḷa of the Uyyakkoṇḍāṉ-
terinda-Tirumañjanattār-vēḷam, to provide for the feeding of pilgrims visiting the
place, with the rice offered to the god during the midday service.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] irājakecaripatmarāṉa cakravattikaḷ śrī kulottuṅkadēvaṟku
yāṇṭu [10]- āva-

l.ta:
tu uyyakkoṇṭān terinta tirumaiñcaṉattār veḷattuk kaikkoḷaṉ
kūttan vimanāna

ṭukkuc caṭṭi oṉṟukkum vaicca nilam nallaraicūr erivā[y*]cceyyum
itan melaicceyyum u-

ḷḷiṭṭut taṭipala nila mukkāluṅ kaikkoṇṭu nima[n]tañ ceyyakkaṭavom
isttāna-

inscription.head:
No. 150.
(A.R. No. 150 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KULŌTTUNGA-CHŌḶA—date lost.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 16 kāśu for burning a lamp in the temple during the
two services (morning and evening) by one Sindaikkiṉiyāṉ for the merit of his
father Pugaḻaṉ Periyāṉ.

edition.l: ṭṭaṉpakkal kuṭutta kācu 16- ikkācu patiṉā[ṟum]•••

inscription.head:
No. 151.
(A.R. No. 151 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶADĒVA—2nd YEAR.
inscription.p:
This registers a similar gift of 16 kāśu (like the inscription above) for
burning a lamp during the evening service in the temple by Iḍaṅgoṇḍāṉ Avaiyam-
pukkāṉ for the merit of his father Śōṟan-Iḍaṅgoṇḍāṉ a merchant of the place.

edition.l: sandhi 1- kku erivutāka vaitta tiruviḷakku onṟu
poṟkoyilpaṭṭan pakkaṟ kuṭutta kācu 1[6]- kkum •

inscription.head:
No. 152.
(A.R. No. 152 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. RĀJĀDHIRĀJADĒVA (II)—7th YEAR (A.D. 1169-70).
inscription.p: This is an unfinished record stopping with the mention of the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 153.
(A.R. No. 153 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJ. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶADĒVA (II)—12th YEAR (A.D. 1144-45).
inscription.p:
This records a gift of land at Nallaraiśūr and 45 kalam of paddy (in place
of money ?) by Rājasundari a dēvaraḍiyāḷ of the temple of Tāntōṉṟi-Mahādēvar at
Vīrarājēndrapuram (Jambai) to provide for offerings to god Ūruḍaiya-Paramēśvara
during the early morning service.

dēvarkku yāṇṭu 12- vatu virarājēntrapurattut ti-

inscription.head:
No. 154.
(A.R. No. 154 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SECOND PRĀKĀRA.
KŌ-PERUÑJIṄGADĒVA—30th YEAR.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. XII., No.

inscription.head:
No. 155.
(A.R. No. 155 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
The writing seems to be of about the 13th century A.D. The inscription
declares two persons Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍa-Pērayaṉ and Nānūṟṟuva-Pērayaṉ and their
descendants as the rightful holders of the pāḍikāval (police) right of the temple
of Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍaruḻiya-Nāyanār and of the village Iṟaiyānaraiyūr alias Śōḻakēraḷa-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

l.ta:
caṭavaipaḷavaraiya vaṅcattil uḷḷār ūrpākaṅkoṇṭa perayaṉum
nānūṟṟuva peraiya-

inscription.head:
No. 156.
(A.R. No. 156 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is a curious inscription recording the declaration of a lady by name
Dēvapperumāḷ calling herself the vēḷaikkāṟi of (a chief) Rājarāja-Malaiyakularāyan
alias Nīrēṟṟaperumāḷ Edirigaḷnāyaṉ that she would not survive her master and
husband (Edirigaḷnāyaṉ) after his demise and an imprecation on herself and
others if she fails in her vow or is prevented by them from keeping up to it.
The writing seems to be of about the 12th century A.D.

l.ta:
veḷaikkāṟiyāṉa tevapperumāḷeṉ etirikaḷnāyaṉukkup piṉpu irunte
nākil eṉ māṟṟāṭṭimāṟku maṇṭaiyu miṭṭu i-

varkaḷukku niruṅkuṭuttu iva[r*]kaḷ kalaccoṟṟaiyum uṇkiṟeṉ eṉ
miṇāḷaṉ oṭu eṉṉaic cāvaveṇṭāmeṉpār eṉmi

ṇāḷaṉ eṟukiṟa kutiraikkup pullu iṭum kāḻican etirikaḷ nāyaka mutumaiyā
ṉukku taṉ miṇāṭṭiyaiyuṅ kuṭuppāṉ nā-

n cāvātiruntenākil eṉṉai neruppil kaṭṭi pukaṭutal kolluta[lai]
ceyyātavaṉ taṉ ṉoṭṭaiyāṟku tan miṇāṭṭiyaiyuṅ kuṭuttu eṉ

miṇāḷaṉ eṟukiṟa kutiraikku pulliṭukiṟa kāḻicanāṉa etirikaḷ nāyakamutumai
yāṉukku taṉ miṇāṭṭiyai kuṭuppāṉ eṉṉai kaṇṭāl kollātavaṉ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 157.
(A.R. No. 157 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJ. (?) KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA (III)—5th YEAR (A.D. 1182-83).
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the introduction Puyalvāyttu vaḷamperuga etc.
This registers the gift of certain taxes like kīḻiṟai, antarāyam, taṟiyiṟai, taṭṭārpāṭṭam
etc., due from the Śeṭṭis, Vāṇiyar and wage earners to the chief Arasanārāyaṇaṉ
Ēḻiśaimōgan alias Jananātha-Kachchiyarāyaṉ of Kūḍalūr, to the temple of Ūrbāgaṅ-
koṇḍaruḷiya-Nāyanār at Iṟaiyānaraiyūr to meet the expenses of the unguents
required for the daily anointment of the deity and of the tiruviśūḻ-śāttugai (?) once
every year.

l.ta:
puyalvāyt[tu] vaḷamperukap poyyāta nāṉmaṟaiyiṉ
ceyal vāyppat tirumakaḷum jayamakaḷum viṟṟiruppa veṇmatipol kuṭai viḷaṅka
velventa raṭipaṇiyac cakkara-

muñ ceṅkolun tikkaṉaittuṅ kāvalpūṇa kaṟpakālam puvikāppa poṟ
pamainta muṭipuṇa[r*]

ttu ulakuṭai mukkokkiḻānaṭika
ḷoṭum viṟṟiruntaruḷiya ko-

coḻakeraḷac caruppetimaṅkalattu uṭaiyā rūrpākaṅkoṇṭaruḷiya(ṉa)
nāyanār[k*]ku kūṭalūr aracanārāyaṇaṉa eḻicaimokaṉāna janaṉāta kacciyarāyaneṉ
iṉṉāyanār[k*]ku āṇṭutoṟum tiruvvi-
cū[ḻ] cāttukaikkum nittappaṭi tirumelpūccuc cāttukaikkum nāṉ viṭṭa

māvatu ivvūr kuṭi irukkum ceṭṭikaḷum vāṇiyarum paṇice[y*] makkaḷum
uḷḷiṭṭāriṟukkum kiḻiṟai anta-

rāyam taṟiyiṟai taṭṭārppāṭṭam epperpaṭṭaṉavum iṉnāyanār śrīpaṇ
ṭārattukku āṇṭutoṟum iṉanāyaṉār śrīkāriyañceyvār taṇṭi śrīpaṇṭārattile
oṭukki i

vaṟ cellakkaṭavatāka kalveṭṭi viṭṭeṉ kūṭalūr aracanārāyaṇaṉ
eḻicaimokanāna jaṉaṉātak kacciyarā[ya*]neṉ

inscription.head:
No. 158.
(A.R. No. 158 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. VIRARĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA (KULŌTTUṄGA III)—6th YEAR
(A.D. 1183-84).
inscription.p:
The details of date are given as Makara śu. 2, Monday, Aviṭṭam may
correspond to A.D. 1183, December 19, the tithi having ended at .66 on the
previous day.

This records a gift by Kiḷiyūr Malaiyamāṉ Sūriyaṉ Nīrēṟṟāṉ alias Rājarāja-
Malaiyakularāyaṉ the chief of Malāḍu, of the taxes kaḍamai and pāḍikāval due to
him in kind from Puttūr a village belonging to the sabhā of the place, to provide
for offerings and unguents to the deity in the temple of Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍaruḷiya-
Nāyanār at Iṟaiyānaraiyūr. Other taxes also like taṟiyiṟai, taṭṭārpāṭṭam, veṭṭi-
muṭṭaiyāḷ were made over to the temple and were to be collected by the sabhā on
behalf of the temple.

edition.l:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī vi[ra*] rājentiracoḻatevaṟk[ku] yāṇṭu
6- vatu makara nāyaṟṟu pūrvapakkuḻattu dvitiyaiyum tiṅkaṭkiḻamaiyum pe[ṟṟa] aviṭṭatti
nāḷ malāṭā-

muṭṭ[ai]-

yāḷum nāṉkoḷkiṟa eppeṟpaṭṭa tevaiyum ceyvārkaḷākavum
ippaṭi naṉceyyāl vanta kaṭamai nāṉ koḷkiṟapaṭiye śrīpaṇṭārattile ic

ṭamaiyāyuḷḷatu aḷantu taravukoḷḷakkaṭavārkaḷāka kalveṭṭivitteṉ
irājarājamalaiyakularāyaneṉ ittaṉmam aḻippāṉ taṅkaḷammaikkut tāne maṇāḷaṉ
villavaraiyaṉ cat

ti[ya*]m keṅkai iṭai kumari iṭai ceytāṉ ceyta pāvaṅkoḷvāṉ
itu paṉmākecura ilaṭkṣai . ivvūr nāṉkellaiyuḷppaṭṭa naṉceynilamuṟṟum
viṭṭatu ippaṭikkut tiru-

inscription.head:
No. 159.
(A.R. No. 159 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KŌ-PERUÑJIṄGADĒVA—30th YEAR.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions Vol. XII., No. 235.

inscription.head:
No. 160.
(A.R. No. 160 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE SAME PRĀKĀRA.
MĀR. VĪRA-PĀṆḌYADĒVA—7th YEAR.
inscription.p:
The details of the date given as Tulā, śu. 14, Friday, Aśvati correspond to
A.D. 1333, October 22, and would enable the identification of this king with the
one who ascended the throne in A.D. 1326-27. This records an endowment of
four pieces of land measuring 1250 kuḻi in all by Āṭkoṇḍadēvaṉ Toṇḍappiḷḷai a
merchant of Tirunāvalūr in Tirumuṉaippāḍi-nāḍu to provide for the various
requirements of worship in the temple of Ūrbagaṅkoṇḍaruḷiya-Nāyanār during the
four festival days of the year, at the rate of ten paṇam for each festival. The
rituals observed on such occasions are described; so also the articles required for
hem are named such as rose-water, musk, camphor, saffron and sandal paste.
The lands are said to have been purchased before gift by the donor from the
legal heirs of the owner who had run away unable to pay the taxes.

edition.l:
śrīmaṟkittimel komāṟapaṉmar tirupuvaṉaccakravatti śrīvirapāṇṭiya
tevaṟku yāṇṭu 7- vatu tu-

inscription.head:
No. 161.
(A.R. No. 161 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE EAST WALL OF THE THIRD PRĀKĀRA.
inscription.p:
Vijayarāya-Mahārāya “who witnessed the elephant hunt” (Dēvarāya II ?)
—Śaka 1368. The details of the date are given as Akshaya, Tulā, śu. 10, Friday,
Aviṭṭam corresponding to A.D. 1446, September 30.

This is a copy of No. 23 of 1905 from Kīḻūr. It states that as a result of
(the forcible) collection of kāṇikkai (presents) from the ryots of the Valaṅgai and
Iḍaṅgai classes by the officers of state on each change of regime or administra-
tion (manvantara) the former became impoverished and ran away from their
homes to other kingdoms consequently worship and festivals in temples ceased
and the land became infested with disease causing intense mental anguish to the
people. This reached the ears of the king who forthwith issued orders through
Aṇṇappa-Uḍaiyar prohibiting such extortion of the iṉavari and iḍaṅgai-vari in
future and to get his command recorded on stone (throughout the state). As it
was left out in some places people petitioned to Nāgarasa-Uḍaiyar to rectify this
omission. On receipt of his permission this is stated to have been engraved in this
temple also in the presence of the members of the nāḍu (assembly) of Vaḻudilam-
paṭṭu-chāvaḍi, the karaṇika, the parivāra and Toṇḍaimānār Kachchiyarāyar.

l.ta:
kku tappuvarāyar
kaṇṭaṉ muvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭanāṭu koṇṭu koṇṭanāṭu koṭātāṉ

tāpa vicaiya
rāya mahārāyar piṟutuvirācciyam paṇṇiaruḷāniṉṟa kṣaya

rattu tulānāyaṟṟu pūṟuvapakṣattu veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum tecamiyum
peṟṟa aviṭṭattu nāḷ vaḻutilampaṭṭu cāvaṭi nāṭum karaṇikkarum parivāramum
toṇṭaimāṉār kacci[ya*]rāyar kacci[ya*]rāyarum kūṭi[iruntu irāyar]

ku nākaraca uṭaiyar nirupappaṭi iṉavari iṭaṅkaivari
kaḻittu kalluveṭṭivittapaṭi [||*] irācciyattil valaṅkai iṭaṅkai inta iraṇṭu
vakaiyil kuṭikaḷaiyum piṟatāṉikaḷ maṉmantaraṅkaḷtoṟum kāṇikkai ko-

ṇṭu ittāle kuṭikaḷ uḷḷatu nalaṅki puṟairācciyame oṭippoy
ittāle tevatāṉaṅkaḷ pūcai puṉa

ram tirunāḷum kulaintu irācciyam viyātiyum
āy maṉittar uḷḷam cettu nalaṅkiṉapaṭiāle intacceyti irāyar tiruvuḷḷam
paṟṟi aruḷi

valaṅkai yiṭaṅkaiyil uṇṭuāṉa kuṭikaḷukkum kāṇikkai koḷḷakkaṭava
talla eṉṟu aṇṇappa uṭaiyaṟku irāyar irāyatam tiruvuḷḷampaṟṟi[aruḷiya]
aḷavil inta iṉavariyum iṭaṅkaivariyum kaḻittu aṇṇappa uṭaiyar cila iṭa-

āle intac ceyti nākaraca uṭaiyaṟku viṇṇappañceytapaṭiāle immatiyā
[tik]ku nākaraca uṭaiyar nirupam tarukaiyil iṉṉāḷmutalukku valaṅkai

iṭaṅkaiyil palakuṭikaḷukkum iṉavari iṭaṅkaivari eṉṟu koḷḷakkaṭava
tallavākavum itukku irācakarattukku uṭkuṭuttu kalaivu vicārittavaṉ civatturoki
paṭṭatum paṭṭu

keṅkaikkaraiyil kapilavatai paṇṇiṉa pāpattile pokakkaṭavarkaḷ
ākavum [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 162.
(A.R. No. 162 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF
THE SAME (THIRD) PRĀKĀRA.
KAMPAṆA-UḌAIYAR—PRAMĀDI, (KAMPA II)—A.D. 1373-74.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of five cows to the temple of Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍaruḷiya-
Nāyanār for the sacred pañchagavya bath of the god by a certain Kāḷattināthaṉ
a son of Kachchiyarāyar (or family ?) of Paruvūr.

Close to this is another inscription dated in the cyclic year Śubhakṛit
(A.D. 1362-63 ?) which records a gift of two pieces of land measuring together
550 kuḻi in extent, for a perpetual lamp in the temple, by one Tiruvēgamba-
muḍaiyān Tirumuḍikkāri of Śīdēvi village adjoining Śīkkāḍu by order of Goppaṇṇa-
Uḍaiyar.

l.ta: yar piḷḷaikaḷil kāḷattinātaṉ viṭṭa pacu iraṇ-
ṭum kaṉṟu tāyppacu muṉṟum ākappacu añ-

mākecurar rakṣai [||*]
(ā•ṟ• ṇo• 162-ā of 1906)
vāṇātarāyaṉ taṭi oṉṟu kuḻi 330- m piṟaiuṭaiyāṉ
taṭi oṉṟu kuḻi 220- m vaṭamelai mūlai kiṇaṟum viṭṭe[ṉ*]

inscription.head:
No. 163.
(A.R. No. 163 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE WALL OF THE SECOND GŌPURA,
RIGHT OF ENTRANCE.
PAR. RĀJARĀJADĒVA (II)—13th YEAR (A.D. 1158-59)
inscription.p:
This begins with the introduction Pūmaruviya Tirumādum etc. It registers
a grant of twenty vēli of land separated from out of four hamlets and grouped
together as one village under the name Rājarājamaṅgalam and converted into a
dēvadāna iṟaiyili from the original ūrkiḻ-iṟaiyili of the component parts, under
orders of the king at the request of the Kiḷiyūr Malaiyamān chief Periya-Uḍaiyāṉ
Nīrēṟṟāṉ alias Rājarāja-Malaiyakularāyaṉ, for the requirements of worship etc.,
in the temple of Ūrbagaṅkoṇḍaruḷiṉa-Mahādēva. The king is stated to have
been seated in the hall Pallavarāyaṉ-maṇḍapa in his palace at Āyirattaḷi while
making this grant. The chief Nīrēṟṟān figures later as donor (in No. 158 above)
in the reign of Kulōttunga III.

l.ta:
ṭait tikaikkaḷūṟeṭṭum taṅkutanikkūṭam tāneṉa viḷaṅkak karuṅkalippaṭṭi
yaic ceṅkol turappa poruṅkati rāḻippuvi vaḷa[r*]

variraṭṭar minavar ciṅkaṇar pallava[r*] nutaliyar pāttivar paṇiya e[ṇ*]
ṇaruṅ kaṟpa[ka*]m maṇṇakam puṇa[r*]ṉtu cempon vira

m viṟṟiruntaruḷiya kopparakecari patmarāṉa tribhuva[ṉa*]ccakkarava[r*]ttikaḷ
śrīrājarājadēvaṟku yāṇṭu 13- vatu ḷ 184- nāl āyirattaḷiyiṟ koyilil uḷḷā-

kiḷiyūr malaiyamāṉ periyauṭaiyāṉ nireṟṟāṉāṉa

vaṟku veṇṭum

yāṇṭu 13- vatumutal veli 1- kku i 80- ḷamāka iṟaikaṭṭiṉa[paṭi] yāṇṭu 110- vatu
mutal dēva-
