maikku ivai śrīkaraṇanāyakam paḻaiyanū
ruṭaiyā ṉeḻuttu ivai puravari śrīkaraṇanāyakam paṉaiyūruṭaiyā ṉ[eḻuttu] [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 164.
(A.R. No. 164 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJ. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA (I)—48th YEAR (A.D. 1117-18).
inscription.p:
The introduction begins with the words Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga. The inscrip-
tion registers a sale of (a quarter vēli and half mā of) wet land with a well
and two house sites, twenty kuḻi in extent, belonging to two ex-accountants in
the village Iṟaiyānaraiyūr by the sabhā of the place to the temple of Ūruḍaiya-
Paramēśvarar, for the requirements of worship to the goddess Tiruppaḷḷiyaṟai-
Nambirāṭṭiyār consecreted by Araiyaṉ Ponnambalakkūttan alias Toṉḍaimānār the
headman of Arumbākkam adjoining Maṇavil in Maṇaiyiṟ-kōṭṭam a division of
Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻamaṇḍalam. The cost of the lands amounted to 13 and odd kāśu
and 3 kaḻañju of gold of which the former was the cash endowment made by
Toṇḍaimānār at the time of consecration and the latter, that made by the
madhyastha of the village for the supply of ghee on the day of festival in Kārttigai
month every year. The necessity of the sabhā for the sale of land is said to be
its obligation to make up the amount of kaḍamai due to the king which had been
defalcated by the two accountants, whose property therefore was now confiscated
by the order of the king.

l.ta: laikeṭa villavar kulaitara eṉaimaṉnava ririyaluṟṟaḻitarat ti-

raṟku jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu maṇaiyiṟkoṭṭattu
maṇavil arumpākkiḻāṉ arai-

ruḷuvitta tirup
paḷḷiaṟai nampirāṭṭiyārkku nāṅkaḷ viṟṟukku-

ṭutta nilamāvatu ivvūr ūrkkaṇakkan iḷamai veṅkaṭamāṉa paṇṭitap
piriyaṉum iḷamai karumāṇikkamāṉa

ṉum ivviruvarum itaṉkiḻ yāṇṭu nāṟpattāṟāvatu ūrkkaṇakkeḻutik
kaṭamaippaṭṭuk kaṭamai pokkaṟāte kuṭi oṭi-

ppoṉamaiyil ikkaṭamai eṅkaḷukku iṟukkaveṇṭiṉamaiyil ik
kaṭamaikku celavāka ivvūr melpiṭākai cempiyaṉmā-

teviyil śrīvānavaṉmātevippererikiḻ naṉpār nirnilam uttamacoḻa
vatikku kiḻa

taṭi iraṇṭu nilam kālearaimāvum itil kiṇaṟum ivvū[r*] nattattu
ivarkaḷ kaṇakkakkāṇi maṉaiyil eṭuttuniṉ-

ṟa eṭuppum to[ṭṭa*]mum akappaṭa vitiviṭaṅkaṉ kolāl irupatu kuḻiyum
āka ittaṉaiyum viṟṟukkuṭukkaveṉṟu

yokam uṇṭāṉamaiyil innilamum ikkiṇaṟum immaṉaiyum viṟṟuk
kuṭuttukkoḷvatāna emmilicai

ruḷk kācu patiṉmuṉṟe nālumā mukkāṇiyum kā[cu] kallāl okkum
pon mukkaḻañcum āka ittaṉaiyum koṇṭ[o]m

tuvarukiṟa ney tūṇi nāṉāḻiyālk kaṭava poṉ mukkaḻañcum
ittoṇṭaimāṉār ippirāṭṭiyārkkut tiruvārātaṉai-

kum nilaṅkoḷḷa varakkāṭṭiṉa kācu patiṉmuṉṟenālu
māmukkāṇiyum āka ipponnukkum ikkācu
ṉnilamum ikkiṇaṟum immaṉaiyum viṟṟu vilaiyāvaṇañ ceytukuṭuttom

inscription.head:
No. 165.
(A.R. No. 165 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
VIKRAMA-CHŌḶA—17th YEAR (A.D. 1134-35).
inscription.p:
This is left incomplete towards the end. The inscription registers the uḷvari
(entry in Tax-register) for the lands endowed after purchase by one Taṟaiyuḍaiyāṉ
Vichchādiraṉ Sahāyaṉ alias Viñjirāyaṉ to provide for the requirements of worship
and offerings (specified in detail) on all important festival Sundays in the temple
of Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍadēvar at Iṟaiyānaraiyūr, on which occasions the image of the
goddess Tiruppaḷḷiyaṟai-Āḷuḍaiyāḷ was taken in procession round the temple to
the recital of Tiruchchāḻal hymns (of Māṇikyavāchaka).

l.ta:
koyilil tiruppaḷḷiaṟai āḷuṭaiyāḷ tirunāyaṟṟukkiḻamaitoṟum
puṟampe eḻuntaruḷit tiruccāḻal keṭṭaruḷi amutuce[y*]taruḷi koyillaic cuḻal
eḻuṉtaruḷi mele eḻuṉtaruḷukaikku aṟṟaināḷaikku veṇṭum aḻivukku uḷvari
tiru-

ruvamutu arici mukkuṟuṇikku nellu eḻukuṟuṇi nānāḻiyum kaṟiamutukku
nellu nānāḻiyum neyyamutu uḻakkukku nellu kuṟuṇiyum taiyiramutu iru
nāḻikku nellu nānāḻiyum miḷa(k)kuamutu oru piṭikku nellu muṉnāḻiyum uppamutu
urikku nellu irunā••••

yum tiruviḷakkeṇṇai nāḻikku nellut tūṇiyum āka nāḷ oṉṟukku
nelluk kalane mukkuṟuṇi āka ippaṭi

tittavaṟ cellakkaṭavatāka iṉnimaṉtam
celkaikku taṟaiuṭaiyāṉ viccātiraṉ cakāyanāna viñcirāyaṉ ivvūr pā••••

ivviruvar pakka(l)lum vilaikoṇṭu viṭṭa coḻakeraḷanallūrkkuk kiḻ
pā[ṟ*]kkellai ittevar

kum piṭākai ciṟu
pāttuellaikku me[ṟ*]kkum teṉpā[ṟ*]kke[l*]lai ūriniṉṟum kumaramaṅkala[t*]
teṟappona vaḻikku vaṭakkum melpā[ṟ*]k•••••••••

āka icaitta perunāṅke[llai]uḷ akappaṭṭa eriyum naṉceyum
puṉceyum ā[ka*] ittaṉaiyum it[t*]evar tirunāmattile vilaikoṇṭu ittanmam
tittavaṟ śrīkāriyañceyvār mutal iṭṭuk koṇṭu śrīpaṇṭārattile aḷa

inscription.head:
No. 166.
(A.R. No. 166 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME GŌPURA WALL, LEFT OF ENTRANCE.
PAR. RĀJARĀJADĒVA (II)—6th YEAR (A.D. 1151-52)
inscription.p:
The inscription has the introduction Pūmaruviya Tirumādum etc. It records
the gift of the taxes perumpāḍikāval, śiṟupāḍikāval, taṟiyiṟai, taṭṭārpāṭṭam, anta-
rāyam and taraviniyōgam due from Parundal alias Malaiyavichchādiranallūr and
other dēvadāna villages by the chief Āḷappiṟandāṉ Mōgaṉ alias Rājarāja-
kāḍavarāyaṉ of Kūḍalūr, who had the title Nāludikkum-veṉṟāṉ, to the temple of
Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍaruḷiya-Mahādēva for the requirements of worship on the festival
day of Tiruvōṇam in the month of Puraṭṭādi.

l.ta: riraṭṭar minavar ciṅkaṇar pa[l*]lavar nutaliyar pā[t*]tivar paṇi-

edition.l: ṟku yāṇṭu 6- vatu malāṭāna jaṉanātavaḷanāṭṭup pānūṟkūṟṟattu [bra]-

l.ta: yam taraviniyokaṅkaḷum epperpaṭṭa e[l]vait tevai ma-

edition.l: tityavat panmāhēśvara rakṣai |||—

l.ta:
[puravu]vari śrīkaraṇanāyakam iḷaṅkuṭaiyā neḻuttu [ivai puravari tiṇaikkaḷa
nāya]kam pāraṇainallūr kiḻavaneḻuttu ivai nikarilicoḻap pallava-

[rā]yaneḻuttu ivai puravuvari śrīkaraṇattu mukaveṭṭi nākaṅko[neḻuttu
i]vai puravuvari śrīkaraṇattu mukaveṭṭi muḻainakaruṭaiyā neḻut-

[tu] ivai puravuvari śrīkaraṇattu mukaveṭṭi koṟṟamaṅkalamuṭaiyā[ṉ
eḻuttu ivai] ilāṭattaraya ṉeḻuttu ivai rājarāja viḻupparaiya ṉeḻuttu

ivai toṇṭaimā ṉeḻuttu ivai vecālipperayaṉ e[ḻuttu]••••••
tirumantiraolai irā
eḻuttu ivai nuḷamparāya(ṉ)eṉ eḻuttu ittilāṭi caṅkara

inscription.head:
No. 167.
(A.R. No. 167 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
RĀJ. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA I—35th YEAR (A.D. 1104-05).
inscription.p:
The introduction Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga etc. is only briefly given in this record.
It registers the transfer (by order of the king) of the village Vāṅgal alias
Parundeṅgal comprising twenty vēli of wet and dry lands in Kiḻār-kūṟṟam under
a new name Malaiyavichchādiranallūr from (the jurisdiction of) Vāṇakōvaraiyar
as dēvadāna to the temple of Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍaruḷina-Mahādēva, with the tax (iṟai)
due to the temple fixed at 70 kalam of paddy on every vēli of wet land and
30 kalam on dry land.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa ṉilamakaḷ nilava malarmakaḷ
puṇara urimaiyiṟc ciṟa

maṇimuṭi cūṭi minavar nilaikeṭa villavarkulaitara eṉaimanṉava
riḻiyaluṟṟaḻitarat tikkaṉaittu

patmarāṉa tribhuvaṉacakravattikaḷ śrīkulottuṅkacoḻadēvaṟkku yāṇṭu 35- āvatu
nāḷ 145- nāl milāṭāṉa jaṉaṉātavaḷanāṭṭup pāṉūṟk kūṟṟattu iṟaivānarai
yūrā-

tevatāṉa iṟai(ṟa)yili iṭṭa iṉāṭṭu

veli 1-kku i 70 ḷa-mākavum punce[y*] veli 1-kku i 30 ḷa-mākavum yāṇṭu
35- [va*]tu mu-

ivai nākaṅ koṟṟaṉ eḻuttu ivai puravari tiṇaikkaḷa mukaveṭṭi piṭavūr veḷāṉ
eḻuttu ivai

inscription.head:
No. 168.
(A.R. No. 168 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
VIKRAMA-CHŌḶA—4th YEAR (A.D. 1121-22).
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with introduction Pūmādu puṇara etc. It states that
while the king was seated on his throne Śēdirājaṉ in the hall called Rājēndra-
śōḻaṉ in the inner courtyard of his palace at Muḍigoṇḍaśōḻapuram, (the chief or
officer) Vāṇakōvaraiyar represented to him that the māḍai equivalent of paddy
from the tax-free dēvadāna at Parundal was not specified in the earlier document
(recorded in No. 167 above). It was therefore now ordered by the king that the
defect be rectified, as a result of which the māḍai was settled to be paid at 169
and odd to the temple (every year) with the extent and boundaries of the land
clearly defined.

l.ta:
pūmātu puṇara puvimātu vaḷara nāmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa
taṉ nirupatumalar maṉnavar cūṭa maṉniya urimaiyāl maṇimuṭi cūṭic ceṅkolceṉṟu
ticaitoṟu maḷippa veṅkalinikki meyyaṟa

ḻaippa kaliṅka[m*] iriya kaṭalmalai
naṭātti valaṅkoḷāḻi varaiyūḻi naṭātti irucuṭaraḷavum orukuṭai niḻaṟṟa mukkok
kiḻānaṭikaḷoṭu-

edition.l:
m viṟṟiruntaruḷiya kopparakecaripatmarāṉa tribhuvaṉa cakravattikaḷ śrīvik
kiramacoḻadēvaṟkku yāṇṭu 4- āvatu nāḷ 330- nāl muṭikoṇṭa coḻapurattuk
koyiliṉ uḷḷālai āṭṭattu veḷimelai maṇṭapam rājēntracoḻanil cetirājanil
eḻuntaruḷiiruntu ceyyat tiruvā-

iṟaikaṭṭinapaṭi tavirttu innilam uṭppaṭa ivvūr māṭai 169va - m tavirttu
malai-

tūr ellai

kum meṟ[k]kum teṉpāṟkkellai meṟppaṭiyūrellaik

ṇṭum nimantaṅkaḷukku iṟuppatāka yāṇṭu 4- āvatumutal antarāyam
uṭppaṭa tevatāna iṟaiyili iṭṭukkuṭuppa[tu] ippaṭi tiruvāy moḻintaruḷina
maikku ivai paravaināṭṭu veḷān eḻuttu yāṇṭu 4 ḷ 342- nāl tevatāna

ncey nilam 10 veliyum āka nilam 20 velikku nirnilam veli 1- kku i

pāṭṭamum uṭppaṭa dēvatāna iṟai-

yili iṭṭa paruntalāṉa malaiyaviccātirinallūr māṭai 160-m yāṇṭu

kaḷattu mukaveṭṭi kuḷattuḻāṉ eḻuttu i-

inscription.head:
No. 169.
(A.R. No. 169 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
VIKRAMA-CHŌḶA—13th YEAR (A.D. 1130-31).
inscription.p:
The introduction is the same as the one given in No. 168 above. The
inscription registers a written understanding (uḷvari ?) agreed to by the Śiva-
brāhmaṇas of the temple fixing the quantum of allowances to be received by them
and of the items of offerings they had to provide during the daily early morning
service to gods Vighnēśvara, Naṭarāja, Pirāṭṭiyār (goddess) and Kshētrapāladēva
in the temple of Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍa-Dēvar.

l.ta:
pūmātu puṇarap puvimātu vaḷara nāmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa
tantiruppatu malar maṉṉavar cūṭa maṉṉiya urimaiyil

maṇimuṭi cūṭi ceṅkol ticaitoṟum vaḷarppa veṅkali nikki meyyaṟan
taḻaippak kaliṅka miriyak kaṭarmalai naṭātti valaṅkoḷāḻi varaiyāḻi naṭātti

yam iṟaiyānaraiyūrāṉa śrīco[ḻa*]keraḷac catuppetimaṅkalattu
ūr[p]ākaṅ koṇṭaruḷiṉa

rom it
tevar koyiliṟ piṭilikai vāriyāl nāṅkaḷ kaikkoḷḷakkaṭavaṉattukku uḷvari
araikkāluṭpaṭṭa

poṉnum tiramam oṉṟum tūṇi uṭppaṭa ariciyum nel irutūṇip
patakkum ney eṇṇai nānāḻi uṭpaṭṭatum aṭaikkāyamutu pā

miḷakum uppum neyyamutu tayiramutum pākku••••irupatum āka ittaṉaiyum

inscription.head:
No. 170.
(A.R. No. 170 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
PAR. RĀJARĀJADĒVA (II)—6th YEAR (A.D. 1151-52)
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with introduction Pūmaruviya Tirumādum etc. This
records the construction of the main hall (tirumāḷigai) in the temple of Ūrbāgaṅ-
koṇḍaruḷiya-Mahādēvar by (the chief) Āḷappiṟandāṉ Mōgaṉ alias Rājarāja-
kāḍavarāyaṉ Nāludikkum-veṉṟāṉ of Kūḍal.

l.ta:
pūmaruviya tirumātum puvimātum jayamātum nāmaruviya kalaimātum
pukaḻmātum naya

ḻaippa varumuṟai urimai maṇi
muṭi cūṭit tiṅkaḷ veṇkuṭait ticaikkaḷuṟeṭṭu

viḷaṅkak karuṅkalip paṭṭiyaic ceṅkol turappa poruṅkatirāḻi
puvi vaḷar[n*]tuṭanvara villavariraṭṭar minavar ciṅkaṇar pallavar nutaliyar pāttivar
paṇiya eṇṇaruṅ kaṟpakamaṇṇakam puṇar[n*]tu cempoṉ vira
vaṟ[ku*] yāṇṭu āṟāvatu milāṭāṉa jananātavaḷanāṭṭu pānūṟkūṟṟattu
var koyilil tirumāḷikai ceyvittāṉ kūṭal āḷappiṟa
k kāṭavarāyaṉ nālutikkum veṉṟāṉ itu pa

inscription.head:
No. 171.
(A.R. No. 171 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE (SECOND GŌPURA)
TRI. VIKRAMA-CHŌḶADĒVA—12th YEAR.
inscription.p:
The king meant is evidently Vikrama-Chōḷadēva. The inscription records
a gift of money (one kāśu and 3 mā) for burning a twilight lamp (with the
interest thereon) before god Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍaruḷiya-Dēvar by a certain Tiruvālak-
kōyil Tēṟṟi alias Tiruchchiṟṟambala-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ of Āvūr-nāḍu in Muḍigoṇḍa-
śōḻa[-vaḷanāḍu*].

l.ta:
kku
vaitta viḷakkoṉṟukkuk koṇṭa kācu oṉṟe muṉṟumāvukkum

vaṟ erikkakkaṭavatākak kaikkoṇṭom ik
koyil civa

edition.l: tampimārum itu panmāhēśvara rakṣai |||—

inscription.head:
No. 172.
(A.R. No. 172 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
TRIBH. VĪRARĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA—6th YEAR.
inscription.p: The details of date are given as Makara, śu. 2, Monday.

The inscription is mutilated. It seems to record a gift of money for
offerings and worship to the deity by Sūriyaṉ Nīrēṟṟāṉ alias Rājarāja-Malaiyakula-
rāyaṉ the same donor mentioned in No. 158 above, the Malaiyamān chief of
Kiḷiyūr.

l.ta:
•••••cūriyaṉ nireṟṟāṉāna rājarāja malaiyakularāyaneṉ iṉnāyanāṟku
amutupaṭikkum tirumeṟpūccukkum nāyanāṟku nāṉ nirvār[t*]tu kuṭutta•••••••••

•••••ivvūr paṇice[y*] makka[ḷu]m uḻavukuṭimakkaḷ koyilile veṭṭi
muṭṭaiyāḷum nāṉ koḷkiṟa epperppa[ṭ*]ṭa tevaiyum cevārkaḷ

inscription.head:
No. 173.
(A.R. No. 173 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE WALL OF THE OUTER GŌPURA,
RIGHT OF ENTRANCE.
ŚRĪRAṄGADĒVA-MAHĀRĀJA I—ŚAKA 1504.
inscription.p:
The details of the date are Chitrabhānu, Kārttigai 13, Monday, dvādaśi,
corresponding to A.D. 1582, November 12.

The epigraph is badly damaged in the latter portion. It seems to record
the renovation of certain parts of the temple like tank and the gōpura, and to
make provision for the renewal of worship in the two shrines (of the god and
goddess) by order of Veṅkappar Lingarājayyaṉ probably an officer of the king.

l.ta: micurakaṇṭaṉ kaṭṭāri cāḷu[va] tulukkā taṉavipāṭaṉ emma-
ṭai kaṇṭaruḷiya śrī ciraṅkatevamakā irāyar piṟutuvirācciyam
yum peṟṟanāḷ veṅkappar liṅkarācaiya[ṉ] kaṭṭaḷai iṭukaiyil

inscription.head:
No. 174.
(A.R. No. 174 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME GŌPURA, LEFT OF ENTRANCE.
ACHYUTADĒVA-MAHĀRĀJA—ŚAKA 1457.
inscription.p:
The details of date are Manmatha, Mithuna, śu. 3, Thursday, Maghā
(A.D. 1535; June 3, the nakshatra was however Punarvasu).

This records an endowment of the village Śīyamaṅgalam for worship and
other requirements to god Bhōgīśuramuḍaiya-Tambirānār in the temple of
Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍaruḷiya-Nāyanar at Iṟaivānaraiyūr by Bhōgaiyadēva-Mahārāja under
the orders of Salakayya Tirumalaiyadēva-Mahārāja for the merit of the king.
Bhōgaiyadēva is given the titles Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara, Chōḷakula-tilaka and Uṟaiyūr-
puravarādhīśvara. Salakam Tirumala is known to have been a brother-in-law of
the king and his chief general.

edition.l: ciyampaṇṇi aruḷāniṉṟa śakābdam 1457- yita[ṉ*] melccellāniṉṟa

l.ta:
ṭaiyatampirānāṟku tiruppaṇi mahāpūcai aṅkaraṅka vaipokaṅkaḷukkum
muṉ

piṭākaippaṟṟā[na] ciyamaṅkalam kiṟāmam oṉṟu inta kiṟāmam oṉ[ṟu]m
naṉcey puṉcey nāṟ-

ṇṇikkuṭuttom inta nayiṉār ūrpākamkoṇṭaruḷiya nayināṟkum
uṭaiyār

pokicuramuṭaiya nayināṟkkum inta kiṟāmam [ci]yamaṅkalam tevatānammākac
cantirātitta-

varaiyum naṭakkakaṭa[va]tākavum inta taṉmattukku yātoruvar ahitam
paṇṇinavarkaḷ

ivai cavuntarapāṇṭiya malaiya••••• ṉayiṉār eḻuttu ivai koṅka
[rā]yar cempuṉātar eḻu

inscription.head:
No. 175.
(A.R. No. 175 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
ACHCHINDA (ACHYUTA)DĒVA-MAHĀRĀYA—ŚAKA 1453.
inscription.p:
The date is given as Khara, Dhanus, śu. 2, Sunday, Uttirāḍam (A.D. 1531,
Decr. 10).

This records the grant of certain taxes by the Nāṭṭavar of the Twelve-
Paṟṟus of Iṟaivānaraiyūr except that on the sale of horses (kudirai-kāṇikkai ?)
amounting to 36 poṉ and 5(1/4) paṇam, from Kumiḻi in Paḻaṅgūr-paṟṟu, for the
requirements of worship in the temple of Narasiṅgamuḍaiya-Nayinār at Tiruk-
kōvalūr. Reference is made to the consecration of god Narasiṅga-Munaiyadaraiya-
Perumāḷ in the temple. The gift is said to have been made by Kachchirāyar on
behalf of the Nāṭṭavar in the presence of Bhōgaiyadēva-Mahārāja.

l.ta: ṉāṭu koṇṭu koṇṭa nāṭu koṭātāṉ metiṉimicurakaṇṭa kaṭṭāri
cāḷuvaṉ tulukkar oṭṭiyartaḷavipāṭaṉ e[m*]maṇṭalamum koṇṭa rā-
paccima uttara catucamuttiṟātipati kecaveṭṭai kaṇṭaruḷiya śrī accinta-
naraciṅkamuṭaiya nayiṉār koyil caṇṭecura nayiṉār śrīpaṇṭārattukku
ppaṇi naṭattaveṇṭi naraciṅka muṉaiyataraiyap perumāḷ nayiṉāraiyu-

m eḻuntaruḷap paṇṇikkoṇṭu tāṉattārumpar nilamumāka iṟaivā
narai-

yūrile varukaiyil śrīmaṉmaṇṭalecuraṉ timma(ppa ?)yarācāviṉ [vi]ra
puttiraṉ po-
rum nāṭṭavarkaḷum inta rekaipoṉ muppattāṟum paṇam añcekā-
lum paṉṉiraṇṭupaṟṟilum nāṅkaḷ iṟuppatāka patiñcuko[ṇṭu] e-
ṅkaḷ nayiṉār naraciṅkamuṭaiya nayiṉāṟku caṟuvamāṉṉiyamāka tiruppu ka••
ttile pokakkaṭavanākavum taṉ mātā pitāvukku iraṇṭu niṉaitta pāvat-

tile pokakkaṭavanākavum ivai kaccirāyaṉ eḻuttu ivai cavuntara
pāṇṭiya [maṉaimu]-

ṉṉāyan eḻuttu kāṭṭu mayiluṭaiyā[ṉ*] eḻuttu ivai koṅkarāyar
eḻuttu viraca-

mpavāṇṭāyar eḻuttu ivai tirumuṭikkāriyār eḻuttu kurukulat
taraiyar e-

inscription.head:
No. 176.
(A.R. No. 176 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE FLOOR AT THE
ENTRANCE.
RĀJARĀJA I—7th YEAR (A.D. 991-92)
inscription.p:
The inscription is left incomplete. It seems to record the merger of two
villages Śembiyanmādēvi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam and Araṇkalakki-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam founded by the Milāḍu chiefs Irāmadēvan Paṭṭālakaṉ alias Vikramaśōḻa-
Malāḍuḍaiyāṉ and Siddovaḍavaṉ-Gāmuṇḍaṉ (?) alias Uttamaśōḻa-Malāḍuḍaiyāṉ
respectively in the 15th year of Uttama-Chōḷadēva, and probably their gift to the
temple in this year. Mention is made of a princess (?) by name Pūdi Parāntaka-
dēviyār.

inscription.head:
No. 177.
(A.R. No. 177 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
ON A ROCK NEAR THE (ANK CALLED) ŌṬṬAIKKUḶAM.
VIKRAMA-CHŌḶADĒVA—9th YEAR.
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the introduction Pūmādu puṇara etc. This
records the remission of half of the perumpāḍikāval and śiṟupāḍikāval taxes
payable to the chief Sūṟṟiyaṉ Irāmaṉ alias Rājendraśōḻa-Malaiyakularājaṉ, the
Malaiyamāṉ of Kiḷiyūr from the dēvadāna lands belonging to the temples of
Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍadēvar, Badari-Āḻvār, Svarggāḻvār, Rājanārayaṇa-Viṇṇagar-Āḻvār,
Vāṉavaṉmādēvi-Viṇṇagar-Āḻvār, Durggaiyār and Piḍāri at Iṟaiyānaraiyūr.

l.ta: pūmātu puṇarap puvimātu vaḷara nāmātu vi-
ḷaṅka jayamātu virumpat tan tirupāta malar mannavar
cūṭa manniya urimaiyil maṇimuṭicūṭi ceṅkol ce-
nṟu ticaitoṟum vaḷarppa veṅkali nikki me[y*]yaṟa[n]
taḻaippa kaliṅka miriyak [kaṭalmalai*] kaṭātti valaṅkoḷāḻi varai-
ūḻi niṟutti irucuṭaraḷavum orukuṭai niḻaṟkiḻc cem-

inscription.head:
No. 178.
(A.R. No. 178 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR.
ON THE SAME ROCK (NEAR OṬṬAIKUḶAM)
VĪRARĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA—6th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records the remission of all the taxes except kuḍimai due from the
tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam lands of five of the temples mentioned in No. 177 above by
Sūryadēvaṉ Nīrēṟṟāṉ alias Rājarāja-Malaiyakularāyaṉ, the Malaiyamāṉ chief of
Kiḷiyūr, for meeting the expenses of worship and offerings to the deities therein.

edition.l: tevaṟku yāṇṭu 6- vatu kiḷiyūr malaiyamāṉ cūriyatevaṉ [nir]-
ttāḻvāṟkum durggaiyāṟkum idēvaṟku 6- vatu nāḷ iṟaiyiliyāka ka-

yāṭṭaṅkaḷāṉa nilaṅ[ka]ḷ naṉcey puṉcey uḷḷiṭṭa eppe[ṟ]-

ttukku aḻivu ceyvār taṅkaḷ ammaikkut tāne miṇāḷaṉ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 179.
(A.R. No. 179 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR
ON THE SAME ROCK.
inscription.p:
The inscription seems to be engraved in characters of about the 10th-11th
century A.D. It records an endowment of a hundred kāśu raised by donation
by Śaṅkaraṉ Nīlakaṇṭaṉ a native of Śēndanūr in the Pāṇḍya country, for feeding
some devotees in the temple (at Iṟaiyānaraiyūr ?) on the day of Jyēshṭhā in
Dhanus month every year.

l.ta: ṭṭu centa[nū]r piccaipukku niṉṟotuvārkku mutal kāttu po-
likoṇṭu[ṇ*]ṇa nūṟu kācu aṭṭiṉāṉ itu[ṇ*]ṇum kaccamā-
yil me[y*]kkāṭṭi uṇpatu allatum kaccattu pa-

inscription.head:
No. 180.
(A.R. No. 180 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
PARAKĒSARIVARMAN—29th YEAR.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions Vol. XIX, No. 434.

inscription.head:
No. 181.
(A.R. No. 181 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. RĀJARĀJADĒVA (II ?)—3rd YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records the remission of the pāḍikāval taxes due to him from the
wet and dry lands (of the village) in favour of the temple of Tiru-Āṟṟuttaḷi-
Āluḍaiyār at Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Muḍiyūr-nāḍu of Tiru-
muṉaippāḍi a sub-division of Rajarāja-vaḷanāḍu, by Mōhan Āḷappiṟandāṉ alias
Anapāya-kāḍavarāyaṉ who had the pāḍikāval (police guard) right over the nāḍu.
The title of the chief is after the surname of king Kulōttuṅga II (Anapāya).

l.ta:
vaḷanāṭṭu tirumuṉaippāṭi muṭiyūr nāṭṭu śrī kulottuṅkacoḻa
catu••••

kalattu uṭaiyār tiru āṟṟuttaḷi āḷuṭaiyārkku innāṭu pāṭi
kāvaluṭaiya m[o]kan āḷappiṟantāṉāna aṉapāyakkāṭavarāyanen tiruyāṟṟuttaḷi
āḷuṭaiyār••••

koḷvār vallava[ raiyar sattiyam] [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 182.
(A.R. No. 182 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARĀNTAKA I—29th YEAR (A.D. 935-36).
inscription.p:
This records some provision made for burning a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Mūlasthāṉattu-Mahādēva of Śrī-Āṟṟuttaḷi at Tirumuḍiyūr by one Kāri
Piramaṉ (a servant) of the household of prince Rājādittadēvar.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] matiraikoṇṭa kopparake[ca*]ripaṉma[r*]kku yāṇṭu
29- ā

inscription.head:
No. 183.
(A.R. No. 183 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARĀNTAKA I—29th YEAR (A.D. 935-36).
inscription.p:
The inscription is built in at the end. It records a gift of gold for burn-
ing a perpetual lamp in the temple of Mūlasthāṉattu Mahādēva of Śrī Āṟṟuttaḷi
with the interest thereon, by a private individual whose name is not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 184.
(A.R. No. 184 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
PARAKĒSARIVARMAN who took Madurai and Īḻam (PARĀNTAKA I)—41st YEAR
(A.D. 947-48).
inscription.p:
This records a gift of sheep for burning a lamp in the temple of Śrī
Āṟṟuttaḷi-[Mahādēva] at Tirumuḍiyūr by Mādēvaṉ Vīśamēḻi, a resident of
Kūṟṟamaṅgalam in Maṅgala-nāḍu.

edition.l:
matiraiyu miḻamuṅkoṇṭa kopparakecari pa[ṉ][mark*]ki yāṇṭu 41- vatu
maṅkalanā

inscription.head:
No. 185.
(A.R. No. 185 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARĀNTAKA I—35th YEAR (A.D. 941-42)
inscription.p:
This records a tax-free gift of land made after purchase from the Ūrār of
Poyyaraiśūr in Kīḻāṉmūr-nāḍu for burning two lamps in the temple of Mūlas-
thāṉattu-Perumāṉaḍigaḷ of Śrīyāṟṟuttaḷi at Tirumuḍiyūr by a certain Kōvaḍi-
uḍaiyāṉ of Brahmapurinallūr in Maḻa-nāḍu.

l.ta: ḻanāṭṭup piramapurinallūrk kaṇṭattup piramapurinallūr kovaṭi uṭaiyā-
ṉ koil cantirātittava[ṟ v]aiytta nantāviḷakku iraṇṭukku vilai-

inscription.head:
No. 186.
(A.R. No. 186 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARĀNTAKA I—32nd YEAR (A.D. 938-39).
inscription.p:
This is damaged in part. It seems to register the gift of a village tax-free
(probably with the income specified in paddy and gold) by prince Rājādityadēva
for the expenses of worship in the main shrine of the temple of Śrī-Āṟṟuttaḷi at
Muḍiyūr.

edition.l: muṭiyūr śrī āṟṟuttaḷi śrīmūla[ stāṉa]-

inscription.head:
No. 187.
(A.R. No. 187 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARĀNTAKA I—31st YEAR (A.D. 937-38).
inscription.p:
This is an incomplete inscription recording a gift of utensils required in
connection with the worship in the temple of Śrī-Āṟṟuttaḷi-Perumāṉaḍigaḷ prob-
ably by some army men of prince Rājāditya.

edition.l: ṟku yāṇṭu 31- ā-
l taḷikai 1 niṟai 122- m

inscription.head:
No. 188.
(A.R. No. 188 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA—16th YEAR.
inscription.p:
The inscription is built in at the end of lines. This records a gift of
“three fourth” of a perpetual lamp to the temple of [Śrī-Ā]ṟṟuttaḷi-Mahādēva
at Parāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam (Grāmam) by one Periyāṉ Kaṇavadi of Sāḷūr in
Maṅgala-nāḍu of Vāṇagappāḍi a division of Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu in expiation
of his having shot by mistake (while hunting) a resident of Ēnādimaṅgalam
(name not clear).

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] ci kolottuṅka coḻatevar[k*]ku yāṇṭu 16- vatu
rājēntracoḻavaḷanā•••••••••

maṅkalattuk kaṭappa•••••••••
n āmāttūrai veṭṭaiyil kāṇākkol paṭṭamaiyil śrī parā

inscription.head:
No. 189.
(A.R. No. 189 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PAR. RĀJĒNDRADĒVA—10th YEAR (A.D. 1061-62).
inscription.p:
This records a gift of “half” a perpetual lamp to the temple of
Śrīyāṟṟuttaḷi-Mahādēva at Parāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Muḍiyūr-nāḍu of
Tirumuṉaippāḍi-nāḍu, a division of Rājendraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu by Peṟṟān (Periyāṉ ?)
Uttaman, a farmer of the village for the merit of Peṟṟāṉ Sōmaṉ (probably his
brother).

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] kopparakecaripanmarāṉa uṭaiyār śrīrājēntradēvar[k*]ku
yāṇṭu [10]- vatu rājēntracoḻavaḷanāṭṭu tirumuṉaippāṭināṭṭu muṭiyūrnāṭṭu

kuṭippaḷḷi peṟṟāṉ [c]omaṉukku kuṭippaḷḷi peṟṟāṉ uttamaṉ cantirātittavaṟ
periyāṉ vaitta nantāviḷakku arai-

inscription.head:
No. 190.
(A.R. No. 190 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE
PAR. RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶADĒVA—16th YEAR (A.D. 1027-28)
inscription.p:
The inscription stops incomplete with the mention of the 16th regnal year
of the king after giving at length his usual praśasti beginning with the words
Tirumaṉṉi vaḷara etc. Parts of the inscription are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 190A.
(A.R. No. 190-A of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶADĒVA—10th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This is incised immediately below No. 190 and gives no introduction to the
king. This records a gift of 72 sheep for burning “three fourth” of a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Śrī Āṟṟuttaḷi-Mahādēva by Śelvaṉ Paḷḷikoṇḍāṉ alias
Rājarāja-Periyaraiyaṉ a kuḍippaḷḷi (farmer) of Śevalaimēḍu in Kāliyūr-kōṭṭam a
division of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa maṇḍalam to atone for the death of Mādāni Kuḷattūraṉ
of the village, caused by him [unconsciously ?].

l.ta:

inscription.head:
No. 191.
(A.R. No. 191 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PAR. RĀJĒNDRADĒVA—4th YEAR
(A.D. 1055-56).
inscription.p:
This begins with the introduction Tirumagaḷ maruviya etc. It is mutilated
and stops with the mention of the temple. After the space of two lines however
which is left blank, are three lines of writing which records a sale of some land
as tax-free dēvadāna to the temple by the Ūrār of Kuśappāḍi.

edition.l: ṉaiellām porutu [ puṟattu] nikaḻ-

inscription.head:
No. 192.
(A.R. No. 192 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARĀNTAKA I—39th YEAR (A.D. 945-46)
inscription.p:
This is left unfinished after the mention of prince Rājādittadēvar and the
temple of Śrīyāṟṟuttaḷi-Perumāḷ.

inscription.head:
No. 193.
(A.R. No. 193 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJ. RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶADĒVA (KULŌTTUṄGA I)—4th YEAR.
inscription.p:
The inscription is damaged. It registers the sale of half a vēli of land
tax-free for twenty kāśu by the assembly of Parāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in
Muḍiyūr-nāḍu to a certain Paṭṭan Pūvan (?) whom they helped to settle in
their village and also gave him the surname Parāntaka-Pēraiyaṉ after the name
of the village. At the bottom of the record is a fragment of the praśasti of king
Rājēndradēva.

l.ta: raya[n pa]kkal nāṅkaḷ koṇṭa kācu i-

inscription.head:
No. 194.
(A.R. No. 194 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE EAST WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA
IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
VIRŪPĀKSHA (II)—ŚAKA 1317, PAṄGUṈI, 2. (A.D. 1395)
inscription.p:
The king is called the son of Harihara (II). This records an order given
by the chief Nañjaṇaṅgaḷ (Minister Nañjaṇṇa) to the authorities of the temple
of Bokkiṇaṅkuḍuttaruḷiya-Nāyanār in the village remitting six out of sixteen poṉ
due (as tax) to the palace, as worship had been stopped in the temple and the
temple itself deserted, evidently owing to excess of taxation.

edition.l:
kumārar śrīviraviruppaṇ[ṇa uṭai]yar [ piruthvirā] jyam paṇṇi aruḷāniṉṟa
cakāptam 1317- ṉmel cellāniṉṟa

l.ta:
tiyati śrīmatu nañcaṇaṅkaḷ kirāmattil nayiṉār
pokkiṇaṅkuṭuttaruḷiya ṉāyaṉār ko-

yil tānattāṟku nirupam taṅkaḷ koyilukku pūcai mikutikkup patiṉā[ṟu]
poṉ irekai āṉapaṭiyāle koyil

mutalukku āṟu
poṉ kaḻittup pattup poṉ

āka varuṣantoṟum koḷḷumpaṭi araimaṉaiyil kaṇakkilu[m*] eḻutu
vittuviṭṭa aḷavukku immariyāti pattu-

ttukku hāni paṇ
ṇiṉavaṉ keṅkaikkaraiyi[l*] kārāmpacuvai

inscription.head:
No. 195.
(A.R. No. 195 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
This is a mutilated verse mentioning the Śēndaṉ (Chōḷa king) of Uranda
(Uṟaiyūr).

inscription.head:
No. 196.
(A.R. No. 196 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE ŚELVĀMBIKĀ SHRINE.
JAṬ. SUNDARA-PĀṆḌYA I—18th YEAR (A.D. 1268-69).
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the Sanskrit praśasti of the king, Samasta-
jagad-ādhāra etc. The record is damaged. It seems to register an endowment of
land made by the king for the expenses of worship and offerings to the deity on
the day of a special festival instituted in his name, and for the formation of a
garden where the god was to be taken in procession on such occasions. Provision
is also made for the daily supply of 200 lotus flowers to the temple and of
2000 lilies on festival days by the grant of 2(1/4) vēli of land to one Vikrama-
Pāṇḍyaṉ alias Śrī Mūlasthāna-Vēḷāṉ for the purpose. The god is called Śrīyāṟṟuttaḷi
Mūlasthānam-Uḍaiyār Bokkaṇaṅkuḍuttaruḷiya-Nāyanār.

l.gr:
svasti [||*] samasta jagatādhāra sōmakulatilaka madhurāpurimādhava kēraḷavamśa
[ni]rmūlana la[ṅkādvipaviluṇṭha]na

pāṇḍyadēva[ṟ]ku yāṇṭu 18- [vatu] naṭuvil maṇṭalattu tirumuṉaippāṭi
nāṭṭu grāmattu uṭaiyār śrīyāṟṟuttaḷi mulasthāna-

innāyaṉā(ā)ṟku namperāl kaṭṭiṉa

nāḷ eḻunta[ru]ḷi eṟi aruḷu[n] tiruttoppuc ceyyavum pūcaikku[m]
tiruppaṇikkum tirunāmattukkāṇi iṟaiyiliyāka viṭṭa

teṟku taraippiḷḷaiyār koyilukku meṟku

inscription.head:
No. 197.
(A.R. No. 197 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST AND SOUTH WALLS OF THE
SAME SHRINE.
JAṬ. SUNDARA-PĀṆḌYA I—15th YEAR (A.D. 1265-66).
inscription.p:
This also begins with the praśasti Samasta-jagad-ādhāra etc. It is damaged
in places. The inscription records (another) endowment by the king of 9(1/2) vēli
of land, free of all taxes, for the expenses of the service instituted in the temple
in the name of the ruling king by Vīra-Pāṇḍya. Reference is made to some lands
granted in the time of Kōpperuñjiṅgadēva.

l.gr:
sva[sti] śrīḥ [||*] svasti samastajagatādhāra sōmakulatilaka madhurāpurimādhava
kēraḷa vamśa nirmmūlana laṅkā [dvipa*] [vilu]ṇṭha na

ṇṭiyad[ē]vaṟkku yāṇṭu 15- vatu tribhuvanaccakravartti koṉerimel
koṇṭāṉ naṭuvilmaṇṭalattu kirāmattil ciyāṟṟut-

taḷi ci mula[tt]āṉamuṭ[ai]ya pokkaṇaṅ kuṭuttaruḷiya nāyaṉār koyilt
tāṉattār innāyaṉārkku virapāṇṭiyatevan

kkum tirunāḷukkum ivvūril
kopperuñciṅkar nāḷil paḻaṅkuṭippa-

[ṟṟu] niṅkalāṉa nila[tti]l [in]nāyaṉārkku tirunāmattukkāṇi iṟai
yi[liyā]ka viṭṭa viḷainilam eḻuveliyum••

patiṉaiñcāvatu•••••

kāṭṭac coṉṉom . ivai vikkiramapā-

ṇṭiya vāṇātarāyaṉ eḻuttu . 15- vatu ḷa 155 ivai toṇṭaimāṉ
[eḻuttu] ivai vaḻutiyata-

inscription.head:
No. 198.
(A.R. No. 198 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, GRĀMAM.
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH AND WEST WALLS OF THE
SAME SHRINE.
JAṬ. SUNDARA-PĀṆḌYA I—19th YEAR (A.D. 1269-70).
inscription.p:
This also begins with the same introduction Samasta-jagad-ādhāra etc. It
is damaged in the middle portion. It registers another endowment of land tax-
free by the king, but the extent of the land and the purpose of the gift are lost
in the damaged portion. Out of this land one vēli was to be set apart as jīvita
for Vikrama-Pāṇḍyaṉ alias Śrī Mūlasthāna-Vēḷān (mentioned in No. 196 above)
for a (further) daily supply of 1200 lotus flowers to the temple.

l.gr:
svasti śrīḥ svasti [||*] samasta jagadādhāra sōmakulatilaka madhurāpurimādhava
kēraḷavamśani-

rmmūlana laṅkādvipaluṇṭhana tvitiyarāma cōḷakulaśailakuliśa karṇāṭarāja
vidrāva[ṇa]

kāṭa[ka]karikūṭapākala vividharipudurggamardana virakandaragōpāla vipinadāva
dahana gaṇapati

nā[ṭṭu] kirāmattu uṭaiyā[r] pokkaṇaṅkuṭuttaruḷiya nāyaṉār koyil
tāṉattārkku innāyaṉār

kaḷum pattoṉpatāvatu aippaci māta[muta]-

l [teva]tāṉayiṟaiyili ākat tantom ippaṭikku ivvolai piṭi
pā[ṭā]ka koṇṭu kallilum cempilum veṭṭik-

nilattile oru veli nilam aṭaittuk kuṭukkavu[m]

inscription.head:
No. 199.
(A.R. No. 199 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
KANNARADĒVA (RĀSHṬRAKŪṬA KṚISHṆA III)—17th YEAR
(A.D. 953-54).
inscription.p:
The inscription is left in complete. It seems to record some provision
made for the Śrībali offerings and music during the three services in the temple
of Mūlasthānattu-Perumāṉaḍigaḷ at Pēriṅgūr by a native of Karuśūr in Śeṅguṉṟa-
nāḍu of Kaḷattūr-kōṭṭam, a division of Toṇḍai-nāḍu. The name of the donor is
not clear. Just above the last portion of the 2nd line is found engraved a frag-
ment giving the name of Parakesari ‘who took Madurai’.

l.ta: śrī kacciyun tañcaiyuṅ koṇṭa śrī śrī kaṉṉara

ṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭuk karucūr kuvaḷaikiḻācamuttiraṉ kiḻa[va]ṉ muṉaip
pāṭip periṅkūr śrī mu

inscription.head:
No. 200.
(A.R. No. 200 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARĀNTAKA I—17th YEAR (A.D. 923-24).
inscription.p:
This is also an incomplete record. It seems to register a sale of land by
the Sabhā of Periṅgūr to a certain Nakkaṉ Vīra[gā]ṅgaṉ.

l.ta:
āvaṇak kaiyyeḻuttu nakkaṉ vira[kā]ṅkaṉukku nāṅkaḷ viṟṟukkuṭutta
nilamāvatu nāṅka

inscription.head:
No. 201.
(A.R. No. 201 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PAR. RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA I—30th YEAR (A.D. 1042-43).
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 90 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Mūlasthānam-uḍaiya-Mahādēva at Pēriṅgūr a brahmadēya of Tirumuṉaippāḍi, a
division of Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu by Pūdi Gaṇapperumāṉ and his son Gaṇap-
perumāṉ Dēvargaṇḍaṉ, both resident at the village.

l.ta:
ku ivvūr vaṭakarai uṭaiyāṉ(m) pūtikaṇapperumāṉ vaiytta tiru
[nu]ntāviḷakkaraiyum ivaṉ makaṉ

kaṇapperumāṉ tevarkaṇṭaṉ vaitta tirunantāviḷakkaraiyum ākat
tirunantāviḷakkoṉṟukkum āṭu to-

ṇṇūṟum ittiru[nu]ntāviḷakkerivatāka viḷakku [kāṟ cā]ṇ niḷat
teriya vaitta viḷakkum vaittoṉ ka-

ṇapperumāṉ tevarkaṇṭaṉ vaittāṉ cantirātittavaṟ ittirunantā
viḷakku aviyāme-
y kāppāṉ śrī pātam e[ṉ talaimelatu] ||

inscription.head:
No. 202.
(A.R. No. 202 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARAKĒSARIVARMAN—date doubtful.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 40.

inscription.head:
No. 203.
(A.R. No. 203 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARAKĒSARIVARMAN “who took the head of the Pāṇḍya”—ĀDITYA II
KARIKĀLA—5th YEAR (A.D. 968-69).
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 96 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple by
Gaṇapperumān alias Kulamāṇikka-Muttaraiyan a resident of the place.

edition.l: hēśvararakṣai ||—

inscription.head:
No. 204.
(A.R. No. 204 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJA I—date lost.
inscription.p:
This is a mutilated inscription with the introduction Tirumagaḷpōla etc.
It records a gift of sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Mūlasthānattu-
Paramasvāmi by Kuḷattuḻāṉ, a resident of Kuśappāḍi in Tirumuṉaippāḍi-Muḍiyūr-
nāḍu.

l.ta: vaḷarūḻi ellāyāṇṭum toḻutakai viḷaṅkum yāṇṭe ce[ḻiyarai]-

inscription.head:
No. 205.
(A.R. No. 205 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA—16th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This stops with the mention of some maṉṟāḍis (shepherds) and Chētakaras
(architects).

inscription.head:
No. 206.
(A.R. No. 206 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJA I—17th YEAR (A.D. 1000-01)
inscription.p:
The inscription is badly damaged. It begins with introduction Tirumagaḷ pōla
etc., and records a gift of sheep for a lamp in the temple by one Munaiyamāṉ
Tirumullai••••

edition.l: [ṟku] yāṇṭu [ 17- vatu] ••• ṅkūr•••sthā-
•••• vai cāvāmuvāp perāṭu 90••••••

inscription.head:
No. 207.
(A.R. No. 207 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARAKĒSARIVARMAN—3rd YEAR.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. XIX, No. 41.

inscription.head:
No. 208.
(A.R. No. 208 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
RĀJARĀJA I—24th YEAR (A.D. 1007-08).
inscription.p:
The inscription is left unfinished. The details of date are given as Vṛiśchika,
śu. 5, [Wednesday], Tiruvōṇam corresponding to A.D. 1008, November 6,
Saturday (not Wednesday). On this date the sabhā of the place and the Sēnāpati
(commander) Mummuḍiśōḻa-Brah[mārāyar] are said to have met in the temple
at Pēriṅgūr (evidently for transacting some business).

l.gr: svasti śrī [||*] ēaiśvaryamastuḥ sammā[nya]tām śrīrājarāja rājadē
l.ta:
ttup [putaṉ]kiḻamaiyum pañcamiyum peṟṟa ti[ru]vo[ṇa]t
tiṉ nāṉṟu••• ampalattil

inscription.head:
No. 209.
(A.R. No. 209 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA I—
inscription.p:
Only a portion of the introduction of the king beginning with the words
Tirumaṉṉi vaḷara etc. is preserved.

l.ta:
tirumaṉṉi vaḷara irunilamaṭantaiyum poṟceyappāvaiyuñ cirtta[ṉi]c
celviyun taṉ perun teviyarāki yiṉpuṟa neṭitiritūḻiyu ḷiṭaituṟaināṭun tuṭarvaṉa
velippa•••••••••

pakkaṟ ṟeṉṉavaṉ vaitta cuntaramuṭiyum intiranāramun teṇṭirai yiḻa
maṇṭala muḻuvatum eṟipaṭaikkeraḷaṉ muṟaimaiyiṟ•••••••••

rāmaṉ melvaruñ cāntimaṟṟivaraṇ karuti iruttiya cempoṟṟiruttakumuṭi
•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 210.
(A.R. No. 210 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PAR. RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA (I)—2nd YEAR (A.D. 1013-14).
inscription.p:
Portions of the inscription are lost. It seems to record a gift of land to
the temple at Pēriṅgūr, a brahmadēya in Pēriṅgūr-nāḍu of Tirumuṉaipāḍi, a division
of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻamaṇḍalam by a cavalry man (name not clear) of the com-
mander (Sēnāpati) Rājē[ndraśōḻa]-Brahmamārāyar, who got it exempted from tax
by payment of a lump-sum to the sabhā of the place.

edition.l: kēsaripanmarāṉa śrīrā[jēndra*]coḻadēvarkku yāṇṭu 2-
periṅkūrnāṭṭu brahmadēyam periṅ[ kūrsa*] bhaiyom sēṉāpati rā••••

l.ta: mārāyar kutiraiccevakaṉ co•••• tu rājarājavaḷanāṭṭup paṉ-

ṟiyūr aṭṭuvāṉ paḷaviyalūr vāṉa•••• [ṭai]yāṉ cāttaṉ [u*]la
kuyya••

•••••lāl kuḻi eṇ•••• ppatteḻu kuḻiyum tāṉ vilai•••• ivvūril
liruntu [muca]tti[ya]••••

teva••••

•••••vāṟṟuk kaḻañ•••• ṟappeṟuvatāka oṭṭikkuṭuttom [ sabhai]
yom innilam uḻuṅkuṭikaḷai••••

inscription.head:
No. 211.
(A.R. No. 211 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN—4th YEAR.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. XIII, No. 83.

inscription.head:
No. 212.
(A.R. No. 212 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This consists of the concluding portions of two inscriptions. It seems
possible that one of them might form part of No. 218 below. The other portion
which is noticed here is dated in••••••••••• trayōdaśi, Saturday, Punarvasu and
registers a sale of land about 150 kuḻi in extent by one Nelvaṅgai Pārthasārathi-
Bhaṭṭaṉ a member of the Āḷuṅgaṇam of Pēriṅgūr in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu to the
temple of Mūlasthānam-uḍaiya-Paramasvāmi. The writing is of about the 11th
century A.D.

l.ta:
yam periṅkūrāḷuṅ kaṇat
tāru[ḷ] nelvaṅkaip pārtta
n nilavilaiyāvaṇam ivvūr uṭaiyār śrī mula

inscription.head:
No. 213.
(A.R. No. 213 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJ. RĀJĀDHIRĀJADĒVA—date lost.
inscription.p:
This consists of twenty disjointed fragmentary pieces uniting (probably of
more than one inscription). The introduction is that of Rājādhirāja I beginning
with the expressions Tiṅgaḷēr taru etc. The inscription seems to record a gift of
money by a private individual for burning a lamp in the temple of Mūlasthānam-
uḍaiyār, and the purchase of some land tax-free with that amount (by the temple)
from the assembly of the place.

l.ta:
tiṅkaḷertaru taṉṟoṅkal veṇkuṭaikkiḻ nilamakaḷ nilava
malar
ṟirupputalvar tammaiyum tuṉṟeḻil vāṉavaṉ villavaṉ miṉava

valavaṉ kaṉṉa kucciyar kāvalaṉ eṉa poṉ••••makuṭañcūṭṭip paṭar
pukaḻāṅkavarkkavar

edition.p: The other eleven pieces are too fragmentary to make any sense.

inscription.head:
No. 214.
(A.R. No. of 214 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJA I—12th YEAR (A.D. 995-96).
inscription.p:
This is an incomplete inscription, recording probably the purchase of some
land by the Rudragaṇapperumakkaḷ who managed the affairs of the temple of
Mūlasthānam-Uḍaiya-Paramasvāmi at Pēriṅgūr, from a shepherd of the place for
5 kaḻañju of gold.

l.ta:
tile aiṅkaḻaiñ[cu po]ṉ kuṭuttu nāṟpattaiñcu [kuḻi] koṇṭa[ti]v
vūriruntuvāḻum maṉṟāṭi cāttaṉ kūttaṉākiya

inscription.head:
No. 215.
(A.R. No. 215 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA I.
inscription.p:
Only a portion of his praśasti beginning with the words Tirumaṉṉi vaḷara
is engraved.

l.ta:
tiruma[ṉṉi] vaḷara iru[ṉi]lamaṭantaiyum poṟceyaṟpāvai
yum cirtaniccelviyun tan perunteviyarāki yiṉ[puṟa] neṭutu•••••••••

paṭarvanavāciyum cuḷḷi[cū]ḻ ma[ti]ḷ koḷḷippākkaiyu ṉaṇṇal
karumuraṇ maṇṇaikaṭakkamum porukaṭaliḻattaraicartamuṭiyum [āṅ]kavar te
•••••••••

teṇṭirai īḻamaṇṭala muḻuvatu[m*] eṟipaṭai keraḷa[ṉ*] muṟaimaiyil cūṭu
•••••••••

lamum caṅkatir•••••• tolperuṅk[āval] pala[paḻa]ntivum [ceṟuviṟ
ciṉavi]llirupattorukālaraicukaḷai kaṭṭa[paracurā]maṉ mel[va]ruñ cāntimaṟṟiva
[raṇ] karuti iru•••••••••

yil mutukiṭṭoḷitta cayaciṅkaṉ naḷapperum pukaḻoṭum piṭi iraṭṭapāṭi
eḻarai ila[kka]mum navanetikkulap perumalaikaḷum vikkiramavirac cakkarakoṭṭamum
mutirvaṭavallai maturai•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 216.
(A.R. No. 216 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJ. RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA.
inscription.p:
This has a short introduction beginning with the words Būmiyum Tiruvum.
It is damaged and also incomplete. It seems to register a sale of land by the
assembly of Pēriṅgūr to the temple (for the expenses of worship) to god Pichcha-
dēvar (Bhikshāṭana).

inscription.head:
No. 216A.
(A.R. No. 216-A of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJA I—11th YEAR (A.D. 994-95).
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with a short Sanskrit version of the contents of the
record. It registers an endowment of land for burning a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Mūlasthānam-Uḍaiyār by Muḍumbai Śōlaippirāṉ-Kramavittaṉ, a member
of the Āḷuṅgaṇaṁ of Pēriṅgūr, after purchasing the same from the assembly tax-
free.

l.gr: ntasya vṛttim datvā paṭṭa[m] pādāmśamidam ma[va]hitasya•••••••••

l.ta:
riṅkūriyāḷuṅ kaṇattāruḷ [muṭumpai]c colaippirāṉ kiramavittaṉeṉ
ivūr śrī mula

mikku cantirātittavaṟ oru nontāviḷak kerippatākak
kuṭutta nilamāvatu •

••• t teṟkil periyerivāyāṉa maṭaiynilam kiḻpālellai teṟk[ku
n]okkip pā[ñca]-

inscription.head:
No. 217.
(A.R. No. 217 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This consists of a few disconnected pieces of writing, of which the first
begins with introduction Tirumaṉṉi vaḷara etc., of Rājēndra-Chōḷa's records and
seems to register a gift of land to the temple by one Uyyapirān-Bhaṭṭan.

edition.p: Two more pieces are quite fragmentary.

inscription.head:
No. 218.
(A.R. No. 218 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH AND WEST WALLS
OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
RĀJARĀJA I—28th YEAR (A.D. 1011-12)
inscription.p:
This begins with introduction Tirumagaḷpōla etc. Portions of the inscrip-
tion are wanting. A piece of four lines found with No. 212 above seems to be
a part of this record and hence has been added after line 5. Thus connected
together the inscription records a gift of sheep for burning two perpetual lamps
in the temple, one by a lady by name Nīlichchāṉi, the wife of a member of the
Āḷuṅgaṇam of the village, and the other by a farmer (kuḍippaḷḷi) called Aṇṇāvaṉ
Appuli. At the end of the inscription is also recorded a gift of a twilight lamp
by Śiṟunaṅgai, wife of Kaīlāyattaḍigaḷ a resident of the village.

l.ta:
ṇṭamai maṉakkoḷak kāntaḷūrccālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi veṅkaināṭuṅ kaṅka
pāṭiyu nuḷampapāṭiyun taṭikaipāṭiyuṅ kuṭamalaināṭuṅ kollamuṅ kaliṅka-

mu meṇṭicai pukaḻtara īḻamaṇṭalamum iraṭṭapāṭi eḻarai ilakkamun
tiṇṭiṟalveṉṟit taṇṭāṟkoṇṭu taṉṉaruḷ vaḷarūḻiyu ḷellā
yam periṅkūr yāḷuṅ kaṇattā[ruḷ]•••• ṉṉil poṉṉār
—kku vaitta nuntāviḷakku oṉṟu āṭu toṇṇūṟu ivai

inscription.head:
No. 219.
(A.R. No. 219 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJA I—22nd YEAR (A.D. 1005-06)
inscription.p:
The inscription has the introduction Tirumagaḷ pōla etc. It records a gift
of sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple by Tāḷi Nakkaṉ Ōraḷi, a resident of
the village and a nother gift for burning a twilight lamp before god Gaṇapati, by
a shepherd.

l.ta:
tirumakaḷpola perunilaccelviyun taṉa[k*]key yurimaiy
pūṇṭamaiy ma-

ṭikapāṭiyuṅ kuṭamalaināṭuṅ kollamuṅ kaliṅkamum
eṇṭicaiy pukaḻtara vīḻamaṇṭalamum iraṭṭa-

ṇṭiṟal veṉṟit taṇṭāṟkoṇṭu taṉṉeḻil
vaḷarūḻi ellāyāṇṭun toḻuteḻa viḷaṅkum yāṇṭe ceḻi-

ṉarait tecukoḷ śrīkovirājarāja rājakecaripaṉmarāṉa śrīrāja rāja
rāja
yāṉ tāḷinakkaṉ oraḷi vacca viḷakku oṉṟu āṭu toṇṇūṟum

inscription.head:
No. 220.
(A.R. No. 220 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, PĒRAṄGIYŪR.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN—6th YEAR.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. XIII, No. 131.

inscription.head:
No. 221.
(A.R. No. 221 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, GINGEE TALUK, EYIL.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE ON THE HILL—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE.
MAHĀMAṆḌALĒŚVARA VIRUPPAṆA-UḌAIYAR
(VIRŪPĀKSHA II)—PRAMĀTHI.
inscription.p:
The cyclic year should correspond to A.D. 1399-1400. The inscription is
slightly mutilated. It records a gift of 300 kuḻi of land by a female relation of
the king for offerings to god Tirumāḍappēṟuḍaiya-Nāyaṉār at Eyil which is said
to be a jīvita (of the donor).

l.ta:
tiyati śrī makāmaṇṭalicuraṉ
viraviruppaṇa uṭaiyar marumakaṉār teviyar irācar nammuṭaiya civitamāṉa eyil
tirumāṭap peṟuṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku

edition.l:
••••••• cantiāka amutukku viṭṭa ceṟu koṉṟaittaḻuvalum itukku
vaṭakkiṟ kuṇamalliraṇṭum kuḻi [3]00- m cantirātittavarai[yum i]ṭakkaṭavatākavum
ittaṉmattukku akutam paṇṇinavan ke-

inscription.head:
No. 222.
(A.R. No. 222 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, GINGEE TALUK, EYIL.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE ON HILL—ON THE EAST WALL OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE.
inscription.p:
The inscription is not dated, but may be ascribed to the 14th century A.D.
It records a gift of 500 kuḻi of land for the expenses of worship in a service
called the Rājavibhāṭan-sandi in the temple.

edition.l: cantirātittavaraiyum naṭappatākavum ||—

inscription.head:
No. 223.
(A.R. No. 223 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, GINGEE TALUK, EYIL.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE ON HILL—ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records the assignment of two families for the maintenance of a per-
petual lamp in the temple. It is signed at the end by Rāmarasaṉ and Duvārapati-
vēḷāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 224.
(A.R. No. 224 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, GINGEE TALUK, EYIL.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE ON HILL—ON THE SOUTH AND EAST WALLS OF
THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
JAMMAṆṆA-UḌAIYAR SON OF VĪRA-KAMPAṆA (KAMPA II)—ĀNANDA.
inscription.p:
The cyclic year Ānanda corresponds to A.D. 1374-75. The inscription
records a sarvamānya gift of some land for burning a perpetual lamp in the
temple by order of the ruler.

l.ta:
ṇṇa uṭaiyaṟkuc cellāniṉṟa
āṉanta varuṣattu māci mātamutal [ni]rupappa-

ṭi tirununtāviḷakkukku viṭṭa nilam teṟkil vaṉavaṭattil vaṉamotarak
kuṇṭil ceri ceṟu iraṇṭum
varatapperiyum ākat taṭi āṟum cantirātattavaraiyum caṟu-

inscription.head:
No. 225.
(A.R. No. 225 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, GINGEE TALUK, EYIL.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE ON HILL—ON THE SAME WALLS.
inscription.p:
The inscription is simply dated in the cyclic year Raudra. It records the
gift of a house and 300 kuḻi of land to a stone mason by name Maṇḍalam son of
Māḍappiḷḷai (residing) in the maḍaiviḷāgam of the temple by the deity (Nāyiṉār).

l.ta: kam ka[ṟ*]ttaccaṉ māṭappiḷḷai makaṉ maṇṭalattukku tirumukam
kuṭuttapaṭi nām kuṭutta maṉaiyum maṉaipaṭappaiyum amarāpati kā-

edition.l:
ttā[r*] nila[m*] vaṭakkaṭaiya kuḻi 300- m kaikkoṇṭu tiruppa••••
ḻaiyya

l.ta:
akitam ceytār keṅkai [karaiyil*] pacuvai koṉ[ṟa*] pāvam
koṇṭāṉ .

inscription.head:
No. 226.
(A.R. No. 226 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, GINGEE TALUK, EYIL.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE ON HILL—ON THE SAME WALLS.
inscription.p:
This is also dated merely in the same cyclic year, and records another gift
of 300 kuḻi (each ?) of land by the same donor to a blacksmith, a carpenter and
a goldsmith, out of the dēvadāna lands belonging to the temple of Tirumāḍap-
pēṟuḍaiyār.

l.ta: tāṉattil nilam amarāpati kāttā[r*] nila[m*] kollaṉ taccaṉ taṭ-

vum itukku akitam ceyitāl keṅkai [karaiyil*] pacuvai koṉṟa
pāvam ko-

inscription.head:
No. 227.
(A.R. No. 227 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, GINGEE TALUK, EYIL.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE ON HILL—ON THREE LOOSE STONES LYING
NEAR THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
JOMMAṆA-UḌAIYAR, SON OF KUMĀRA KAMPAṆA UḌAIYAR (KAMPA II)—
SAUMYA (= Śaka 1291—A.D. 1369-70)
inscription.p:
This records the assignment of one Aḻagapperumāḷ alias Śoṭṭaikkōṉ for
the maintenance of a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tirumāḍaippēṟuḍaiya-
Nāyaṉār at Eyyil. This is signed at the end, among others, by Duvārapativēḷāṉ,
probably the same person mentioned in No. 223 above.

inscription.head:
No. 228.
(A.R. No. 228 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, GINGEE TALUK, EYIL.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE ON HILL—ON TWO MORE LOOSE STONES
LYING IN THE SAME PLACE.
SAK. RĀJANĀRĀYAṆA-SAMBUVARĀYA.
inscription.p:
This is fragmentary. The second piece, probably a part of this record,
registers a gift of land at Nuḷambaichchiṟṟēri alias Aḻagiyanāyanallūr for offerings
and worship to god Attimalla-Nāyakar (newly consecrated in the temple ?).

l.ta: kuḷpaṭṭa naṉce[y*] puṉce[y*] nilamum menokkiṉa mara-

inscription.head:
No. 229.
(A.R. No. of 229 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, GINGEE TALUK, EYIL.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE ON HILL—ON THE ROCK TO THE SOUTH OF THE TEMPLE.
TRIBH. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA—4th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of 80 kāśu to the temple of Tirumāḍaippāṟai-
Uḍaiyār by a certain Araśaṉ Vayiri of Eyil alias Rājēndraśōḻanallūr, for meeting
the expenses of worship and procession of the image of Viṉāyaka-Piḷḷaiyār on
the day of Āmāvāsya every month.

inscription.head:
No. 230.
(A.R. No. 230 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, GINGEE TALUK, EYIL.
ON A STONE SET UP TO THE LEFT SIDE ON THE WAY TO THE HILL.
inscription.p:
This is merely dated in the cyclic year Plavaṅga. It records the formation
of a street called the Śrī Bhaṇḍārināyakap-perunderu on the dēvadāna lands at
Eyil for the colonisation of weavers therein on specified terms of taxes per
loom to be paid by them to the temple year by year.

edition.l: 12- tiyati eyyil tirumaṭaiviḷāka

inscription.head:
No. 231.
(A.R. No. of 231 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, GINGEE TALUK, EYIL.
ON A ROCK TO THE SOUTH OF THE VILLAGE.
VIKRAMA-PĀṆḌYADĒVA—7th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of a thousand kuḻi and odd of land made by
one Ālālasundara-Vēḷaikkāṟaṉ of Tiruvaṇṇāmalai, for the maintenance of a well
constructed by a kaikkōḷa named Āyiramārāyaṉ of Eyil and for the requirements
of worship to the image Vēṅgai-Vināyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 232.
(A.R. No. 232 of 1906)
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, GINGEE TALUK, EYIL.
ON A SECOND ROCK TO THE SOUTH OF THE VILLAGE.
inscription.p:
This is dated merely in the cyclic year Īśvara and records a grant of some
lands as tirunāmattukkāṇi to the temple of Tirumāḍappēṟuḍaiya Nāyanār at Eyil
alias Rājēndraśōḻanallūr by a lady by name Tiruvēṅgaṇa-Nāchchi alias Būppaṇa
Māṇikkattār by the order of one Narasiṅgarāja-Uḍaiyar. The identity of this
chief is not clear. Eyil is said to be situated in Murugamaṅgalap-paṟṟu, a division
of Siṅgapura-vaḷanāḍu in Palkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

edition.l: palkuṉṟakkoṭṭattu ciṅkaperuvaḷanāṭṭu murukamaṅkalappaṟṟu irā( ca

centira) centiracoḻanallūr āṉa eyil tiṟappil teṟtillaiyil paḻam
pūṇṭiyil eri etu[r*]-

l.ta:
vāykku (vākku) vaṭameṟku kiḻaikkuṉṟattil cellappeṇṭirentalum
naṉcai nilamum erivā[y]

ciṟumalaiyāṉental ellaittaṟuvarikku kiḻakkum paṭṭittollai tātakāṟukku
kiḻakkum

uḷpaṭṭa puṉcai nilamum pātaivaḻikkum kucavaṉ kuḷattukkum teṟkum
teṉkiḻakkum uḷpaṭṭa

puṉcai nilamum uṭaiyār tirumāṭappe[ṟu]ṭaiya nāyaṉār tirunāmakkāṇi
āka ulakaṉār-

makaḷ tiruveṅkaṇa nācciy pūppa[ṇa]māṇikkattāṟku naraciṅkarāca uṭaiyar
kuṭutta

[ni]rupa[paṭi]kku uṭaiyavaṟku t[ā]ṉam tārai viṭṭamaikku kuṭiniṅkāt
tevatāṉamāy

varuṣam o[ṉṟu*]kku cūlavariāka patiṉañcu paṇam iṟuttu cantiṟātitta
varaiyum aṉupavittu-

koṇṭu erimiṉ paṇamum tāṅkaḷe taṇṭukoḷḷakkaṭavar ākavum i[nta]
taṉmattukku

akutam paṇṇiṉavarkaḷ keṅkai karaiyil karāmpacuvaik koṉṟa[tu]
pāvattile

inscription.head:
No. 233.
(A.R. No. 233 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPAṈAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE TĀḶAPURĪŚVARA SHRINE.
RĀJĀDHIRĀJA I—3[2]nd YEAR (A.D. 1049-50)
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with introduction Tiṅgaḷēr taru etc. It registers a
sale of land free of all taxes as dēvadāna-iṟaiyili to the temple of Tiruppaṉaṅgā-
ḍuḍaiyār at Vanpākkam in Kāliyūrkōṭṭam by the assembly of Vallūr in Kāyiram-
pēḍu nāḍu in the same kōṭṭam said to be a division of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-
maṇḍalam. The details of the transaction are lost in the damaged portion of
the record. The cost of the land was probably collectively paid by some persons
named in the inscription, as endowment for meeting the expenses of worship in
the temple, and for feeding daily four Śivayōgins and tapasvins.

l.ta:
[tiṅkaḷe]rtaru taṉṟo[ṅkal veṇku]ṭaikkiḻ nilamakaḷ
nilava malarmakaḷ puṇarntu ceṅko loccik karuṅ[kali kaṭintu taṉ ciṟiya tātai]-

yun tiruttamaiyaṉaiyuṅ kuṟikkoḷat taṉniḷaṅkokkaḷaiyum neṟiyuṇart
taṉṟirupputalvartammaiyu

ṅkaikkiṟaiva[ṉ oṅ]keḻiṟ pallavar kannakucciyar kāvalaneṉap po[ṉ*]
naṇi cuṭarmaṇi makuṭañcūṭṭip paṭarpukaḻāṅkavarkkavar nāṭaruḷip pāṅkuṟu manṉu

edition.p: The next line is too much damaged to be read.

inscription.head:
No. 234.
(A.R. No. 234 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
SAME SHRINE.
RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA I—32nd YEAR (A.D. 1043-44).
inscription.p:
The inscription has the introduction Tirumaṉṉi vaḷara etc. It is damaged.
It registers a sale of land similar to that mentioned in No. 233 above, to the
temple of Tiruppaṉaṅgāḍuḍaiya-Mahādēva at Vanpākkam in [Kaḻu]mala-nāḍu by
the sabhā of the place.

l.ta:
tirumaṉṉi vaḷara irunila maṭantaiyum porcceyappāvaiyum
cirttanic celviyu

perunteviyarāki yiṉpuṟa neṭutuyarūḻiyu ḷiṭaituṟaināṭun tuṭarvana
velippaṭa-

caṅkatir velai-

ṭoḷitta caya-

inscription.head:
No. 235.
(A.R. No. 235 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA I—45th YEAR (A.D. 1114-15)
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the introduction Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga etc. It
records a gift of 10 kāśu made by Vēḷaṉ Muḍigoṇḍāṉ alias Śembiyaṉ Vilup-
paraiyaṉ the headman of Karambiyam in Kīḻvēṅgai-nāḍu a division of Rājarāja-
vaḷanāḍu and deposited with two Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple of Tiruppaṉaṅ-
gāḍuḍaiyār for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple. The quantity of oil to
be supplied by them as monthly interest on the sum received is said to be 3 uḻakku
per kāśu.

l.ta: mpa nilamakaḷ vaḷara malarmakaḷ puṇara uri
kaḷ kaikkoṇṭa kācu pattu ikkācu pattukkum ti-

edition.l: ṅkaḷ policai kācu 1- kku eṇṇai muvvuḻakkāka kā-

l.ta: cu pattukku eṇṇai eḻunāḻi uriyāka polivatākavum polin

inscription.head:
No. 236.
(A.R. No. 236 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
VIRUPPAṆṆA (VIRŪPĀKSHA II), SON OF HARIYAṆA (HARIHARA II)—Śaka 1310.
inscription.p:
The details of the date are given as Vibhava, Vṛiśchika, ba. 10, Monday,
Hastā corresponding to A.D. 1388, November 23. The inscription records the
appointment of a certain Śellappiḷḷaiyār Śaivaśēkharakkālāḷ by the temple autho-
rities of Tiruppaṉaṅgāvuḍaiya-Nāyaṉār for the daily supply of water for the
sacred bath of the deity, and for rearing a flower garden called the Dānapari-
pālana-nandavanam at Śiṟupaṉaṅgāḍu, for both of which duties he was granted
150 kuḻi of land as his jīvita, two house sites for residence and three nāḻi of
offered rice every day.

l.ta:
pati śrīvira
hariyaṇa uṭaiyar kumāra viruppaṇa uṭaiyaṟku cellāniṉṟa

miyum peṟṟa attat
til nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu kāliyūrko-

ṭṭattil kaḻumalanāṭṭil pirammatecapaṟṟu tiruppaṉaṅkāṭu āḷuṭaiyār
tiruppaṉaṅkāvuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil āticaṇṭecuraṉ aṟulicceyalpaṭi ik
koyilukku tirumaiñcaṇamum

eṭuttu tirumeṟpūccum amukka tirumālaiyum paṇippaṟṟa nilai oṉṟum ciṟu
paṉaṅkāṭṭil tānaparipālana tirunantavaṉam payiṟceyya nilai oṉṟum āka inta nilai
iraṇṭum cokkaṉār makaṉ cellappiḷḷaiyār caivacekarakkālāṟ

edition.l:
ku kāṇiāṭciākak kuṭuttom inta nilaikaḷukku civitamaṭaitta caṭai
erikiḻ vaṭakoṭṭil muṭivaḻaṅkiṉāṉ taṭi 2- m ārumpūṇṭāṉ vaṭapātiyum cuppiṟa
maṇiyaṉ taṭi 3- m kuṟuñcilvaṭṭamu māñcaṉ vammi pulampaniṉṟāṉ paṅkaṉ āka

taṭi pattiṉāl kuḻi 150- m koyilil ṉāḷvaḻi peṟuñ coṟu muṉṉāḻiyum
ālālacuntaraṉ tiruvitikku teṟku maṉai iraṇṭum maṉaippaṭappaiyum kaikkoṇṭu
inta nilaikaḷ tāḻvu varāmal naṭattakkaṭavārākavum ippaṭikku i-

l.ta:
rarum t[āṉattār]om ippaṭikku āṇṭa
perumāḷ tirucciṟṟampalapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu••••• āḻvāṉ tillaikkūttapaṭṭar
eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 237.
(A.R. No. 237 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE EAST WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
The writing of the inscription seems to be of about the 15th century A.D.
It is incised in a space of about six feet between two palmyra trees which bear
a small horizontal arrow in their middle portion to indicate the length. It states
that this is the linear measure of the nāḍu for the lands of the brahmadēśappaṟṟu
(Tiruppanaṅgāḍu) of Tambirānār.

inscription.head:
No. 238.
(A.R. No. 238 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE KṚIPĀNĀTHA SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This begins with the birudas of the Vijayanagar kings, but the name of the
king is lost. It is also unfinished. It seems to record the appointment of a
stone-mason by the temple authorities (with the grant of some land for his
living).

l.ta:
rirāya vipāṭaṉ pāṣaikkut
tappuvarāyara kaṇṭaṉ paccama

tiruppaṉaṅkāṭuṭaiya ācārikku uṭaiyār tiruppaṉaṅkāṭu u[ṭai*]ya
nāyanā[r*] aruḷicceyaṟpaṭikku kaṟtaccakāṇi kuṭutta paricāvatu ivaṉ nam iṭa

inscription.head:
No. 239.
(A.R. No. 239 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
VIRŪPĀKSHA (II), SON OF HARIHARA (II)—Śaka 1313.
inscription.p:
The details of the date are given as Prajāpati, Siṁha, śu. 13, Sunday,
Tiruvōṇam corresponding to A.D. 1391, August 13. This records the sale of the
post of the temple and village accountant by the temple authorities to a certain
Tiruvaṇṇāmalai-uḍaiyār Ishabhavāhana-dēvar for 350 paṇam. Mention is made
of a severe draught in the previous year (Pramōdūta) as a result of which the
temple got no yield on its lands and had to pay taxes to the king with this sale
amount. The appointment is said to have carried with it, two free-hold houses
situated to the west of Śaivaśēkharakālār's house, and certain other privileges.

edition.l:
svasti [||*] śrīman mahāmaṇḍalēśvaran arirāya vipāṭaṉ pāṣaikkut tappuvarāyar
kaṇṭaṉ pūṟuva dakṣiṇa paccima samudrātipati śrīvira ariyaṇa uṭaiyar kumārar viruppaṇa
uṭaiyar pṛthivi rācciyam paṇṇi aruḷāni••••••• yirattu [mu]ṉṉūṟṟu oru
pattu muṉṟiṉmel cellāniṉṟa praśāpati samvaṟsarattu siṅṅanāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu
traiyōdaśiyum

l.ta:
nāyaṟṟukkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa tiruvoṇattu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻa
maṇṭa[la*]ttu kāliyūṟ koṭṭattu kaḻumalanāṭṭu

[h*]matecappaṟṟu uṭaiyār
āḷuṭaiyār tiruppaṉaṅkāṭuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyilil śrīmā

rum tānattārum
immaṇṭa••••••••• ṉāṭṭu ippaṟṟu nāṭṭuk kaikkoḷarum pākkam uṭaiyāṉ
akampaṭiveḷār tiruvaṇṇāmalai uṭaiyār iṣapavākaṉa tevaṟku aru[ḷi*]c
ceyal-

mātota varuṣam maḻai illāmal veṇ
talaioṭu uṟuṇṭa karippāy paṇattukkup patiṉāḻi nellu koḷḷakkiṭaiyāmal ūru
oru varuṣam āka kum••••••• atikāri coṉṉapaṭi tervaippaṇam uḷḷatu
taraveṇum eṉṟu eṅkaḷai [paṇi]ttuvaikkaiyil kuṭu[kka] mutal illātapaṭi āle
uṭaiyār

tiruppaṉaṅkāṭuṭaiya nāyaṉār tirunāmattukkāṇiāṉa koyiṟkaṇakkum
ūrkaṇakkukāṇi āṉa tiruppaṉaṅkāṭu ciṟupaṉaṅkāṭu veḷḷaikuḷam attippaṟṟu
vaṉpākkam tiṟappu pāti uṭppaṭa āka ivūrkaḷil nāṟpāṟkellaikku uṭpaṭa pala
tāṅkalum pala kuppamum āka ivūrkaḷil ūrkkaṇakku kāṇik••••••• āti
caṇṭecuravilaiāka emmil icainta vilaipporuḷ aṉṟāṭu kaṉmikkum vācipaṭāta
naṟ

ippaṇam muṉṉūṟṟu aimpatukkum innāyaṉār koyil kaṇakku
kāṇiyum

ālāla cuntaraṉ tiruvitikku melai orucciṟakaṉ tiruvitiyil caivacekara
kālār maṉaikku melaimaṉai iraṇṭum maṉaippaṭappaiyum tiruppaṉaṅkāṭu ciṟuppaṉaṅ
kāṭu vaḷḷaikuḷam vaṉpākkam āka ivūrkaḷil pala kuppamum pala tāṅkalum uṭppaṭa
maṉaikkaṇakku kāṇiyum maṉaikaḷum maṉaippaṭappaiyum kaṇakkaṉ paṭṭikaḷum kaṇakkaṉ
peṟum••••••• vai mutalum te[vaiyum] kaṇakku ilakkaiyum aruḷicceyalpaṭi
āticaṇṭecuravilai āvutākavum ituve vilai

kāṇiyum ūrkk[ā*]ṇiyum maṉaikaḷum kaṇakkuppaṭṭikaḷum cantiṟātitta
varaiyum ivarkku viṟṟu oṟṟi

nāṅkaḷ paṟṟiṉatai nāṉ ivūrkaḷ cūlavarikku oṟṟi
vaittatu

ākap paṇam muṉṉūṟṟu
aimpatukku kāṇiyum caṇṭecuravilai āka vilaippiṟamāṇam paṇṇikkuṭuttom
veṇpākkamuṭaiyār akampaṭi veḷār tiruvaṇṇāmalai uṭaiyār

iṣapavākaṉatevaṟkku āḷuṭaiyār tiruppaṉaṅkāṭuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil
śrī mākecurarum tāṉattārom intak kāṇiāṭccikku oru kalaṉum illaik kalaṉuḷa
vāyt toṟṟuppaṭil nāṅkaḷe tirttu kuṭuk[ka kaṭavom] ivai uṭaiyānāna

centāmaraikkaṇṇan tillaināyakaṉ eḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 240.
(A.R. No. 240 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL OF THE
KṚIPĀNĀTHA SHRINE.
KṚISHṆADĒVA-MAHĀRĀYA—Śaka 1432.
inscription.p:
The details of date are given as Pramōdūta, Karkaṭaka, śu. 3, Monday,
Punarvasu corresponding to A.D. 1510, July 8. The nakshatra was Maghā not
Punarvasu. The king is called the son of Narasānāyaka who bears the birudas
Gaṇḍa kaṭāri Sāḷuva and Vanyavēṭṭai-kaṇḍaruḷiya. The inscription records the gift
of the taxes on yarn due to him from the weavers of the village by Tirumalai-
nāyakar, son of Ellappa-nāyakar, for meeting the expenses of special worship and
offerings known as Vasanta-maṇḍapa-ubayam on the 6th day of the festival in
the month of Vaigāśi in the temple of Tiruppaṉaṅgāḍuḍaiyār.

edition.l:
sakābdam 1432- ṉ melcellā niṉṟa pramōtūta samvatsarattu kaṟkaṭaka
nāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu tṛtikaiyum sōmavāramu[m] peṟṟa puṇarpūcattu [n]āḷ
jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattuk kāliyūrk koṭṭattuk kaḻumala-

l.ta:

pacuvaik koṉṟa toṣattile poka-

inscription.head:
No. 241.
(A.R. No. 241 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST AND SOUTH WALLS OF THE MAṆḌAPA
IN FRONT OF THE TĀḶAPURĪŚVARA SHRINE.
VIRŪPĀKSHA II, SON OF HARIHARA II—Śaka 1303 (wrong for 1304).
inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Dundubhi, Makara, śu. 5, Friday, Rēvati
corresponding to A.D. 1383, January 9. This registers the sale of some specified
houses and house-sites situated in three streets of the tirumaḍaiviḷāgam belonging
to the temple of Tiruppanaṅgāvuḍaiya-Nayanār at Brahmadēśappaṟṟu in Kaḻu-
mala-nāḍu, a division of Kāliyūrkōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻamaṇḍalam, to certain
Kaikkōḷas, Kaikkōḷa-mudalis and dēvaraḍiyār for 150 paṇam, so as to meet the
demand of śūla-vari (from the king) when the local tank had breached, and the
temple had no other resources to pay the tax. Mention is made of a street
Vaśavaṇaṉ-tiruvīdi and of a house belonging to one Adiruṅkaḻal-Perumāḷ
Toṇḍaimāṉār.

l.ta:
ariyaṇa uṭaiyar kumāra viruppaṇa
uṭaiyarkku cellāniṉṟa

nāḷ ceyaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattuk kāliyūr koṭṭattuk kaḻumala
nāṭṭup piramatecappaṟṟu tiruppaṉaṅkāṭṭu āḷuṭaiyār

tiruppaṉaṅkāvuṭaiya nāyaṉār tirunāmattukkāṇi tiruppaṉaṅkāṭu kaiyk
koḷaṟkkum kaiykkoḷamutalikaḷukkum tevaraṭiyāṟkum taṇṭecuravilaiyāka viṟṟa
tirunāmattukkāṇi tirumaṭaiviḷākattil teṟkut tiruvācalukku teṟku caṉṉatit
teru tevar aṭiyār maṉaikiḻciṟaku kalnāṭṭukku vaṭakkumaṉaiyum maṉaippaṭappaiyum
melciṟaku melaittirumatiḷ teṟkukkoṭikku cevvai vacavaṇaṉ tiruvitiyile uṟa
maṉaiyum

maṉaippaṭappaiyum teṟku vacavaṇaṉ tiruviti teṉciṟaku uvaccar maṉai
iraṇṭum niṅkal āka itukku meṟku melaittiruvitiyil kiḻciṟakil koṭiyil
cevvaikku kiḻakku maṉaiyum kalṉāṭṭu cevvai maṉaippaṭappaiyum vaṭaciṟaku melait
tiruvitiyil kiḻciṟakukku kiḻakku caṉṉatit teruvile uṟa maṉaiyum kalṉāṭṭu cevvai
maṉaiyump paṭappaiyum vaṭakkut tiruvācalukku kiḻakku mā

maṭattukkut teṟku
tirumatiḷile uṟa maṉai iraṇṭum maṉaippaṭappaiyum itukku meṟku vaṭakkut tiruviti
vaṭaciṟaku melaittiru-

vitikku kiḻakku maṉaiyum maṉaippaṭappaiyum teṉciṟaku a[ti]ruṅkaḻal
perumāḷ toṇṭaimāṉār maṉaiyum maṉaippaṭappaiyum uṭpaṭa itukku meṟku
melaittiruvitiyile uṟa maṉaiyum maṉaipaṭappaiyum ākat teru muṉṟum caṇṭecura
vilaiyāka niṟcayitta

ippaṇam nūṟṟu aiympatum ivvūril caṭaieri matakile
uṭain(t)tu pokaiyil cūlavarikku mutal illātapaṭiāle inta teru muṉṟum
kāṇiāṭciāka kaikkoḷar kaiy[k*]koḷamutalikaḷ-

ḷum tevaraṭiyārkkum viṟṟu ippaṇam nūṟṟuaiympatum vāṅki kuṭut
tom itteru muṉṟiṉāl uḷḷa maṉaiyum maṉaippaṭappaiyum ivarkaḷukku viṟṟoṟṟi

tāya tāṉaṅkaḷukkum uri[t*]tāvatākavum ippaṭikku viṟṟu vilaip
piṟamāṇam paṇṇikkuṭuttom ikkoyilil

rum tāṉattārom ippaṭikku
ivai āṇṭaperumāḷ tirucciṟṟampalapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai cirāḻvāṉ
tillaikkūttapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu

ippaṭikku ivai koyil kaṇakku piramātarāyaṉ eḻuttu ikkoyil
poṟkoyil ācāri eḻuttu .

inscription.head:
No. 242.
(A.R. No. 242 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALLS.
inscription.p:
This is dated in the cyclic year Sarvadhāri without giving the Śaka year.
It is possible that the inscription might be of the same period as No. 241 above.
It records an agreement between the temple authorities and the Kaikkōḷas and
Kaikkōḷa-mudalis of Tiruppanaṅgāḍu fixing the amount of taxes due on each
loom to the temple, and the tax-free grant of 1350 kuḻi of land under the Śaḍai-
yēri tank to nine persons as jīvita at 150 kuḻi each by the temple for keeping
watch over the temple. These persons were to get besides, two nāḻi of offered
rice every day from the temple.

l.ta:
tiyati uṭ[ai]yā[r*] tiruppaṉaiṅkāvuṭaiya
nāyiṉār koyil śrīmāhe

rarum tāṉattārum tiruppaṉaiṅkāṭṭil kaikk(e)oḷar
kaikkoḷamutalikaḷukku kalveṭṭikkuṭuttapaṭi ivarkaḷ perkku taṟikkaṭamai vācal
maṟṟum pala upātikaḷum uṭpaṭa

varuṣattukku taṟi oṉṟukku nālu mum kiḻttaṟikku iraṇṭu mum
putukkuṭi[k*]ku

m koḷḷakkaṭavatākavum tirumeṉikāvalukku mutalikaḷ per
oṉpatukku per oṉṟukku nilam caṭaiyerikiḻ nilatti-

le nūṟṟuaiympatu kuḻi āka āyirattu muṉṉūṟṟu aiy[m*]patu kuḻiyum
civitamāka ca

ṟātittavaraiyum naṭakkakaṭavatu ākavum mutalikaḷukku
tirumenikāvalukku aṭaicca nilaṅkaḷukku kaṭamai ponvari coṭi cūlavari mel
piṟa[k*]kum paḻavari putuvari eṉṟu cellakaṭava[tu*]

āka caṉtir[ā*]tita
varai naṭakka[kka*]ṭavatākavum tirumeni kāvalukku irunāḻi ariciccoṟu nāḷ
toṟum iṭakkaṭavatākavum mutalikaḷukku varuṣattukku taṟikku

koḷḷak
kaṭavom aṭaippu nilam taṟi pattukku oṉṟu kuḻikku ceṟa[k*]kaṭavomākavum

ippaṭikku cimāyecuranampi yeḻuttu ippaṭikku tirucciṟṟampalapaṭṭaṉ
eḻuttu ippaṭikku koyil kaṇakku tiruppilicuramuṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 243.
(A.R. No. 243 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
SAME MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
This is also dated merely in the cyclic year Vikāri, Māśi, Śivarātri. The
writing seems to be of a later date than in No. 242 above. The inscription
records the consecration of the images of Dakshiṇāmūrthi, Mahā-Vishṇu, Brahmā
and Durgā (in the niches round the central shrine) in which the temple was
hitherto wanting, and the provision made for their worship by land and money
gifts by one Veṇṇaikkūttaṉ, son of Anantaṉ of the Ātrēya-gōtra, a resident of
Īchchambāḍi. Mention is made of another donor also by name Veṅgappar-Piḷḷai,
who is said to have given his magamai and uḷḷāyam incomes for an additional
worship in the evenings to Dakshiṇāmūrthi and for offerings during the sacred
bath on Fridays of the god and goddess in the main shrine.

l.ta: vikāri varuṣam m[ā]ci mātam civarāttirai puṇṇiyakālattile
yilukku tevaku[ṭi] nāyamār aṉṟiyile irukkaiyil taṅka-

kku tekṣaṇāmuṟtti nayiṉāṟku muṉṟu cantikku arici muṉṉāḻiyum tuṟkā
tevikku
arici nāḻiyum āka arici nāṉāḻikku añciliraṇṭuppaṭikku nāḷ oṉṟukku

edition.l:
āka varuṣam 1- ku i 37 ḷa ku maṅaru viṭṭa ettattaṉūr pututiruttu
nantavaṉappa

ṟku yirāttiri viḷakku 3kkum viṭṭa kavaraitteru tāṟu— 5i 10 periya
kollai

tinayiṉāṟku tiṉam tiruvantikkāppu naṭakkakka[ṭa]vatākavum makamai
uḷḷā-
yam veṅkappar piḷḷai taṉmamāka tampirāṉāṟkum nāccimāṟkum comavā-

ram••• cukkiṟavārattukku apiṣekattukku teṅkāy paḻam cakkarai
viṭṭa maka-

tar akitam niṉaittavar kaṅkaikkaraiyile kārāmpacuvaik koṉṟa
toṣatti-

lum taṉṉiṭa mātā pitāvait taṉ kaiyyāra vataitta toṣattilum
pokak-

inscription.head:
No. 244.
(A.R. No. 244 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE EAST WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA
IN FRONT OF THE KṚIPĀNĀTHA SHRINE.
RĀJ. KULŌTTUṄGA (II)—10th YEAR (A.D. 1142-43).
inscription.p:
The inscription has the introduction Pūmēvu vaḷar etc. It records a gift
of 32 cows for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple by Kampaṉ alias
Vikramaśōḻa-Periyaraiyaṉ, a farmer kuḍippaḷḷi of Eṉṟattūr the eastern quarter
of Śri Madhurāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam an independent village (taniyūr) of
Kaḷattūr-kōṭṭam a division of Jayangoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam. Tiruppaṉaṅgāḍu was
situated in Kāliyūr-kōṭṭam of the same maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
pūmevu vaḷar tirupponmārvu puṇara ṉāmevu kalaimakaḷ nalam
peritu ciṟappa vicayamāmakaḷ velpuyattiru-

ppa icaiyin celvi eṇṭicai viḷaṅka nirupar vantiṟañca niṇilamaṭantai
yait tirumaṇam puṇarntu cirvaḷar tarum aṇimuṭika

vitteṉa maṇimuṭi cūṭi mallaiñālattup palluyiṟ kellām ellaiyi
linpam iyalviṉileyti veṇkuṭai niḻaṟṟac ceṅ-
kolocci vāḻi pallūḻi yāḻi naṭappac cempon vira

vaṟku
yāṇṭu pattāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattuk kāli-

yūṟkoṭṭattuk kaḻumalaṉāṭṭu uṭaiyār tiruppaṉaṅkāṭuṭaiyāṟku im
maṇṭalattuk kaḷattūṟkoṭṭattu taṉiyūr

maṅkalattuk kiḻpiṭākai eṉṟattūṟ kuṭippaḷḷi kampaṉāṉa
vikkiramacoḻap periyarai[yane]n ittevaṟku vaitta tirununtāvi-
ḷakku oṟṟukku viṭṭa pacu muppattiraṇṭum ittevar

edition.l:
paṇṇuvār keṅkaiyiṭaik kumariyiṭai ceytār ceyta pāpattaip
paṭuvār itu panmāhēśvara rakṣai |||—

inscription.head:
No. 245.
(A.R. No. 245 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. RAJARĀJADĒVA (III)—18th YEAR (A.D. 1233-34)
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 32 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Tiruppanaṅgāḍuḍaiyār by Ṉīṟaṇindāṉ alias Śēdirāyaṉ, a resident of Uṟandaiyūr
(near Paṉaṁguḍi ?) in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, a division of Śōḻamaṇḍalam, who is
called a kaṉmi (agent ?) of Śambugarāyar.

l.ta:
ṟku yāṇṭu pati
ṉeṭṭāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu

kkāliyūr koṭṭattuk kaḻumalaṉāṭṭu āḷuṭaiyār tiruppaṉaṅkāṭu uṭai
yāṟku campukarāyar kaṉmika-

inscription.head:
No. 246.
(A.R. No. 246 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records a ban (imposed in the name of the god ?) against the felling
of the living palmyra trees (of the village).

inscription.head:
No. 247.
(A.R. No. of 247 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This seems to state that some lands situated in Vaṉpākkam, Śaḍaippaḷḷi
and Tiruppaṉaṅgāḍu villages which constituted a unit known as Tirukkaṇṇappa-
viḷāgam were granted for offerings to god Vadakkil-uḍaiya-Nāyanār (Kṛpānātha)
in the temple at Śiṟupanaṅgāḍu by the Vēṭṭai-Vichchādirar for the merit of
Śambuvarayar (chief ?). The imprecation at the end implies that they claim to
belong to the hunter clan of Tirukkaṇṇappar.

l.ta:
uṭaiyār tiruppaṉaṅkāṭuṭaiyār tevatāṉam vaṉpākkamum
vaṭakaḻaṉi [ca]ṭaipa[ḷḷi]yum tiruppaṉaṅkāṭāṉa uḷḷūrum tiruvellaiyum śrīkoyilu
mā-

ṉa tirukkaṇṇappaviḷākamuñ ciṟupanaṅkā[ṭṭi]l vaṭakkiluṭaiya nāya
ṉārkku tiruvamutupaṭi celuttuvatākac cammatittu campuvarāyakaḷukku naṉṟāka

viṭṭom veṭṭai viccātirarom itukkaḻivu colluvār veṭṭaik
kāṟarum tirukkaṇṇappavaṅcamumallātār itu paṉmāhe

inscription.head:
No. 248.
(A.R. No. 248 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE EAST AND NORTH BASES OF THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
KAMPAṆA-UḌAIYĀR (KAMPA II) SON OF BOKKAṆA
(BUKKA II)—Śaka 1291.
inscription.p:
This is dated in Saumya, Kumbha, śu. 7, Aśvati corresponding to
A.D. 1380, February 2, Saturday (not Wednesday). It records the appointment
as tiruviḷakkukkuḍi of three shepherd residents of the place for the daily supply
of an āḻākku of ghee and 2 nāḻi of milk each (with necessary endowments) for
god Viḍaiyinmēlvaruvār and Aḍaiyil-Anbuḍaiyār in the temple at Tiruppanaṅgāḍu
by order of the king. The village is said to have formed part of Brahmadēśap-
paṟṟu in Kaḻumala-nāḍu a division of Kāliyūr-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-
maṇḍalam. Mention is made of two officers of the king viz., Viṭṭappar of
Ānaigondi and Nambi-Āḻvār Toṅgarāyar.

edition.l:
svasti [||*] śrīmanmahāmaṉu maṇṭaleśvaran arirāyavipāṭaṉ pāṣaikkut
tappuvarāyar kaṇṭan paccama pūrva dakṣiṇu samutrātipati śrīvirapokkaṇa uṭaiyar
kumārar kampaṇa uṭaiyaṟku cellāniṉṟa śakābdam 1291- ṉ meṟ cellāninṟa
savumiya varuṣattu kumpanāyaṟṟu pūṟuvapakṣattu sattamiyum aśvatiyum peṟṟa putaṉ
kiḻamai nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu kāliyūṟ koṭṭattuk kaḻumalanāṭṭup
piramatecap paṟṟut tiruppaṉaṅkāṭṭu

l.ta:
āḷuṭaiyār viṭaiyiṉmelvaruvārkku[m*] uṭaiyār aṭaiyilan
puṭaiyāṟkum uṭaiyā[r*] aruḷicceyaṟpaṭikku āṉaikunti viṭṭapparum nampi
āḻvār toṅ[ka]rāyarum inta nāyaṉmāṟku tiruviḷakkukuṭi āka viṭṭa tiruppaṉaṅ
kāṭṭil irukkum maṉṟāṭikaḷil māccā[t]taṉ makaṉ iḷaiyaperumāḷaiyum pirā
ṉāṇṭai makaṉ pirāṅkoṉaiyum kāḷa[t]tikkoṉ makaṉ vāttakoṉaiyum
ca

neyyum per oṉṟukku iruṉāḻi pāl amutukku aḷakkakkaṭavatāka
viṭṭom ippaṭikku nāḷ oṉṟukku āḻākku ne[y*]yum nāḷoṉṟukku iruṉāḻi
pālum

titavaraiyum celvatāka viṭṭom
itukkaḻivu paṇṇuvār keṅkaikaraiyiṟ kurāṟpacuvaik koṉṟāṉ pāvatte
puka[k*]kaṭavarkaḷ itu paṉmā

inscription.head:
No. 249.
(A.R. No. 249 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
SAME MAṆḌAPA.
KAMPAṆA-UḌAIYĀR (KAMPA II) SON OF BOKKAṆA-UḌAIYĀR.
inscription.p:
The date is given as Sādhāraṇa, Siṁha, ba. 5, Rēvati which would
correspond to A.D. 1370, August 11. (Śaka 1293 current). This records the
assignment of three more families by order of the king for the supply of the
same quantity of ghee and milk to the temple as mentioned in No. 248 above.
Aḍaippattu Ilakkappar and Ānaigundi Viṭṭappar are mentioned here as the
officers of the king who made the appointment.

l.ta:
arirāyavipāṭaṉ pāṣaikku tappuva
rāyarkaṇṭaṉ śrīvirapokkaṇa uṭaiyar kumāra kampaṇa uṭaiyarkkuc cellāniṉṟa

yum peṟṟa tiṅkaḷ
kiḻamaināḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattuk kāliyūrkkoṭṭattuk kaḻumalanāṭṭup
piramatecappaṟṟut tiruppaṉaṅkāṭṭu āḷuṭaiyār

viṭaiyiṉmel varuvārkkum aṭaiyilaṉpu uṭaiyāṟkum uṭaiyar
aruḷicceyalppaṭikku aṭaippattu ilakkapparum āṉaikunti viṭṭapparum inta
nāyaṉmāṟku tiruviḷakkukkuṭiyāka viṭṭa tiruppaṉaṅkāṭṭil irukkum maṉṟāṭikaḷil
nilāṇṭai maṇiyakkoṉ makaṉ mutaliyaiyum cokkaṉ makaṉ peruṅkaṉāṉa vāṉava
taraiyakkonaiyum ivaṉ maccunaṉ tiruvaraṅkaṉaiyum

cantiṟātittavarai nāḷ oṉṟukku peroṉṟukku āḻākku neyyum irunāḻi
pālum amutukku aḷakka[k*]kaṭavatāka viṭṭom ippaṭikku intak kuṭi muṉṟum

nāḷoṉṟukku uḻakku āḻākku neyyum aṟunāḻi pālu[m*] inṉāḷ mutal
ca

tavarai nāḷvaḻi aḷakka[k*]kaṭavatāka viṭṭom itukku aḻivu paṇṇu
mavar keṅkaikkaraiyil kurāṟpacuvai koṉṟāṉ pāvattaip paṭuvarkaḷ itu
paṉmā

inscription.head:
No. 250.
(A.R. No. 250 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
VĪRA KUMĀRA KAMPAṆA (KAMPA II)—Śaka 1289 (Wrong for 1290).
inscription.p:
The details of the date are given as Kīlaka, Āvaṇi 2[4], Simha, śu. 5,
Friday, Chitrā which would correspond to A.D. 1368, August 18. It was
Āvaṇi 21 not 24. The inscription records the appointment by the temple autho-
rities of a certain Ārya Bhāradvāji-Mudali alias Vijayaśiṅgadēva of the Bōdāyana-
sūtra as supervisor of the affairs of the temple assigning to him a house for his
residence.

edition.l:
svasti śrīman arirāyavipāṭaṉ pāṣaikku tappuvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ mahā
maṇḍaliśvaraṉ śrī virakumāra kampaṇayuṭaiyaṟku cellāniṉṟa cakābdam 1289- mel
cellāniṉṟa kilaka varuṣam āvaṇi mātam 2[4]- tiyati siṅhanāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu
pancamiyum veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa

l.ta:
mutaliyāṉa vicaya
ciṅkatevaṉukku tiruppaṉaṅkāṭṭil viṭaiyiṉmel varuvārum aṭaiyilaṉpuṭai
yārum aruḷicceytappaṭikku yikkoyilil u[ṭ*]kkaṅkāṇiyum pala mutal
kaṅkāṇiyum nammuṭaiya muttiraiyu miṭṭu nammuṭaiya vācalumakak kaṭavarākavum

maṉaiyum maṉaipaṭaippum āṭiyaruḷuvāṉa maṉaikku vaṭakku ati[ru]ṅkaḻalpperumāḷ
maṉaikku teṟku mukkol taṟai kuṭuttom itu caṉtirātittavaraiyāka yivaṉukku
naṭakkumpaṭi kuṭuttom ivai āṇṭaperumāḷ tirucciṟṟampalapaṭṭa

inscription.head:
No. 251.
(A.R. No. 251 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
MAHĀMAṆḌALĒŚVĀRA VIRUPPAṆA-UḌAIYĀR VIRŪPĀKSHA II—Śaka 1303
(Wrong for 1304).
inscription.p:
This is dated in Dundubhi, Mīna, śu. 5, Wednesday, Uttiram correspond-
ing to A.D. 1383. The details given are irregular. It registers the sale of a
plot of wet and dry lands called Iḷaiyaperumāḷ-tāṅgal belonging to the temple as
tirunāmattukkāṇi, for 50 paṇam to a certain Iḷaiyaperumāḷ son of Māchchāttaṉ
who is one of the three persons mentioned in No. 248 above. With this amount
the temple authorities are stated to have repaired the breach in the local tank
called the Śaḍaiyēri which had left the lands of the whole village uncultivated
and fallow for a long time.

l.ta:
ariyaṇa uṭaiyar kumāra[r*] viruppaṇa
uṭaiyaṟkuc cellāniṉṟa

maiyum peṟṟa uttirattunāḷ ceyaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattuk kāliyūr
koṭṭattuk kaḻumalanāṭṭup piramatecappaṟṟut tiruppaṉaṅkāṭu āḷuṭaiyārkku
