inscription.head:
No. 252.
(A.R. No. 252 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
ACHYUTADĒVA-MAHĀRĀYA—Śaka 1460.
inscription.p:
The details of the date are given as Viḷambi, Mēsha, śu. 11, Monday
corresponding to A.D. 1538, April 8. The tithi was śu. 10. This records an
endowment made by the king of half of the village Vanpākkam with its annual
income of 40 poṉ left as poliyūṭṭu-ubayam with one Muḍumbai Veṅkappar of
Īchchambāḍi for meeting the daily expenses of an offering to god Tiruppanaṅgāv-
uḍaiya-Nāyanār Anbuḍaiya-Nāyanār.

ṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattuk kāliyūṟ koṭṭattu kaḻumalavaḷanāṭṭu
piramateca-

ppaṟṟu tiruppaṉaiṅkāṭu uṭaiyār tiruppaṉaiṅkāvuṭaiya nayiṉār
aṉpuṭaiya nayiṉār tampirā-

rāyaṟku puṇṇiyamāka īccampāṭiyil muṭumpai

yūṭṭu upaiyamāka viṭṭa

ceytaruḷa nāḷ 1- kku amutukku citaṅakku taḷikai 6- kkum paṭi veñ

inscription.head:
No. 253.
(A.R. No. 253 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
ACHYUTADĒVA-MAHĀRĀJA—Śaka 1453, Khara (A.D. 1530-31).
inscription.p:
This records an order of (the chief) Bhōgayadēva-Mahārāja regranting
the remission of the jōḍi amounting to 50 poṉ in favour of the temple of Tirup-
panaṅgāḍuḍaiyār, for the merit of the king, after obtaining the sanction of
Tirumalaidēva Mahārāja. It is stated that this remission of the jōḍi tax had
been already granted in the time of Krishṇadēva-Mahārāya, but was being held
up by Vīra Narasimharāya-Nāyakkar even after his receipt of orders from
(minister) Śāḷuva Timmaya. This fact was now represented by the sthānikas of
the temple to Tirumalaidēva, while he was camping at Kāñchīpuram after
capturing Vīra-Narasimharāya-Nāyakkar in the course of his campaign against
Tiruvaḍi-rājya.

l.gr: svasti śrīmān mahāmaṇḍalēśvara mētini misurakhaṇḍa khaṭṭāri-
rāya mahārāya pūrvadakṣaṇa paścima uttara satu samudrātipati śrī-

edition.l:
1453- ṉ meṟcellāniṉṟa karasamvatsarattu śrīman mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
tirumalai

vaḷanāṭṭup piramatecappaṟṟu

tiruppaṉaiṅkāṭu uṭaiyār tiruppaṉaiṅkāvuṭaiya nayināṟku joṭi cutta

inscription.head:
No. 254.
(A.R. No. 254 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, TIRUPPANAṄGĀḌU.
ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE INNER SIDE OF THE WEST WALL OF THE FIRST PRĀKĀRA.
DĒVARĀYA-UḌAIYAR (DĒVARĀYA I) SON OF HARIHARA II—Śaka 1320 (current)
inscription.p:
This inscription is badly damaged. It is dated in Īśvara, Kanyā, śu. 10,
Wednesday, Uttiram, corresponding to A.D. 1397, September 2, but the week
day was Sunday. It seems to register the sale of land by five persons to the
Ūravar of Kaṇṇippākkam for twenty poṉ for digging a feeder channel from the
river to the irrigation tank of their village, after getting the latter repaired
Mention is made of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Sāmayadēva-Mahārāja son of Vēṅgi
Tribhuvanamalla Chaḷukki-Nārāyaṇa Sūryadēva-Mahārāja who was probably the
regional administrator at the time.

edition.l:
su[va*]sti [śrī] makāmaṇṭalicuran ariyarāyavibhāṭan pāṣaikkut tappuva
rāyar kaṇṭan muvarāyarakaṇṭan pūṟuvate[k*]ṣaṇa uttira camuttirātipati śrīvira
ariyaṇa uṭaiyar kumāra[r*] tevarāya uṭaiyar piṟati[vi*] irācciyam paṇṇi
aruḷāniṉṟa cakāttam 13[20]- ṉmel cellāniṉṟa icuvaṟa varuṣam kaṉṉi
nāyaṟṟu puṟuvapakṣattu tecamiyum putaṉkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa uttirattunāḷ śrīmanmakā
maṇṭalicuran veṅkitrihuvanamalla ca[ḷu]kkanārāyaṇa••• [cūri]yatevamakārācāviṉ

[piram]matecappaṟṟu [nit]taviṉotanallūr

inscription.head:
No. 255.
(A.R. No. 255 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, PUDUPPĀḌI.
ON A STONE KEPT IN THE P.W.D. BUNGALOW.
inscription.p:
This is a short label inscription in characters of about the 11th century
A.D. and mentions Ravikulamāṇikka-perumbaḷḷi evidently marking a Jaina
temple then existing.

inscription.head:
No. 256.
(A.R. No. 256 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, PUDUPPĀḌI.
VĒDANĀRĀYAṆA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH AND WEST WALLS OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE.
inscription.p:
The writing of this inscription seems to be of about the 17th century A.D.
It records the re-consecration of god Vēdanārāyaṇa-Perumāḷ in a new temple at
Karaivaḻi Puduppāḍi in Kalavaippaṟṟu in his amara nāyakattanam in Paḍaivīḍu-
rājya by the chief Ōbuḷa-Nāyaka son of Arasappuḍaiyar, who is also stated to
have endowed lands for the rivival of worship therein, at the request of Aṇṇap-
paṅgār son of Ugappaṇaṅgaḷ. The gift is said to have been made by the
donor on the banks of Pampāsāgara in the presence of God Viṭhalēśvara (at
Vijayanagara). It is also stated that the original tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam lands which
had not been properly utilised (by the donee) were now madeover along with a
house site to a newly appointed priest by name Tiruvēṅkaṭa-Tātar (Dāsar) on
an annual rental of 30 paṇam, by the ūravar of the village with the condition
that he was also to look after the management of the tirunāmattukkāṇī lands
newly endowed. Among the boundaries is mentioned an arhat temple.

edition.l:
irattākṣi varuṣam tai mātam 15- tiyati śrīmatu paṇṭ[āram] aracappuṭaiyar
makaṉār opuḷanā[ya*]kkaṉ paṭaiviṭu irācciyattil namakku amara-

mum naṭattik••••• naṭattumpaṭikku tiruveṅkiṭatātarai kaṟpittapaṭiyāle taṉakku
veṇ[ṭum]

kuṭiyum eṟṟuvittu veṇṭum payirum payiṟceyvittukoḷḷavumkkaṭava
ṉākavum inta tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam āka viṭṭa••••ttukku co[m] āka kaṟpitta

inscription.head:
No. 257.
(A.R. No. 257 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, PUDUPPĀḌI.
VĒDANĀRĀYAṆA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1418, Naḷa, Dhanus, śu. 10, Monday, Tiruvōṇam
probably corresponding to A.D. 1496, May 30, or June 20, if the month could
be Mithuna. If it is Dhanus it is A.D. 1496, December 15, but the weekday
and nakshatra do not tally with śu. 10. It records a gift of 2100 kuḻi of wet
and dry lands to the temple of Vēdanārāyaṇa-Perumāḷ at Pāṇḍinallūr alias
Puduppāḍi by Gōvinda-Nāyakkar, son of Vaṇḍippēṭṭai Kāmu-Nāyakkar during
his nāyakkattanam (of the district ?).

edition.l:
śubhamastu svasti śrī śrīmatu cakāttam 1418- mel cellāniṉṟa naḷa
varuṣam tanunāyiṟṟu pūṟuva-

l.ta:
pakṣattu tecimiyum comavāramum peṟṟa tiruvoṇattunāḷ nammur
pāṇṭinallūrāna putuppāṭi perumāḷ vetanārāyaṇa-

perumāḷukku nampirā•••• emperumāṉ aṭiyāṟkum vaṇṭippeṭṭai
kāmunāyakkar makaṉ kovinta nāyakkar

•••••• [nā]yaka[taṉa]ttile ivvemperumāṉukku viṭṭa nilattukku
taṉmacātaṉam paṇṇikkuṭutta nilattukku vakai

••••• taṉmattukku akitam paṇṇiṉavarkaḷ keṅkaikkaraiyil kārām
pacuvaiyum pirāmaṇaraiyum

inscription.head:
No. 258.
(A.R. No. 258 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, PUDUPPĀḌI.
VĒDANĀRĀYAṆA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is probably a Kannaḍa version of No. 256 above. It is much
damaged and hence the gift portion is not clear except that a land was granted
to the temple as mānya for the requirements of worship to the deity. Mention
is made of a person by name Tiruvēṅgaḍa••••

edition.p:
1 he•ru Śrī[kaṁṭha]•• la sīmeyanū ūru [māṁnyadalū]••••
[āreraḍara]•••• chatu-sīmeyanū mānyavāgi biṭeu7 māṁnyada seṭṭi-

2 [ya maga] Tiru[veṁ]gaḍa•••• ācheyāgi māḍisi•••••• manege
pū•[haṇa]•• riyanū salisi āchaṁdrārka-stāyi āgi pūje punaskāravanu

[bha]vadbhi dā[nā]tsēyōnupālanaṁ dānātsarggam-avāpnōti pālanād-Achyutaṁ
padaṁ

inscription.head:
No. 259.
(A.R. No. 259 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, PUDUPPĀḌI.
VĒDANĀRĀYAṆA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE LEFT OF ENTRANCE
INTO THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions (Miscellaneous Telugu Inscriptions).

inscription.head:
No. 260.
(A.R. No. 260 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, NARASAMAṄGALAM.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE ON HILL—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
JAT. SUNDARA-PĀṆḌYA I—7th YEAR (A.D. 1257-58).
inscription.p:
The inscription commences with introduction Samasta jagad ādhāra etc.,
which is however a short one in this case. The details of the date are given as
Vriśchika, ba. 3, Monday, Mrigaśīrsha corresponding to A.D. 1256, November 4.
The record is incomplete stopping with the mention of one Puṇḍravardhana
Nārāyaṇa-Bhaṭṭa, a nambi performing worship in the temple of Aruḷāḷa-Perumāḷ
at Tiruvattiyūr a quarter of Tēnai Perumbākkam, and of another person, a
resident of Dūśi Agaram, to whom the ūravar of Narasamaṅgalam in Māvaṇḍūr
nāḍu a division of Kāliyūr-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam are said to
have sold their village in public auction.

vamśa nirmūlana laṅkādvivaluṇṭana dvitiyarāma cōḷa kulaśai-

mahārājādhirāja paramēśvara

ceyaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇ-

inscription.head:
No. 261.
(A.R. No. 261 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, NARASAMAṄGALAM.
ON A STONE SETUP ON THE BUND OF THE LOCAL TANK.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions (Miscellaneous Telugu Inscriptions).

inscription.head:
No. 262.
(A.R. No. 262 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, NARASAMAṄGALAM.
ON THE SAME STONE.
inscription.p:
This is a Tamil version of No. 261 above. It is dated in Śaka 1560,
Bahudhānya, Vaiyāśi 4 corresponding to A.D. 1638, May 2, and records the
construction of a tank at Dūśi-Māvaṇḍūr called Chennasāgaram which was fed by
a canal from the river Pālār, by one Veṇkaṭappa-Nāyakkar son of Chennappa-
Nāyakkar, who is given a number of high-sounding panegyrics.

l.ta: tu makula nāyakarumāy tāmal ciṅkaraca nākicu[raṉu]-
meṉṟu periṭṭu pālāṟatu cārilum koṇṭu va-
kalaṅkamur nākappa mutaliyār kumāraṉ yellappa muta-

inscription.head:
No. 263.
(A.R. No. 263 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, NARASAMAṄGALAM.
ON A SECOND STONE SETUP ON THE BUND OF THE
LOCAL TANK.
inscription.p:
This is a Sanskrit inscription dated in the cyclic year Jaya (Kārthigai 11)
which would correspond to Śaka 1576 (A.D. 1654). It records a provision
made by Veṅkaṭa-Bhūpāla (evidently the donor of the Chennasāgara tank men-
tioned in No. 262 above) for the maintenance of the tank in proper condition
for irrigating the lands, by the grant of the cess collected from the owners.

inscription.head:
No. 264.
(A.R. No. 264 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, NARASAMAṄGALAM.
ON A THIRD STONE SETUP ON THE BUND OF THE TANK.
inscription.p:
This is a Sanskrit inscription engraved in Nāgari characters and is dated
in Śaka 1560, Bahudhānya corresponding to A.D. 1638-39. It gives high praise
to Veṅkaṭappa Nāyaka the donor of the Chennasāgara tank. He is said to have
been a member of the Dāmarla family, of which the earliest mentioned was one
Abba-Mahīpati. His great grand-father was Vēṅgala-Bhūpa and grand-father
Veṅkaṭa-Bhūpa. His father was Chenna-Mahēndra “the vanquisher of his foes”
and his mother was called Kṛishṇamāmbā. This Veṅkaṭa himself was a great
conqueror, and was noted for his beauty, learning, courage and liberality. He
claims to have defeated in battle the Cheñji chief of the time and to have spread
his fame in all directions. He constructed at Māvaḍūr-agrahāra a big tank
called Chennasāgara after his father, and channels to feed the same from the
river and from the tank to irrigate the lands situated in 32 villages round about
the place. He had a younger brother by name Akka-nṛipa whom he calls his
right arm and who was also known as Nava-Bhōja because of his gift of the
Muses.

l.de: śrī[ḥ*] śrīrastu || asti prakāmaṁ jagatīha jātapraśasti dāmarlakulaṁ [viśālaṁ] |
bhūpo••• tyamaniśaṁ sukṛtaṁ ca || [2*] tasmādudītastanayaḥ prajānāmadhīdha-
raścennamahīmaheṁdraḥ | jitāriṇā nā•• tāṁgagarvanirvāpaṇam yena kṛtaṁ raṇā-
āvāsya (?) yena mahatālamabhāji•• sītāṁ purā raghuvareṇa nirudhya (?) laṁkāṁ [|| 4*]
sāragarvāpahaṁ yaddātṛtvamatīva karmasurabhi•• vahaṁ vartate || [6*] saṁkhe (khye) niḥ-
saṁkhya•• turagakarighaṭāsaṁvṛtaṁ maṁḍaleṁdraṁ ceṁji kṣmāpāla mudya-
śauryaḥ | yo devarājāṁghrisarojayugma ma[ka*]raṁda bhṛṁgī bhavadaṁtaraṁgaḥ || [8*]
rāsīt | [ma]hāmahatyapi•• sulame parasya svapnepi yasya viratonya-
vinutakāṁcī puṇyakoṭī vimāna praṇati karaṇa dhanyairbhāsamāne su-
ṭeṁdro vyatānīt || [11*] kacittadativāḍavaḥ kacana kuṁbhajanmā•• kaci-
vito jayati ceṁnasāgaraḥ || [15*] śrī śālivāhanaśaka prabhuvatsarāṇāṁ ṣa-
ṣeṁ śrīcennasāgara iti prathitaṁ taṭākaṁ || [16*] dvātniṁśadgrāmasīmāsamadhi-
te ceṁnnasāgaraḥ || [18*] dakṣiṇabhuja iva [śrīmat] veṁkaṭadharaṇīpateḥ pri-
yo bhrātā | akkanṛpaḥ satkavitā navabhojo jayatu śauryakīrtinidhiḥ || [19*]
asya dharmasya yo vighna kuryādbhuvi narādhamaḥ | sagaṁtā svasya jananīṁ
nujaḥ sasutaḥ śrīmān ciraṁ jīvatu dhārmikaḥ || [23*] divākarādhvarī••••

edition.p: Note:—I am thankful to the Government Epigraphist for this revised reading of my transcript.

inscription.head:
No. 265.
(A.R. No. 265 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
RĀJAK. MUMMAḌI-CHŌḶADĒVA (RĀJARĀJA I)—4th YEAR (A.D. 988-89).
inscription.p:
This records a gift made by the king of 497 kaḻañju of gold and a gold
flower weighing 2 kaḻañju and odd to God Mahādēva at Tirumālpēṟu, when he
paid a visit to the temple to offer worship. The present is said to have been
conveyed by the officer Arumoḻi-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ of Kāraippākkam.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] kōvirāja[ kēsari] panmarāṉa śrīmummaṭicoḻadēvar tamakku
yāṇṭu 4- ā••••••

oṉṟu kuṭiñai-

inscription.head:
No. 266.
(A.R. No. 266 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PĀRTHIVĒNDRAVARMAN “who took the head of Vīra-Pāṇḍya”—13th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 25 kaḻañju of gold deposited with the sabhā of
Paṭṭālam alias Eḻunūṟṟuva-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, for burning two perpetual lamps
in the temple of Tirumālpēṟṟu-Mahādēvar, one on behalf of (queen ?) Vijjavai-
Mahādēviyār daughter of Vallamanār (Pallava) Nandipanma-Kāḍupaṭṭigaḷ, and
the other on behalf of a retainer of the latter. The shepherds appointed to make
the daily supply of ghee were to receive a portion of the sacred offered food
from the temple.

edition.l: ttu sabhaiyom ()-

inscription.head:
No. 267.
(A.R. No. 267 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PĀRTHIVĒNDRĀDHIPATIVARMAN—[3]rd YEAR.
inscription.p:
Both the impression and the transcript of this inscription are wanting.
It is stated against this number in the Annual Report for 1907, that it is
engraved in Tamil and Grantha and is damaged and that it records the building
of the temple and the enclosing verandah by the Virāṭa king Anayamaṉ alias
Paramaṇḍalāditya.

inscription.head:
No. 268.
(A.R. No. 268 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN—6th YEAR.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions Vol. XIII, No. 132.

inscription.head:
No. 269.
(A.R. No. 269 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJA I (?)
inscription.p:
The inscription is badly damaged in the first ten lines. It records a gift
of 12(1/2) kaḻañju of gold for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tirumāl-
pēṟuḍaiya-Mahādēva by Kaṇṇandai Kuṭṭattāḻi of Vaḍagarai in Śembūr-kōṭṭam,
who is said to have deposited the amount with the Ūrār of Nelvāyil in Valla-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 270.
(A.R. No. 270 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA (I ?)—27th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This registers the sale of 1000 kuḻi of temple land tax-free for 4 kāśu to
a certain Kāḷaiyan Dēvagaṇātaṉ alias Adhirājēndra-Tamiḻa-Viḻupparaiyaṉ of
Samangili in Kāyirampēḍu-nāḍu a division of Kāliyūr-kōṭṭam for endowment to
a maṭha in the precincts of the temple. The land is said to have been situated
in the dēvadāna village Śiṟṟiyāṟṟūr in Puriśai-nāḍu a division of Maṇaiyiṟ-kōṭṭam
and had lapsed to the temple as the land holder had been a defaulter in the
payment of the iṟai (tax) thereon.

śrīkulottuṅka coḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu 27- vatu jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻa
maṇṭalattut tāmaṟkoṭṭattu [val]-

inscription.head:
No. 271.
(A.R. No. 271 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA I—46th YEAR
(A.D. 1115-16)
inscription.p:
This begins with introduction Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga etc. It records an endow-
ment of 60 kāśu made by Madhurāntakaṉ Ponnambalakkūttar alias Kāliṅgarājar
the headman of Arumbākkam residing at Maṇavil in Maṇavil-nāḍu a sub-
division of Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam for burning 10 perpetual lamps in the temple at
Tirumālpēru in Valla-nāḍu a division of Dāmaṟkōṭṭam in Jayangoṇḍaśōḻa
maṇḍalam. The Nagarattār of Pandi alias Lōkamādēvipuram in the same
kōṭṭam who received the amount, agreed to measure out as interest thereon,
75 nāḻi of oil every month to the Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple. The record is
attested at the end by 19 signatories including the Nagarakkaṇakku.

inscription.head:
No. 272.
(A.R. No. 272 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MANIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
VIKRAMA-CHŌḶA—7th YEAR (A.D. 1124-25)
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with introduction Pūmādu puṇara etc. It records a
sale of 6000 kuḻi out of their common land to the temple of Tirumārpēṟuḍaiyār,
by the Ūrār of Śiṟṟiyāṟṟūr a dēvadāna village in Maṇaviṟkōṭṭam, for 20 kāśu
received by them inclusive of tax-amount (on behalf of the temple) from
Uttamaśōḻaṉ Ponnambalakkūttaṉ alias Kaṇṇagadaraiyar of Kīḻmāndūr in Purak-
kiḷiyūr-nāḍu, a division of Pāṇḍikulāśani-vaḷanāḍu in Śōḻamaṇḍalam. Half of this
land is said to be in favour of the gods Uttama-Chōḷīśvaramuḍaiyār and Avi-
muttīśvaramuḍaiyār in the temple. The land was to be utilised partly as areca-
nut garden and flower garden and partly as wet land for cultivation to meet the
requirements of worship in the temple. It was let on lease by the temple to one
Taḻuvakkuḻaiñja-Pichchar of the Tiruvēgambamuḍaiyār temple (at Kāñchīpuram ?).
The donor Kaṇṇagadaraiyar is stated to have made a further gift of about
138 kaḻañju of gold for ornaments to the god (in the main shrine) and silver
etc. for some articles of worship.

inscription.head:
No. 273.
(A.R. No. 273 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. VĪRARĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA—6th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 32 cows and a bull for the daily supply of an
uḻakku of ghee for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tirumāṟpēṟuḍaiyār,
made by Tiruvaraṅgamuḍaiyāṉ Danmaparipālaṉ alias Rājadhirāja-Malaiyarāyaṉ
son of Malaitāṅgi Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-Malaiyarāyaṉ Munaiyadaraiyan of Tiruneḍum-
piṟai in Naḍuvilmalai Perumūr-nāḍu a division of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
ṟku yāṇṭu āṟāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu naṭuvinmalaip
perumur nāṭṭut tiruneṭumpiṟaiyil malaitāṅki kulottuṅkacoḻamalaiyarāyan muṉai
yataraiyan makan tiruvaraṅkamuṭaiyāṉāṉa tanmaparipālaṉāna irājā

rāja malaiyarāyan
immaṇṭalattut tāmaṟkoṭṭattu vallanāṭṭu āḷuṭaiyār

tirumāṟ peṟuṭaiyāṟku vaitta tirunantāviḷakku oṉṟukku nāḷ 1- kku
irājakecariyāl ney uḻakkāka munnūṟṟaṟupattaiñcukku ney toṇṇūṟṟoru

maviyāṉa ittanmam cantirātittavarai celvatāka vaittatu itu[k*]ku rakṣai
panmāhēśvarar .

inscription.head:
No. 274.
(A.R. No. 274 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA I—38th YEAR
(A.D. 1107-08)
inscription.p:
This has the introduction Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga etc. after a few expressions
of Vīramē tuṇai introduction of Vīrarājēndra. It records a gift of 30 cows for
burning a perpetual lamp in the temple at Tirumālpēṟu in Valla-nāḍu a division
of Dāmaṟ-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, by Araiyaṉ Vīraśōḻaṉ alias
Vīramārttāṇḍa-Viḻupparaiyaṉ of Eḻumuḷḷūr in Tiruvaḻundūr-nāḍu, a division of
Rājanārāyaṇa-vaḷanāḍu in Śōḻamaṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 275.
(A.R. No. 275 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. RĀJARĀJADĒVA—15th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 64 cows and 2 bulls for burning two perpetual
lamps in the temple by Araiyaṉ Nīṟaṇindāṉ Śēdirāyaṉ of Panaṅguḍi a “follower”
of (the chief) Śīyaṉ Śambuvarāyar. The donor is said to have also made further
gifts to the god, of two gold ornaments, a silver plate and two lamp-stands.

inscription.head:
No. 276.
(A.R. No. 276 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. RĀJARĀJADĒVA—8th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This is an incomplete inscription recording a gift of cows for “half” a
perpetual lamp in the temple by one Śelvappiḷḷai alias Śīyagaṅ[gaṉ] of Kūṟṟaṅguḍi
(a quarter) of Panaippākkam in Uvaṟūr-nāḍu, who is called a Durai-araiśar-
mudali of Kuṇḍalūr.

edition.l:
ṭu 8- vatu mārkaḻi mātattu uṭaiyār tirumāṟpeṟuṭaiyārkku kuṇṭalūr
turai araicar muta-

yāṉa ciyakaṅ••••

inscription.head:
No. 277.
(A.R. No. 277 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBHUVANAVĪRADĒVA (KULŌTTUṄGA III)—34th YEAR (A.D. 1211-12).
inscription.p:
This records the royal order issued at the request of Śēdirāyaṉ, granting
96 vēli of land tax-free at Vadiyūr for the requirements of worship of god
Uttama-Chōḷīśvaram-uḍaiyār consecrated by the chief in the temple of Tirumār-
pēṟuḍaiyār. The signatories to this order are 8 officers of the king including the
Tirumandiravōlai (Secretary) Rājanārāyaṇa-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ and Nīlagaṅgaraiyaṉ.

ri[ṉ]mai k[o]-

inscription.head:
No. 278.
(A.R. No. 278 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN “who destroyed the ships at Kāndaḷūr-Śālai”—
RĀJARĀJA I—Date lost.
inscription.p:
The first half of the inscription is badly damaged. This seems to record
an agreement given by the Ūrār of Paḷgūr to measure out annually in the temple.
93 kāḍi and odd of paddy (as interest) for the 25 kaḻañju of gold which they
had received from some donor whose name is lost.

l.ta: m keṅkaiyiṭaik kumariyiṭai naṭuvu[ṭceytār] ceyta pāvam

inscription.head:
No. 279.
(A.R. No. 279 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN “who destroyed the ships at Kāndaḷūr-Śālai”—
RĀJARĀJA I—12th YEAR (A.D. 996-97).
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 96 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple by a
certain Kaṇṇandai Kuṭṭattāḻi Maṟumakka[mpāri] of Vaḍagarai in Śembūrkōṭṭam.

edition.l: panmaṟku yāṇṭu 12- [ā]-

inscription.head:
No. 280.
(A.R. No. 280 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARAKĒSARIVARMAN—14th YEAR.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions Vol. XIX, No. 341.

inscription.head:
No. 281.
(A.R. No. 281 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN “who destroyed the ships at Kāndaḷur-Śālai”—
RĀJARĀJA I—12th YEAR (A.D. 996-97).
inscription.p:
This records an agreement given by the sabhā of Śiṟṟūr alias Pṛithivimādēvi-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam (a part) of Tiruvālaṅgāḍu in Paṅgaḷa-nāḍu a division of
Maṇaiyiṟkōṭṭam promising to measure out 225 kāḍi of paddy in the temple in
the month of Paṅguni every year (as interest) for the 60 kaḻañju of gold received
by them from the temple at Tirumālpēṟu.

edition.l: yāṇṭu 12- ā-

l.ta: l tuṇainiṟai aṟupatiṉ kaḻañcu ippoṉ a-

m cuṅkamum nāṅkaḷe paṭṭum ūrakattu niṉṟār nārā-

m emmur ūrāṇmai ceyvārey iṉṉellu irunū-

edition.p: The rest is too much damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 282.
(A.R. No. 282 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
RĀJARĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN (RĀJARĀJA I) who destroyed the ships at Kāndaḷūr-Śālai—
12th YEAR (A.D. 996-97).
inscription.p:
This records a similar agreement given by the Ūrār of Paḷugūr in Paḷugūr-
nāḍu that they would pay, by way of interest on 60 kaḻañju of gold received by
them from the temple at Tirumālpēṟu, 225 kāḍi of paddy to be measured out in
the temple in the month of Paṅguṇi every year. Reference is made to a fine of
100 kaḻañju realised by the temple as a result of the inspection of the temple
management made by Madhurāntakaṇ Gaṇḍarādittaṉār in the 4th year of the
king, and out of which 60 kaḻañju had been invested with the sabhā of Śiṟṟūr
as recorded in No. 281 above. Some transaction of the 14th year of a king
(probably Uttama-Chōḷa) is also recorded at the end but the details are not clear.

edition.l: ma[ṟ*]kku yāṇṭu 12-

ḻutiyum ce[l]lum nikkit tūyavākkip pākkūliyum (vāṭākūliyum ?) cu-

inscription.head:
No. 283.
(A.R. No. 283 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJAK. MUMMAḌI-CHŌḶADĒVA (RĀJARĀJA I)—4th YEAR (A.D. 988-89)
inscription.p:
The inscription states that on an enquiry made by Madhurāntakaṉ
Gaṇḍarādittaṉār along with the officers Villavaṉ-Mūvēndavēḷār, Rājamārttāṇḍa-
Mūvēndavēḷār and four others into the affairs of the temple at Tirumālpēṟu, it
was found that the property of god Agnīśvara was misappropriated by the staff
of the temple for their own use, the daily worship to the deity being confined
to the bare offering of rice. The concerned offenders were punished with fines
and the six pieces of land measuring together 2(1/2) vēli were ordered to be resumed
and the taxes thereon being paid to the Kāmakkōṭṭam (Amman shrine) in the
temple. One mā of land at Kuḷattūr and 225 kuḻi at Śiṟṟiyāṟṟūr were also added
to the main temple (See No. 282 above).

edition.l: varkku yāṇṭu 4-

l.ta: ṭaiya tiṇṭi piṭārarum atikārikaḷ villavaṉ muven-
ḷum nivantamuṅ keṭ[ṭu muta]l ikkoyilil tirukkoyiluṭaiy[ār]
nivantaṅkaḷ ceyyāte muṭṭaviṭṭu irunāḻi ariciyāl avikkā[ṭṭiṭṭu]
ittevar nilaṅkaḷ ellām tāṅkaḷe yāṇṭu cellāniṟka i-
varkaḷait taṇṭaṅ koṇṭu iṉṉāḷmutal ivarkaḷ tāṅkaḷey
māṟpeṟṟu (ce) ceṉṉaṟpaḷḷam nilam oru paṭṭiyum ciṅ-
kaṭikuṟai nilam araiye oru māvaraiyum koṟṟattai viḷākam
nilam mukkāle muṉṟumāvum kuḻicceṟu nilam oru paṭṭiyum nel-
ṇiyaṉceṟu nilaṉ oṉṟey kālum āka ivvūr nilaṉ
iraṇṭaraiyālum kāmakkoṭṭattukku iṟai iṟuttu mikka poka-

yam ciṟṟiyāṟṟūr muṉṟu taṭiyāy avvūrkkolāl irunūṟṟu-

inscription.head:
No. 284.
(A.R. No. 284 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN (RĀJARĀJA I) “who destroyed the ships at Kāndaḷūr-Śālai”—
11th YEAR (A.D. 995-96).
inscription.p:
The inscription is left incomplete towards the end. It records the con-
secration of an image of goddess Umā-Bhaṭṭārakiyār Ādisundaradēvadēviyār in
the temple at Tirumālpēṟu by a certain Nimaḷa (Nirmala)-Śivar alias Nāṟpat-
teṇṇāyira-Piḍārar, the head of a maṭha at Tiruppālūr near Takkōlam in Maṇaiyiṟ-
kōṭṭam, and the endowment of a land tax free for the worship of the goddess
after purchasing the same from some Veḷḷāla brothers resident at the place.

edition.l: nma[ṟ*]kku yāṇṭu 11- ā-

inscription.head:
No. 285.
(A.R. No. 285 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN—3rd YEAR.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. XIII, No. 29.

inscription.head:
No. 286.
(A.R. No. 286 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
KŌNĒRINMAIKOṆḌĀN—14th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This is evidently an inscription of Uttama-Chōḷa. It states that while the
king was staying in his palace at Kachchippēḍu (Kāñchīpuram), it was represented
to him through (the officer) Śōḻa-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ that the village Śiṟṟiyāṟṟūr in
Maṇaiyil-nāḍu a division of Maṇaiyil-kōṭṭam had been granted, in the 21st year
of Toṇḍaimānāṟṟūr-tuñjina-Uḍaiyār (Āditya I), as dēvadāna-brahmadēya to the
Sabhā of Puduppākkam in Puriśai-nāḍu in the same kōṭṭam with the stipulation
that they were to pay as dēvadāna-iṟaiyili to the temple of Tirumālpēṟuḍaiyār
3000 kāḍi (of paddy) as puravu and 561 kāḍi as iravu in addition to 200(1/2) kaḻañju
and a mañjāḍi. This order had been confirmed in the 4th year of Parakēsari-
varman “who took Madurai and Īḻam” viz., Parāntaka I, excluding from its
purview the lands in the village which had belonged to one Śaṅgappāḍi-kiḻāṉ,
for which the owner was to pay independently 3000 kāḍi to the temple. This
practice was in force till the 36th year of the king (Parāntaka I), when the
Sabhā of Puduppākkam expropriated the lands of Saṅgappāḍi-kiḻāṉ and dis-
continued the payment he was making. The temple authorities therefore now
appealed to the reigning king against this injustice. The latter thereupon sent
for the complainants and the Sabhā of Puduppākkam, and after due enquiry
into the matter passed orders to the Sabhā to restore the payment in future to
the temple, of the 3000 kāḍi also due on Saṅgappāḍi-kiḻān's land in addition
to their own original dues of the puravu and iravu (rents) in paddy, and the
cash payment they had been making. It was also ordered that this was to be
entered in the Tax-Registers, and the order was issued to the temple authorities
by the king's officers including Parakēsari-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ and Śembiyaṉ Uttara-
mantri.

inscription.head:
No. 287.
(A.R. No. 287 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA I—41st YEAR (A.D. 1110-11)
inscription.p:
The inscription has the introduction Pugaḻ śūḻnda puṇari etc. It registers
a sale of 6700 kuḻi of land known as Moṇḍukoḷḷi as ūrkīḻ iṟaiyili in addition to
another plot of waste land as kuḍiyiruppu (house site) by the Ūrār (comprising
16 members) of Nittavinōdanallūr in Dāmar-nāḍu a division of Dāmar-kōṭṭam
in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam for 20 kāśu to one Kūttāḍi Śīruḍaikkaḻal alias
Śōḻappallavaraiyaṉ of Nāgambāḍi in Maḻa-nāḍu a division of Virudarāja-
bhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu in Śōḻamaṇḍalam for the maintenance of a maṭha established
by him in the tirumaḍaiviḷāgam of the temple at Tirumālpēṟu in Valla-nāḍu under
the name Araśaraṇālayaṉ under the management of a tapasvin called Anbarkk-
araiśāṇḍār. The Ūrār made themselves responsible for the payment of all the
kuḍimai dues on the land such as peruvari, śilvari etc., and for the supply of
water for the irrigation of the land free of cost.

inscription.head:
No. 288.
(A.R. No. 288 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN (RĀJARĀJA I) “who destroyed the ships at Kāndalūr Śālai”
12th YEAR (A.D. 996-97)
inscription.p:
This inscription is damaged. It registers an undertaking given by the
Ūrār of Maṇavil in Maṇavil-nāḍu a division of Manavil-kōṭṭam to the temple of
Tirumālpēruḍaiyār in Valla-nāḍu a division of Dāmaṟkōṭṭam, agreeing to measure
out to the temple every year 225 kāḍi and odd of paddy as interest on 60 kaḻañju
and odd of gold invested with them by the temple.

edition.l: ṭu 12- ā[va]

l.ta: ṉ aṟupatiṉ ka[ḻa]ñce iraṇṭu ma[ñ]cāṭiyum piḷavu-
cāṭiyum piḷa•• kikkum eṟṟi[ṉa] nellu iru-

••••••••• māṉom ippāvirupparum iṉṉellu irunūṟṟirupat
taiṅkāṭi kuṭṭai aññāḻi uriyum āṭṭāṇṭutoṟum maṟavaṭṭuvomāṉom
muṟco-

••••••••• nāṭṭu maṇavillūrom ūrār colla veḻutiṉeṉ
ivūrp peruntirukkoyiluṭaiya tiruvuṇṇāḻikaiyuṭaiya tiruveṅkacivaṉ makaṉ
kāmaci[vaṉ ||*]

inscription.head:
No. 289.
(A.R. No. 289 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJA I—Date lost.
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It seems to record some provision made for the daily
supply of an āḻākku of oil (for burning of a lamp) in the temple at Tirumālpēṟu.

l.ta:
•••• koṇṭu cantirātittaval nicatam āḻākku neyyeṇṇai tirumāl
•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 290.
(A.R. No. 290 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA I—26th YEAR (A.D. 1095-96)
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with introduction Pugaḻśūḻnda puṇari etc. It registers
an agreement given by the Ūrār of the dēvadāna village Tirumālpēṟu in Valla-
nāḍu a division of Dāmaṟkōṭṭam to one Nambaṉ Madhurāntakadēvaṉ alias
Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-Viraināṭṭu-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ of Paṭṭikuḍi in Viraikkūṟṟam a division
of Madhurāntaka-Vaḷanāḍu on the northern bank of the river, to pay, in con-
sideration of a lumpsum received from him, all the kaḍamai taxes such as nelvari,
poṉvari, antarāyam, siṟuśuṅgam, and uppukkāśu and the kuḍimai dues like
echchōṟu, kūṟṟunel and veṭṭimuṭṭaiyāḷ due on some pieces of land measuring
1600 kuḻi in all endowed by him to the temple after purchasing the same from
several individuals.

inscription.head:
No. 290A.
(A.R. No. 290-A of 1906)
inscription.p:
This is engraved in continuation of No. 290 above, and is dated in the
27th year of the same king. It registers a tax-free sale of 1969 kuḻi and odd of
land situated in Śiṟṟiyāṟṟūr in Puriśai-nāḍu a division of Maṇaiyiṟ-kōṭṭam which
is said to be a dēvadāna of the temple at Tirumālpēṟu by the temple authorities
for a consideration of 10 kāśu received by them from the same donor mentioned
above, who endowed it back to the temple for the formation of a flower-garden
in his name.

l.ta:
ittevarkki yāṇṭirupatteḻāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattut
tāmaṟkoṭṭattu vallanā•••••

turāntakavaḷanāṭṭu viṟaikkūṟṟattup paṭṭikuṭiyil paṭṭikuṭaiyā[ṉ] tumpan
maturā

ventaveḷāṉ uṭaiyār tirumālppeṟuṭaiyāṟkuc ceyvitta maturāntaka
tevan tirunantavanappuṟamāka nilavi[lai*] koṇṭu yiṟaiiḻicciṉa mukkā[ṇi]
nāṭṭo[m*] paṇ-

ṭārattoṭukkina kācu pattu ikkācu pattum nilavilaiyu miṟaivilaiyumākak
koṇṭu viṟṟa nilamāvatu ittevar tevatānam maṇaiyiṟko-

ravatikkuk kiḻakku
iraṇṭāṅkaṇṇāṟṟu mātaricaṭṭan ettamum kā[ḷi] mukkayanum kaḷ[ḷi]viḷā-

kamum tenmelaikkuṇṭilikaḷumākak kuḻi āyirattut toḷāyirattu aṟupat
tonpate yiraṇṭumāvum tiruna

[mu]m nilavilaiyu miṟaivilaiyumākak koṇṭu iṟaiyiliyāka vilaiāvaṇañ
ceytukuṭutten āti
tamuccayanen ivai en neḻuttu ippaṭi viṟṟu vilaiyāvaṇa-
ñ ceytu kuṭutten ko[pparai]yan ceṭṭi vāḻava
ḷān kaṇavati eḻuttu ivai kopparaiya neḻuttu ippaṭi

edition.l: viṟṟu vilaiyāvaṇañ ceytu kuṭutten ponnuḻān [ dēvan] kuṭi-

l.ta:
ṟṟu vilaiyāvaṇañ c[e*]ytu kuṭutteṉ nocci kiḻān [oṟṟi] ekāmpara
nāna tennavan cikā-

inscription.head:
No. 291.
(A.R. No. 291 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH, WEST AND SOUTH WALLS OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE.
RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA I—21st YEAR (A.D. 1032-33)
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the introduction Tirumaṇṇi Vaḷara etc. It is
incomplete towards the end. It states that the Sabhā of Puduppākkam in Puriśai-
nāḍu a division of Manaiyil-kōṭṭam had sold as tax free dēvadāna, 2750 kuḻi of
land to the temple of Tirumālpēruḍaiyār in Valla nāḍu a division of Dāmarkōṭṭam
for 30 kaḻañju (of gold), but the sale had been in abeyance without being
executed till the 21st year of the king. In this year Nīḍūr Karumāṇikkam a lady
attached to the royal household donated 10 kaḻañju and 8 mañjāḍi to the temple
for which, after making a fore-head plate for the god, 1000 kuḻi of the land was
set apart for burning a perpetual lamp with the produce thereon. For another
10 kaḻañju and 9 mañjāḍi donated by one Pugaḻaṟai of Marudattūr in Tiruvāli-
nāḍu a division of Rājēndrasiṁha-vaḷanāḍu in Śōḻamaṇḍalam, 1000 more kuḻi
of the same land was assigned for a second perpetual lamp to be burnt in front
of the god after making a similar tiruppaṭṭam (fore-head plate) like the one
mentioned above for the deity.

l.ta:
tuṭarvana velippaṭar va[ṉavāciyum] cuḷḷiccūḻ matiṭ koḷ[ḷi]ppākkaiyu
naṇṇaṟkarumuraṇ maṇṇaikkaṭakkamum porukaṭaliḻat taraiyartamuṭiyum āṅkavar
teviyaroṅkeḻiṉ mu[ṭiyu mun]navarpakkaṟ [te]nnavar vaitta cu

ṇṭirai īḻamaṇṭalamuḻuvatu meṟipaṭai keraḷaṉ muṟaimaiyiṟ cūṭuṅ kula
taṉamākiya palarpukaḻ muṭiyuñ ceṅkatir mālaiyum caṅkatir velait tol peruṅkāvaṟp
palapaḻa

vum ceruviṟciṉavi irupattoru kālaraicukaḷaik kaṭṭa paracurāmaṉ cākara
tivaraṇ karuti yiruttiya cempoṟ ṟiruttakumuṭiyum payaṅkoṭu paḻimika muyaṅkiyil
[mutukiṭ]ṭoḷitta [caya]ciṅkaṉ perum pukaḻoṭu piṭiya ilaṭṭapāṭi eḻarai ilakkamum
[navaṉeti]k kulapperumalaika[ḷu]m vikkiramavirac cakkarakkoṭṭamum [mutirpaṭava]llai
matirama[ṇṭala]muṅ kā[miṭai vaḷai] nāmaṇai koṇaiyum veñcilaivirar pañca[ppaḷḷi]
yum pācaṭai paḻaṉ mācuṇi tecamum ayarvil vaṇkirtti yā[tina]karavaiyil ca

rarataṉai viḷaiyamarkaḷattu kiḷaiyoṭum piṭiccu palataṉattoṭu niṟaikula
taṉakkuvaiyum kiṭṭaruñ ceṟimu[ḷai] oṭṭa viṣaiyamum pūcurarceṟ naṟkocalai nāṭum

taṉmapālaṉai vemmuṉaiyaḻiccu vaṇṭuṟai[colai*] taṇṭaputtiyum iraṇacūraṉai
muraṇuṟat tākki tikkaṉaikkirtti takkaṇalāṭamum kovi

ṉ māviḻintoṭa
taṅkāta cāral vaṅkāḷa tecamum toṭukaṭal caṅkoṭṭal mayipālaṉai veñcamar
viḷākat tañcuvitta[ru]-

edition.l:
ḷi oṇṭiṟalāṉaiyum peṇṭirpaṇṭāramum nittil neṭuṅkaṭaluttaralāṭamum
[v]eṟimalar tirttat teṟipuṉal kaṅkaiyum alaikaṭal naṭuvuḷ palakalañ celutti
ca[ṅki]rāmavijayatuṅkapaṉmaṉākiya kiṭārattaraiyaṉai vākayam porukaṭal kumpak
kariyoṭu makappaṭutturimaiyil piṟakkiya peruneti piṟakkamum ārtta[va]ṉ aṉṉakar
porttoḻi[l*] vācalil viccātiratoraṇamum moyttoḻil puṉamaṇi putavamum
kaṉamaṇi katavamum niṟai śrī vijaiyamum tuṟainirpaṉṉaiyum paṉmalaiyuṟaiyil toṉ
malaiyūrum āḻkaṭalakaḻ cūḻ māyiruṭi[ṅkamum*] [ka]laṅkā valviṉai ilaṅkā cokamum
kāppuṟu niṟaipuṉal māpa[ppāḷamum] kāvalampuricai mevili [pa]ṅkamum viḷaippantūṟuṭai
vaḷaippantūṟum kalaittakkor pukaḻ talaittakkolamum [ti]tamāvalviṉai mātamaliṅkamum
kalāmutir kaṭuntiṟal ilāmuritecamum teṉkaṭalārva māṉakkavāramum toṭukaṭal
kāval kaṭumuraḷkaṭāramum [mā]peruntaṇṭāṟ koṇṭa koparakesa[ri] panmarāna
uṭaiyār śrī rājentracoḷadēvar[k*]ku yā[ ṇṭu 21- āvatu] jeyaṅkoṇṭacoḻa
maṇṭalattu tāmarkkoṭṭattu vallanāṭṭu tirumālpeṟṟu tirumālpeṟuṭaiyār
ātidāsacaṇḍēśvararpakkal maṇaiyil ko[ṭ*]ṭattu puricaināṭṭu putupākkattu sabhai
yom taṉ••••cāy [koṇṭa poṉ muppati]ṉ kaḻañcum koṇṭu iṟaiyili
dēvatāṉamāka viṟṟukkuṭutta nilattukku kiḻpār[k*]kellai piṭāriyār nilattukku
meṟkku teṉpāṟkkellai ko[llai]viḻunta nilamākavum melpārkke[l*]lai
pakavatipaṭṭikkum koyilāṉ nilattukkum kiḻakkum vaṭapār[k*]kellai nampikāl-

kkaraikku teṟkum iṉāṉke[l*]laiyu[ḷ*]ḷum akappaṭṭa nilam uṇṇilam
moḻiviṉṟi iraṇṭāyirattu eḻuṉūṟṟu aimpatu kuḻiyum iṟaiyiliyāka viṟṟu
cilālekai ce[y*]tu kuṭuttom sabhaiyom paṇiyāl i[v]vūr madhyasta[ṉ*]
kecavaṉ mālirumcolai eḻuttu eṉṟu viṟṟuttan[ta*] nilattukku yāṇṭu irupa[t*]
toṉṟāvatu varaiyum nivantam ce[y*]yātu kiṭantamaiyil uṭaiyār śrīrājentra
coḻatevaṉ periyaveḷattu peṇṭāṭṭi [ṉiṭur] karumāṇikkam tirunontā
viḷakku oṉṟu cantirātityaval erikkavaitta poṉ kācukallāl tuḷaipoṉ poṉ
patiṉkaḻañce eṭṭu mañcāṭiyum koṇṭu tiruppaṭṭam oṉṟu ceytu cātti ittiru
nontāviḷakku oṉṟukku pākkattu [vi]laikoṇṭu kiṭanta nilattil tirunontā
viḷakku 1- [o*]ṉṟukku āyiraṅkuḻi caṇḍēśvaravilaiyāka inta āyiraṅkuḻiyilu[m*] vanta
svāmipokaṅkoṇṭu cantirātityaval oru tirunontāviḷakkerippatākavum iti
noṭum coḻamaṇṭalattu vaṭakarai rājentrasiṅhavaḷanāṭṭu tiru[vāli]nāṭṭu
marutattūr marutattūruṭaiyāṉ pukaḻaṟai ampalattil tirumālpeṟuṭaiyārkku
valappuṟam etirniṉṟeriya cantrādityaval viḷakkoṉṟiṉukku vaicca poṉ kācu
kallāl tuḷainiṟai patiṉkaḻañce oṉpatu mañcāṭiyum ko[ṇ*]ṭu irājentracoḻa
ṉeṉṟu tiruppaṭṭam cātti ittirunontā viḷakkoṉṟukku viṭṭa [nilamāvatu putup]
pākkattu sabhai[yom] vilaikoṇṭu kiṭanta nilattil [niṭu]r karumāṇikkattiṉ
viḷakkāl niṅkum nilak kuḻi āyiram nikki niṉṟa nilattil ivaṉukku kuḻi āyiram
inni[lam] āyirattilum vanta svāmipokaṅ koṇṭu ivviḷakkerippatākavum it
tirunontāviḷakku iraṇ Stops here.

inscription.head:
No. 291A.
(A.R. No. 291-A of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA I—21st YEAR (A.D. 1032-33)
inscription.p:
This has also the same introduction Tirumaṉṉi vaḷara etc. The latter
portion of the record is very much damaged. It seems to register a sale of two
villages as śālābhōga to the temple at Tirumālpēṟu in Valla-nāḍu a division of
Dāmaṟkōṭṭam by the Peruṅguṟi-sabhā of Nittavinōda-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in
Menmalai [Mēlūr-kūṟṟam] in Jayangoṅḍaśōḻamaṇḍalam in consideration of
1300 kāśu which seems to have been received by them from Kōvaṉ Kumaraṉ
alias Daṇḍanāyakan Madhurāntakavēḷāṉ, an officer of the royal treasury who was
a native of Marudāḍu alias Vikramaśōḻanallūr in Veṇkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam. Some dis-
connected pieces of writing are also found in the same place, which seem to
have formed part of this inscription.

l.ta:
aiyum po[ṟce]yapp[āv]ai
yum cirttaniṟ celviyum taṉ peru

viyarāki yiṉpu[ṟa] neṭutuyar ruḻiyuḷ ḷiṭai
tuṟaināṭu-

tuṭarvaṉavelipaṭarvaṉavāciyum cuḷḷicūḻ [matiḷ ko]ḷḷippākkaiyum
na[ṇ*]ṇa[ṟ*]kkarumuraṇ [maṇ]ṇaikkaṭakkamum porukaṭaliḻattaraiya[r tamu]ṭiyum
māṅkavaṉ

ṭai keraḷaṉ muṟaimaiyiṟ [cūṭum kulataṉam]ākiya palarpukaḻ muṭiyum
ceṅkatir mālai [caṅ]katir velai [to]-

vu[*m] ceru[viṟciṉa]vil irupatto[rukā
laraicukaḷai] kaṭṭa paracurāmaṉ me-

maṟṟivaraṇ karuti iruttiya cempoṟ ṟiruttakumuṭiyum payaṅ
koṭu paḻimika muyaṅkiya[l*] mutukiṭ-

ṭoḷitta jayaciṅka ṉaḷapperum pukaḻoṭum piṭiya liraṭṭapāṭi eḻarai
ilakkamum navanetikkulap perumalaikaḷum vikkiramavirac cakkarakoṭṭa-

mu mutirpaṭavallai maturamaṇṭalamum kāmiṭai vaḷaiya nāmaṇaikkoṇaiyum
veñciṉavirap pañcappaḷḷiyum pācaṭai paḻaṉ mācuṇitecamum [ayarvil vaṇ]
ki[r*]tti yāti nakaravaiyuṭ cantiraṉ ṟolkulat ti[n]tira irataṉaiy viḷaiyamark
kaḷattuk kiḷaiyo-

ṭum piṭittup palataṉattoṭu niṟaikulataṉakkuvaiyuṅ kiṭṭaruñ ceṟimiṉaḷai
yoṭṭaviṣaiyamum pūcurarcer nalkkocalaināṭum taṉmapālaṉai vemmuṉaiyaḻittu
vaṇṭuṟaicolait taṇṭaputtiyum iraṇacūraṉai muraṇuṟattākki tikkaṇai

ṉ māviḻin toṭat taṅkāta
cāral vaṅkāḷatecamum toṭukaṭaṟ caṅkoṭṭal mayipāḷaṉai veñcamarviḷākat
tañcuvittaruḷi yoṇṭiṟal yāṉaiyum peṇṭir paṇṭāramum nittila neṭu-

ṅkaṭa luttiralāṭamum veṟimalarttirtta teṟipuṉaṟk kaṅkaiyum alaikaṭal
naṭuvuṭ palakalañ celutti caṅkirāmavicaiyoṟṟuṅka paṉmaṉākiya kaṭārattaraiyaṉai
vākayam porukaṭalk kumpak kariyoṭu makap-

paṭutturimaiyiṟp piṟakkiya perunitip piṟakkamum ārttavar nakarp port
toḻil vācalil viccātirat toraṇamum moyttoḷi[r*] puṉamaṇipputavamum
kaṉamaṇikkatavamum niṟai

śrī vicaiyamum tuṟainirppaṉṉaiyum paṉmalaiyūraiyiṟ ṟoṉmalaiyūru neṭu
kaṭalakaḻcūḻ māyiruṭiṅkamum kalaṅkāvalviṉai ilaṅkā cokamum kāppuṟuniṟaipuṉal
māppappāḷamum kāva-

tūṟuṭai vaḷappantūrum kalaittakkor
pukaḻ talaittakkolamum tiytamāval(la)viṉai mātamaliṅkamum kalāmuti[r*] kaṭun
tiṟa[li*]lāmuṟitecamum teṉakkavārpoḻil māṉakkavāramum toṭuka-

ṭaṟkāvaṟ kaṭumuraṭkaṭāramum māpporu taṇṭāṟ koṇṭa kopparakecari
paṉmarāṉa uṭaiyār śrī irāje

- āvatu jayaṅkoṇṭa
coḻamaṇṭalattu melmalai[ymelūr kūṟṟattu] nittavinotacatur

yom nilavilaiyāvaṇak kaiyyeḻuttu [veṇ
ku]ṉṟakkoṭṭattu marutāṭunāṭṭu marutāṭāṉa vikkiramacoḻanallūruṭaiyāṉ ciṟu
taṉattupperu

ka [veḷāṉu]kku ivaṉ tāmarkoṭṭattu vallanāṭṭu tirumāṟpeṟ[ṟu*]
āti

rarpakkal nāṅkaḷ koṇṭa kācu āyirattu muṉṉūṟṟuk kācu
ā[yirat]tu muṉṉūṟṟukkum ittevarc cālāpokamāka ṉāṅkaḷ viṟṟukku[ṭu]t-

ta ūrkaḷāvaṉa eṅ[ka]ḷūrt teṉpiṭākai••••••••• ṭṭuppākkamum
mañci••• ṉpāṭiyu••••kku kiḻpāṟkellai eṅkaḷūr piṭākai para

edition.p:
The rest is very much damaged. The following three disconnected pieces seem to be
connected with this inscription.

l.ta:
••••••••• vāykkālum ivvūriraṇṭukku••• pāñcuvarum nirum
vāykkālum melnokkiya maramum kiḻnokkiya kiṇaṟum maṟṟum ivviraṇṭūrilum
uṭumpoṭi āmaiy tavaḻ

nila[m uṇṇila]m oḻiviṉṟi eppeṟppaṭṭatum emmi
li[cai*]

pporuḷukkum iṟaipporuḷukkum āka āyirattu muṉṉūṟu kācum kaik
koṇṭu viṟṟukkuṭuttom ivvūr iraṇṭu[m*] icuṭṭiṉa iṟaiy koyil vācalāṟ
po

kuṭimaiy epperppaṭṭatum ivaka•••••••• āṭṭaik ku[ṭi]maiy
iṟukkumpotu ivvū•••••• ciriḷaṅkovaṉeṉ ivai eṉṉeḻuttu |.

inscription.head:
No. 292.
(A.R. No. 292 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA I—10th YEAR (A.D. 1021-22)
inscription.p:
This has also the same introduction like the above, but shorter in length.
Some portions are lost. It registers a gift of 115 kāśu for the daily supply of
4 kaḻañju of śidāri incence to the temple at Tirumālpēṟu by Kōvaṉ Āchchaṉ
alias Madhurāntaka-Pallavaraiyaṉ, a native of Āḍippuliyūr in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu,
a division of Kshatriyaśikhāmaṇi-vaḷanāḍu in Śoḻamaṇḍalam. The amount was
invested with the sabhā of Punnai alias Parakulāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in
Valla-nāḍu. It also registers another gift of 60 kāśu to the temple made by the
same donor for the daily unguents to the deity, which was left in-charge of the
Ūrār of Nittavinōdanallūr in Dāmar-nāḍu.

l.ta:
tiruma[ṉṉi vaḷa]ra irunilamaṭantaiyum porccayappāvaiyuñ
cirttaniccelviyun taṉ perunteviyarāki iṉpuṟa neṭitiyilūḻi iṭaituṟaiy nāṭun
tuṭarvaṉa veli[paṭar vaṉavā]

yuñ cuḷḷiccūḻmatiṭ koḷḷippākkaiyum naṇṇaṟ
karumuraṇ maṇṇai[k]kaṭakka-

mum porukaṭaliḻattaraicar tam muṭiyu māṅkavar teviya roṅkeḻil muṭiyum
muṉnavarpakkaṟ ṟeṉnavar vaitta cuntaramuṭiyum intiraṉāramun teṇṭirai īḻamaṇṭala
muḻuvatum eripaṭaikkeraḷaṉ muṟaimaiyiṟcūṭuṅ kulatanamākiya palarpuka-

ḻmuṭiyuñ ceṅkatir mālaiyuñ caṅkatirvelait tolperuṅkāvaṟ palapaḻan
tivum ceruvi[ṟci]navi irupattoru kālaraicukaḷai kaṭṭa paracurāmaṉ melvaruñ
cāntimattivaraṇ karuti iruttiya cempoṟṟiruttaku muṭiyum payaṅkoṭu paḻimika
mucaṅkiyil mutukiṭṭoḷitta cayaciṅkaṉ aḷapperum pukaḻoṭu piṭi iraṭṭapāṭi eḻarai
ilakkamum nava[ni]tikkulap perumalaikaḷum māpporu

- āvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattut tāmarkkoṭṭattu vallanāṭṭup
puṉ(va)ṉaiyāna parakulāntakac caturvetimaṅkalattu

kāmaṇi vaḷanāṭṭut tirunaṟaiyūrnāṭṭu āṭippuliyūr āṭippuliyūruṭaiyān
kovaṉ āccaṉā[ṉa] maturā

varkku nicatam nāṟkaḻañcu
citāri iṭuvatāka nivantañ ceyyak kuṭutta kācu nūṟṟorupattaiñcu ikkācu
nūṟṟorupattaiñcum ittirumāṟpeṟṟu tiripuvana

yom koṇṭu ikkācukkup policai
tiṅkaḷ mukkālpoṉ policaiyāka ā-

maṇivaḷanāṭṭut tirunaṟaiyūr
nāṭṭu āṭippuliyūr āṭippuliyūruṭaiyāṉ kovaṉ āccaṉnāṉa matu[rāntaka]p
pallavaraiyaṉ tāmarkkoṭṭattu tāmarnāṭṭu tirumālpeṟṟu mā

taṇṇaiy tirumaippūccukkum••••••••• kuṭutta kācu aṟupatu ikkācu
aṟupatum koṇṭu ikkācukku palicai tiṅkaḷ [eḻu]mañcāṭi•••• yāka āṇṭu
varai iṭuvatāṉa poṉ nāṟkaḻañcaraiya nālumañcāṭiyuṅ kuṉ-

kaṭṭaḷaittuḷainiṟaip tiṅkaḷḷopāti uṭaiyār paṇṭārattu
vaiccut taravukoḷvatākavum ippoṉ taṇṭavantārkku mekkaṇṭu nica-

tam iraṇṭucoṟu kuṭuppatāka ipparicu ippolicai muṭṭil muṭṭiṉa nāḷ
mutal nāḷoṉ••••••••• nicati pattup poṉ maṉ[ṟap peṟuvitākavum]
immaṉṟu

edition.l: brāhmaṇaṉ••••••••• ṉeḻuttu ||—

inscription.head:
No. 292A.
(A.R. No. 292-A of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN—3rd YEAR
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions Vol. XIII, No. 30.

inscription.head:
No. 293.
(A.R. No. 293 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJA I—15th YEAR (A.D. 999-1000).
inscription.p:
This has the introduction Tirumagaḷ pōla etc. It records an agreement
given by the Ūrār of Tirumālpēṟu to measure out a specified quantity of paddy
to the temple once every year for the 15(1/2) kaḻañju of gold received by them
from the latter.

l.ta:
tirumakaḷpolap perunilaccelviyum taṉakke yurimai
pūṇṭamai maṉakkoḷak kā

ḷūrcālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi veṅkaināṭuṅ kaṅkapāṭiyum
nuḷampapāṭiyum taṭikaipāṭiyu[m*] kuṭamalaināṭun taṇṭāṟkoṇṭa taṉṉeḻil ūḻi
yūḻiyuḷḷellā yāṇṭun toḻuteka viḷaṅku[m*] yāṇṭeyteḻiya-

- āvatu tāmark
koṭṭattu vallanāṭṭut tirumālpeṟṟu ūrom kaiyyeḻuttu ikkoṭṭattu
iṉṉāṭṭu ivvūrt tirumālpeṟṟu

rar pakkal yāṅkaḷ koṇṭu
kaṭava poṉ ta

patiṉayṅkaḻañcarai poṉ [koṇ]ṭu kaṭavom ippoṉ patiṉayṅkaḻañ
caraiyuṅ koṇṭa paricāvatu kaḻañciṉvāy ūrakattuniṉṟār nārāyakkāloṭokku
marakkālāl kaḻañciṉvāy mukkāṭimukkālāka•••• [yiru]patteṇkāṭināṉāḻiyum
āṇṭuvarai paṅkuṉiyuñ cit-

tiraiyum paṭappanā[ra]mum patarum vālu[ṅ ku]ttalum puḻutiyu[m*] nikki
uṭaiyār tirumu[ṟṟa]ttuk koṇṭu ceṉṟu aḷantu tevakanmikaḷvacam tara[vu
koḷ]vomāṉom tirumālpeṟṟu••••••••• āṭṭāṇṭutoṟum ca

taṉamutalāka tām veṇṭukovukku nicatam nāl
kāṇam taṇṭam aṭṭuvomāka kuṭuttom•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 294-295.
(A.R. No. 294 & 295 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN—3rd YEAR.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions Vol. XIII, Nos. 31 & 32.

inscription.head:
No. 295A.
(A.R. No. 295-A of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJA I—13th YEAR (A.D. 997-98)
inscription.p:
This is damaged and incomplete. It seems to record a gift of land to meet
the expenses of daily offerings to the deity in the temple of Tirumālpēṟuḍaiyār,
made by a lady by name Araṅgaṉ Periyāḷ, the wife of a local Veḷḷāḷa resident.

l.ta:
- āvatu tāmark
koṭṭattu vallanāṭṭu tirumālppeṟuṭaiyār ātittāḻvā[r]kku ivvūr
veḷḷāḷaṉ ma•••••••

tittaval vaitta ivūrk kūṭṭa•••• ṉṟu per kūvappaṭum
nila•••••••••

laiyum nirnilattuk kiḻpārkkellai vallava ta••••••••• yākkali
paṭṭuṭaiyāṉ•••• nilattukku meṟkum te••••

inscription.head:
No. 295B.
(A.R. No. 295-B of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJA I—4th YEAR (A.D. 988-989)
inscription.p:
This is another damaged record found in the same place. It is dated in
the 4th year probably of the same king, and registers a grant of 15 kaḻañju of
gold for a perpetual lamp in the temple, made by a donor whose name is lost.
The amount is stated to have been invested with the Ūrār of Kaitaṇḍalam in
Dāmar-nāḍu.

l.ta:
- āvatu tāmarkkoṭṭattu vallanāṭṭut tirumāl
peṟṟu āḻvārkku viḻupparaiyanāṭṭu•••••••••

•••••amutinukkut tāmarkkoṭṭattu tāmarnāṭṭuk kaitaṇṭalattu
ūrā[r*] taṉmakaṭṭaḷaiyāl••• poṉ patiṉaiṅkaḻañcu poṉkoṇṭu nicatam
uḻakku ney yeṇ-

••••• nāḻiyoṭokkum uḻakkāl nicatam uḻakku neyyeṇṇai
āḷvār koyilil tiruvuṇṇāḻikai koṇṭuvantu aṭṭuvatākavum iney
yeṇṇai taṇṭavantārkaḷu

edition.p: The next line is badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 296.
(A.R. No. 296 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE FIRST PRĀKĀRA.
KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA I—12th YEAR (A.D. 1080-81)
inscription.p:
This is built in at the right end. It begins with the introduction Pugaḻ-
sūḻnda puṇari etc. It records a gift of 90 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp in
the temple at Tirumālpēṟu, by one Malaitāṅgi Dēvaṉ Sūryaṉ alias Rājēndraśōḻa-
Mummalairājaṉ of Neḍi-nāḍu, a division of Perumbāṇappāḍi.

l.ta:
puviyil poṉṉemi
[a]ḷavun taṉneminaṭappa viḷaṅku jayamaka[ḷai] yiḷaṅkopparu[va]ttuc cakkarakoṭ
ṭattu vikkira[mat to]ḻi[lāl] putu[ma]ṇam puṇarntu matuvaraiyiṭṭam vayirākarattu
vār ayil iḷaikku

ḷava••• taḷamiriya vāḷuṟaikaḻittut toḷvali kāṭṭip
porppari naṭātti kirttiyai niṟutti vaṭaticai vākaicūṭit••••

edition.l:
tait temaru[ka]malappūmakaḷ potumaiyum poṉ niyāṭai naṉnilappāvai
tanimai[ yunta] virntup punitaṟ ṟirumaṇimakuṭam urimai[yiṟ] cūṭit taṉnaṭi yiraṇṭu
ntaṭamuṭiyākat toṉnilaventar cūṭa [muṉṉai] manuvāṟu perukki kaliyāṟu vaṟuppac
ceṅkol ticaitoṟuñ c[el]la veṇkuṭai yirunila[viḷā]kam me[ṅka*]
ṇuntanatu tirunala veṇṇilāt tikaḻa orutani••••

l.ta:
rattu [muva]r tiṟaiviṭu ta[ṟa] kalañcori
kaḷiṟumuṟai niṟpa vilaṅkiya teṉṉavaṉ karu

laittiṭat taṉ poṉṉakarp
puṟattiṭaikkiṭappa viṉṉāṇ piṟkulappiṟaipol niṟpiḻaiyeneṉuñ colletir
koṭiṟṟa[l*]latu [ta]ṉ kaivil letirkoṭā vikkalaṉ kallatar naṅkili tuṭaṅki
maṇalūr naṭuveṉat tuṅkapat••••

ṉmānamuṅ kūṟiya viramum kiṭappa
e[ṟiṉa] malaikaḷum mutuku neḷitara iḻi

ṉaiyum paṉnāḷiṭṭa palpala
mu[tu]kum pattaiti[k]koṭi jayapperu

ṭṭamu miḷātu koṭutta veṅkari[ñi]raiyuṅ kaṅkamaṇṭalamuñ ciṅkaṇa
meṉṉum paraṇiyiraṇṭum [o]rumaiyil kai[k]koṇṭāramu
yākamu[m*] viḷaṅkap pārmicai mevalar vaṇaṅka viṟṟiru

parumpāṇappāṭi neṭināṭṭu malaitāṅki neṭunāṭuṭaiyāṉ tevaṉ
cūriyaṉā[na] rāje

viḷakku
eriya meṟpaṭiyār viṭṭa cāvāmuvāperāṭu toṇṇūṟu ivvāṭu toṇṇūṟuṅ
kaikkoṇṭu nicati uḻakku ney te•••••••••

vaṭikaḷ tirumālpeṟuṭaiyāṉākam maṉṟāṭi coṭṭai tevaṉ••• aṭṭuvā
rākavu[m*] ivarkaḷ ḷivvūriru

tu iṉ ney yaṭṭāte acalūrili[rukkiṉ] inney
taṇṭa ponārkku coṟumiṭṭu tāṅkaḷ kaṭava ney yaṭṭuva[t]ākak kaikkoṇṭa
cāvā muvāp perāṭu toṇṇūṟu itta

inscription.head:
No. 297.
(A.R. No. 297 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST AND NORTH WALLS OF THE
FIRST PRĀKĀRA.
RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA I—30th YEAR (A.D. 1041-42)
inscription.p:
This begins with introduction Tirumaṉṉi vaḷara etc. It registers a sale of
3000 kuḻi of land by the sabhā of Kūram alias Śōḻamārttāṇḍa-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam in Nīrvēḷūr-nāḍu a division of Ūṟṟukkāṭṭuk-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍa-
śōḻamaṇḍalam to the temple at Tirumālpēṟu, for a sum of 36 Rājarājaṉ kāśu
endowed to the temple by Daṇḍanāyakaṉ Sōman Kumaraṉ alias Madhurāntaka-
Mārāyaṉ, an officer of the royal treasury to provide for 75 kalam of paddy, with
which to meet the expenses of the daily supply of tumbai flowers for the god,
to feed two Śivayōgins in the temple after the midday worship and for other
requirements. The payment is said to have been made to the sabhā by the
donor to include the several taxes on the land.

edition.l:
namaśśivāya svasti śrī [||*] tiruma[ ṉṉi vaḷara irunilamaṭantaiyum poṟceyap]
pāvaiyum cirttanic celviyum taṉ perunteviyarāki iṉpuṟa neṭitiyalūḻiyuḷ
[ḷiṭaituṟai]ṉāṭuntuṭarvaṉa velippaṭar vaṉavāciyum cuḷḷi[c*] cūḻmatiḷ koḷḷip
pākkaiyum na[ṇ*]ṇaṟ karumuraṇ ma[ṇ*]ṇaikkaṭakkamum porukaṭaliḻattaraicar
ta[m*] muṭiyum āṅkavar teviyaro••••••••• pakkal ten•••••••••
eṟipaṭai keraḷaṉ mu-

l.ta:
ṟaimaiyil cūṭum kulataṉamākiya palarpukaḻ muṭiyum ceṅkatirmālaiyum caṅkatir
mālai tolperuṅkāvai palpaḻa

vum ceruvil ciṉavil irupa[t*]torukāl
araicukaḷai kaṭṭa parācirāmaṉ melvarum cāntima[t*]tivaraṇ karuti iruttiya
cempoṟṟirutakumuṭiyum [paya]ṅkoṭu paḻimika viyaṅkiyil mutuki[ṭ*]ṭoḷitta caya
ciṅkaṉ aḷapurum pukaḻoṭum piṭi iraṭṭapāṭi eḻarai yila[k*]kamum navanetikula peru
malaikaḷum vikkiramavira cakkarakoṭṭamu[m*] mutirvaṭava[l*]lai maturamaṇṭalamu[m*]
kāmiṭai vaḷa ṉāmaṇai koṇaiyum

veñcilaivira pañcapaḷḷiyum pācaṭai paḻan mācuṇitecamum ayaṟvil vaṇ
kirtti yātiniṟaivayil ca

ra rataṉai viḷaiyamaṟ ka[ḷat*]tu
kiḷaiyoṭum piṭi[t*]tu palataṉattoṭu niṟaikulataṉakkuvaiyum ki[ṭ*]ṭarum
ceṟimiḷaiyo[ṭ*]ṭar viṣayamum pūcura[r*]cer naṟkocalaiṉāṭum ta

pālaṉai vemmuṉai
yaḻi[t*]tu vaṇṭuṟaicolai taṇṭapu[t*]tiyum iraṇacūraṉai muraṇura tākki
tikkaṇaikir[tti*] takkaṇalāṭamu[m*] kovi

ṭa taṅkāta cāral
veṅkāḷa tecamum toṭukaṭal caṅkuvo[ṭ*]ṭal mahivāḷaṉai veñcamarviḷā-

ka[t*]tañcuvi[t*]taruḷi [oṇṭiṟal] yāṉaiyum peṇṭir paṇṭāramu[m*]
ni[t*]tila neṭumkaṭalu[t]tiralāṭamum veṟimalar tirtta teripuṉalkaṅkaiyum alai
kaṭa[l*] naṭuvoṭ palakalam celutti caṅkirāmavijayar tuṅkapa

nākiya kiṭāra[t*]
taraicaṉai vākayam porukaṭal kumpa[k*]kariyoṭum akappaṭutturimaiyil piṟa[k*]kiya
peruniti piṟakkamum ār[t*]tavaṉ akanakar portoḻil vācalil vi[c*]cātira
toraṇamu moy[t*]toḷi puṉaimaṇiputavamu[m*] kaṉamaṇikatavamum niṟaijivijayamum
tuṟainirpa[ṉ*]ṉaiyum vaṉmalaiyūre-

yil toṉmalaiyūrum āḻkaṭalakaḻcūḻ māyiruṭiṅkamum kalaṅkāvalviṉai
ilaṅkācopamum kāpuṟu niṟaipuṉal māpa[ppā]ḷamum kāvalampuricai mevilipaṅkamum
viḷaipañcuṟuṭai vaḷa[p*]pañcūṟum kalaita[k*]kor pukaḻ talaittakkolamum tirumā
valviṉai mārumaliṅkamu[m*] kalāmu[tirkaṭun]tiṟa lilāmuṟitecamum tenakkalar poḻil
māṉakkavāramum toṭukaṭalkāval kaṭumuraḷkaṭāramu māporum taṇṭāl koṇṭa
koparakecari

ṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu ūṟṟukkāṭṭu koṭṭattu nirveḷūr nāṭṭuk
kūramāṉa coḻamāttāṇṭac catu

yom nilavilaiyāvaṇak
kaiyeḻuttuc ciṟutaṉattup peruntaṉam taṇṭanāyakam taṇṭanāyakam comaṉ
kumaraṉāṉa maturāntakamārāyaṉ tāmaṟkoṭṭattu valla nāṭṭut tirumāṟpeṟu uṭai
yāṟkkut tumpaittiruppaḷḷittāman nāḻiyiṉukku

ṭikku uṇṇakkaṭavārkaḷāka••• takku patiṉmuṉṟukkum neykku[m
mo]rukkum pākku veṟṟilaikkum

patakku [mu]nnāḻiyāka
orāṇṭiṉukku nellu arumoḻiteva marakkālāl eḻupattaiṅkala

ku viṟṟukkuṭutta
nilamāvatu eṅkaḷū[r ko]yil kumiḻittūmpāl nirpāytti viḷaiyum nila

tu muvāyiraṅ
kuḻi viṟṟukkuṭutta paricāvatu kiḻpāṟkellai eyiṟkoṭṭattu eyilnāṭṭup peruṅ
karumpū rellaikku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai peruṅkarumpūrerikkup pā-

ynta āṟṟukkālukku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai nāṅkaḷ kalluṅ[karaika]lluṅ
kaḷḷiyum naṭṭu varampaṭṭikkuṭutta peruvarampukku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai•••••
koṭṭattup puricaināṭṭup putuppākkattu ellaikkal-

lukku teṟkum iṉṉāṉkellaiyuḷḷakappaṭṭa nilam uṭpāḻoḻiviṉṟi
mikutikkuṟaivuḷḷaṭaṅka viṟṟukkuṭuttom iṉṉilattukku iṟai

ṇṭu viṟṟukkuṭuttom innilattāl vanta pu[taṟ]poṉ pañcavāram
cilliṟai coṟu māṭṭuveṭṭi amañci niriṟai pulamiṟai maṟṟum koyil vācaliṟ
pontatepporppaṭṭatum nāṅkaḷe yiṟuttu ippūmi cuṭṭi uḻutakuṭiyai epperpaṭṭa

••••• tuvuṅ kāṭṭappeṟātatākavum ipparicu viṟṟu vilaiyāvaṇañ
ceytu

inscription.head:
No. 298.
(A.R. No. 298 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL OF THE FIRST PRĀKĀRA.
PARĀNTAKA I—37th YEAR (A.D. 943-44)
inscription.p:
This is slightly damaged and is incomplete. It registers a sale of land
tax-free by the temple at Tirumālpēṟu in Valla-nāḍu a division of Dāmaṟkōṭṭam,
to a certain Kaḍagaṉ Kuñjaramallaṉ alias Śōḻamārāyaṉ.

l.ta:
matiraikoṇṭa kopparakecarivaṉma[ṟ*]kku yāṇṭu muppat
teḻāvatu tāmarkoṭṭattu vallanāṭṭu tirumālpeṟṟu

ratti
ṉuttom payire••••• mey pāḻāykkiṭanta nilattukku ellai śrīkovintapāṭi
ellaikku meṟkum••••

ṭṭārkālukku vaṭakkum [irukūrk] kālukkun tirunantavānattukku kiḻakkum
•••••• ṟkum innāṉkellaiyilum akappaṭṭa

mi uṇṇila moḻiviṉṟi
viṟṟukkuṭutteṉ emmilicainta vilai-

pporuḷaṟakkoṇṭu iṟaiyiliyāka viṟṟukkuṭutteṉ ituve poruḷaṟutip
poruṭ celavāka viṟṟukkuṭutteṉ kaṭakaṉ kuñciramallanākiya coḻamārāyanukku

inscription.head:
No. 299.
(A.R. No. 299 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
MAṆIKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH, WEST AND NORTH WALLS OF THE
FIRST PRĀKĀRA.
RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA I—Date lost.
inscription.p:
This is a mutilated and damaged inscription which registers an agreement
given by the assembly of Paṅgavērkkam (?) alias Śōḻakulatilaka-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam in Vēḷā-nāḍu a division of Ūṟṟukkāṭṭukōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-
maṇḍalam to the temple at Tirumālpēṟu for an amount of gold invested with
them, by the temple with god Chaṇḍēśvara as agent.

l.ta:
—ṇaikirttit takkaṇalāṭamum kovintacantaṉ māviḻin toṭat
taṅkāta cāral vaṅkāḷa tecamu

hipālaṉai veñcamar
viḷākattañcuvit[taru]ḷi••••••••• yum peṇṭir paṇṭāramu[m*] nittira
neṭuṅkaṭa luttiralāṭamum••••• naṭuvuḷ palakalañ celuttic caṅkirāma

ṉā[kiya] kiṭārattaraicaṉai vākayam porukaṭak kumpakkariyoṭu makap
paṭutturimaiyil piṟakki[ya perune]tip piṟakkamum ārttavaṉ ṉakar (nakar)p port
toḻil vācali[l*] viccātirat toraṇamum moyttoḷirp puṉaimaṇipputavamum
kaṉamaṇikkatavamum niṟaijivi

yamu[m tu]ṟai nirppaṉṉaiyum paṉmalaivūreyiṟ
ṟoṉmalaiyūrum āḻkaṭalakaḻcūḻ [māyiruṭiṅ]kamum kalaṅkāvalviṉai ilaṅkā-

copamum koppuṟuniṟaipuṉal māpappāḷa[mu*]m kāvalampuricai mevilim

inscription.head:
No. 300.
(A.R. No. 300 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE EAST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
RIGHT OF ENTRANCE.
PARĀNTAKA I—26th YEAR (A.D. 932-33)
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It records a gift of sheep for burning a perpetual lamp
in the temple of Perumānaḍigaḷ at Gōvindavāḍi by one Pokkaṇaṅgāttāṉ Dēvaṉ
kēsari alias Kuñjaramalla-Pallavaraiyaṉ.

l.ta: m ma[ṟ*]ku yāṇṭu irupattāṟāvatu veḷā nāṭṭu pokkaṇaṅkāttā-

inscription.head:
No. 301.
(A.R. No. 301 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARĀNTAKA I—Date lost.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of 27 kaḻañju of gold for burning a perpetual
lamp in the temple at Gōvindapāḍi in Valla-nāḍu a division of Dāmaṟkōṭṭam by
prince Parāntakan Uttamaśīliyār.

inscription.head:
No. 302.
(A.R. No. 302 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARĀNTAKA I—34th YEAR (A.D. 940-41)
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 96 Sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Gōvindapāḍi-Āḷvār by a resident of Śirukuḷam (?) by name Māṟaṉ Paramēśvaraṉ
alias Śōḻiyavaraiyaṉ, who seems to have left them in charge of the Ūrār of
Veṇmaṇi-Pudūr in Manaiyiṟ-kōṭṭam for the daily supply of an uḻakku of ghee.
The inscription is damaged.
